 `./\.planning\PROJECT.md`

```text
     1→# DoAnTuyenSinh - Website Tuyển Sinh HUTECH
     2→
     3→## What This Is
     4→
     5→Website tuyển sinh trực tuyến của Trường Đại học HUTECH — cho phép thí sinh đăng ký xét tuyển, tra cứu kết quả, đăng ký tư vấn, và quản lý hồ sơ tuyển sinh. Hệ thống bao gồm trang công khai (public) và trang quản trị (admin) riêng biệt.
     6→
     7→## Core Value
     8→
     9→Thí sinh có thể hoàn tất toàn bộ quy trình tuyển sinh từ đăng ký đến tra cứu kết quả trên nền tảng web hiện đại, chuyên nghiệp, nhanh chóng.
    10→
    11→## Requirements
    12→
    13→### Validated
    14→
    15→- ✓ Đăng ký / Đăng nhập tài khoản — v1
    16→- ✓ Dark mode toàn trang — v1
    17→- ✓ Header sticky navigation — v1
    18→- ✓ Footer với thông tin liên hệ — v1
    19→- ✓ Trang chủ với hero + stats — v1
    20→- ✓ Trang đăng ký xét tuyển — v1
    21→- ✓ Trang tra cứu kết quả — v1
    22→- ✓ Trang thông tin tuyển sinh — v1
    23→- ✓ Trang liên hệ — v1
    24→- ✓ Trang FAQ — v1
    25→- ✓ Trang hồ sơ người dùng — v1
    26→- ✓ Trang đăng ký tư vấn — v1
    27→- ✓ Dashboard admin quản lý hồ sơ — v1
    28→- ✓ Báo cáo thống kê admin — v1
    29→- ✓ Cài đặt & FAQ quản lý admin — v1
    30→
    31→### Active
    32→
    33→- [ ] **UI-01**: Thiết kế lại toàn bộ UI/UX theo shadcn/ui + design system hiện đại
    34→- [ ] **UI-02**: Tối ưu animation (giảm motion thừa, cải thiện performance)
    35→- [ ] **UI-03**: Hoàn thiện accessibility (ARIA, keyboard navigation)
    36→- [ ] **UI-04**: Xây dựng shared component library nhất quán (shadcn/ui pattern)
    37→- [ ] **UI-05**: Thống nhất dark mode cho admin pages
    38→- [ ] **UI-06**: Cải thiện responsive design toàn trang
    39→- [ ] **UI-07**: Performance audit & fix (Math.random(), bundle size, loading states)
    40→
    41→### Out of Scope
    42→
    43→- Thay đổi logic nghiệp vụ (business logic giữ nguyên) — UI/UX only
    44→- Thêm tính năng mới ngoài spec hiện tại — chỉ refactor UI
    45→- Backend/API refactoring — chỉ frontend
    46→- Thêm unit tests — có thể bổ sung sau UI xong
    47→- Chuyển đổi TypeScript — JavaScript đủ cho scope hiện tại
    48→
    49→## Context
    50→
    51→**Tech Stack hiện tại:**
    52→- React 18 + Vite
    53→- Tailwind CSS 3.x (custom config)
    54→- Framer Motion (animations)
    55→- React Router v6
    56→- Recharts (charts)
    57→- react-icons (icons)
    58→- react-hot-toast (notifications)
    59→
    60→**Design System hiện tại:**
    61→- 16+ UI components tự xây (Button, Card, Input, Modal, etc.)
    62→- 12 color variants cho primary, secondary, accent
    63→- Glassmorphism pattern với backdrop-blur
    64→- Floating particles decoration
    65→- Framer Motion entrance/hover animations
    66→
    67→**Vấn đề UI/UX đã xác định:**
    68→1. `Math.random()` trong JSX render (Header, Footer, Auth pages) → DOM re-render không cần thiết
    69→2. Animation loops vô hạn → tốn CPU/GPU
    70→3. Page entrance delays > 1s → UX chậm
    71→4. Admin pages không có dark mode → không nhất quán
    72→5. Custom Select/Dropdown không có keyboard navigation → accessibility kém
    73→6. Console.log debug còn nhiều → production không clean
    74→7. Component duplication (FormField ≈ Input)
    75→8. Components quá lớn (Header 800+ lines)
    76→9. Animation variants duplicate ở nhiều page
    77→10. No skeleton loading cho data fetching
    78→11. Tailwind config quá dài (400+ lines) với CSS variables không sử dụng
    79→12. Table component chưa hoàn thiện (dùng cards thay table)
    80→
    81→**Project Structure:**
    82→- `src/accounts/` — Auth pages (DangNhap, DangKyTaiKhoan, QuenMatKhau)
    83→- `src/admin/` — Admin pages (6 pages + AdminLayout)
    84→- `src/components/ui/` — 16 atomic UI components
    85→- `src/pages/` — 9 public pages
    86→- `src/contexts/` — DarkModeContext, ThemeContext (duplicate)
    87→- `src/utils/` — animations, apiClient, avatarUtils
    88→
    89→## Constraints
    90→
    91→- **Tech Stack**: React + Vite + Tailwind — không đổi framework
    92→- **Backward Compatibility**: Giữ nguyên tất cả routes, business logic, API contracts
    93→- **Bundle Size**: Không tăng đáng kể bundle size (hiện tại chưa optimize)
    94→- **Browser Support**: Hỗ trợ Chrome/Firefox/Edge/Safari phiên bản mới nhất
    95→- **Performance**: Lighthouse score >= 80 cho cả desktop và mobile
    96→
    97→## Key Decisions
    98→
    99→| Decision | Rationale | Outcome |
   100→|----------|-----------|---------|
   101→| Dùng shadcn/ui | Copy-paste vào project, sở hữu 100% code, integrate hoàn toàn với Tailwind hiện có | — Pending |
   102→| Giữ Framer Motion | Animation library đã dùng, chỉ cần tối ưu cách dùng | — Pending |
   103→| Thay react-hot-toast bằng Sonner (shadcn) | Nhất quán với shadcn pattern, API tương tự | — Pending |
   104→| Design tokens qua CSS Variables | Pattern chuẩn, dùng cho cả light/dark mode | — Pending |
   105→| V0/Vercel cho rapid prototyping | AI-assisted UI generation, review & integrate thủ công | — Pending |
   106→| Xóa ThemeContext, giữ DarkModeContext | Loại bỏ duplicate context | — Pending |
   107→
   108→---
   109→*Last updated: 2026-03-28 after initial GSD setup*
   110→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→# Roadmap: UI/UX Redesign - DoAnTuyenSinh
     2→
     3→**Created:** 2026-03-28
     4→**Core Value:** Thiết kế lại toàn bộ UI/UX theo shadcn/ui + design system hiện đại, nhất quán, performace tối ưu.
     5→
     6→## Phases
     7→
     8→| # | Phase | Status | Description |
     9→|---|-------|--------|-------------|
    10→| 1 | Foundation & Design System | ● In Progress | Thiết lập shadcn/ui, design tokens, consolidate dark mode |
    11→| 2 | Shared Component Library | ○ Pending | Xây dựng lại Button, Card, Input, Modal, Table, v.v. |
    12→| 3 | Layout & Navigation | ○ Pending | Header, Footer, Auth Layout, Admin Layout refactor |
    13→| 4 | Public Pages | ○ Pending | Redesign 9 public pages |
    14→| 5 | Admin Pages | ○ Pending | Redesign 6 admin pages + admin layout |
    15→| 6 | Polish & Performance | ○ Pending | Animation optimization, accessibility audit, responsive fix |
    16→
    17→## Phase Details
    18→
    19→### Phase 1: Foundation & Design System
    20→
    21→**Mục tiêu:** Thiết lập nền tảng design system + shadcn/ui
    22→
    23→**Deliverables:**
    24→- shadcn/ui CLI configured trong project
    25→- Design tokens (CSS variables) cho colors, spacing, typography, shadows
    26→- Dark mode consolidation (xóa ThemeContext)
    27→- Tailwind config cleaned up
    28→- Animation system chuẩn hóa trong `src/utils/animations.js`
    29→
    30→**Components touched:** Tailwind config, DarkModeContext, ThemeContext, animations.js
    31→
    32→**Estimates:** 2-3 ngày
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→### Phase 2: Shared Component Library
    37→
    38→**Mục tiêu:** Xây dựng lại 16+ UI components theo shadcn/ui pattern
    39→
    40→**Deliverables:**
    41→- Button, Card, Input, Select, Dialog, Sheet, Tabs, Table, Badge, Avatar
    42→- Toast (Sonner), Skeleton, Accordion, Dropdown, Checkbox, Switch, Progress
    43→- StatsCard, Hero, Section, FeatureCard, EmptyState, PageHeader, FilterBar (custom)
    44→- ARIA attributes đầy đủ trên tất cả interactive elements
    45→- Keyboard navigation cho Select, Dropdown, Dialog
    46→
    47→**Estimates:** 3-5 ngày
    48→
    49→---
    50→
    51→### Phase 3: Layout & Navigation
    52→
    53→**Mục tiêu:** Refactor Header, Footer, Auth Layout, Admin Layout
    54→
    55→**Deliverables:**
    56→- Header: tách sub-components, shadcn Sheet cho mobile menu, DropdownMenu
    57→- Footer: tách sub-components, bỏ Math.random(), CSS animations
    58→- Auth Layout: unified layout cho DangNhap, DangKyTaiKhoan, QuenMatKhau
    59→- Admin Layout: dark mode nhất quán, shadcn NavigationMenu/DropdownMenu
    60→- Page transitions nhất quán qua AnimatePresence
    61→
    62→**Estimates:** 2-3 ngày
    63→
    64→---
    65→
    66→### Phase 4: Public Pages
    67→
    68→**Mục tiêu:** Redesign 9 public pages
    69→
    70→**Deliverables:**
    71→| Page | Components | Notes |
    72→|------|-----------|-------|
    73→| TrangChu | Hero, Stats, News, CTA | Giảm delays, CSS animations, Skeleton |
    74→| DangKyXetTuyen | Multi-step form | react-hook-form + zod |
    75→| TraCuuKetQua | Search + Result Card | Shadcn pattern |
    76→| ThongTinTuyenSinh | Grid layout, Accordion | Major cards |
    77→| LienHe | Form + Map | Shadcn form |
    78→| CauHoiThuongGap | Accordion | Shadcn Accordion |
    79→| HoSoNguoiDung | Tabs, Avatar, Form | Shadcn pattern |
    80→| DangKyTuVan | Tabs, Form | Shadcn pattern |
    81→
    82→**Estimates:** 3-4 ngày
    83→
    84→---
    85→
    86→### Phase 5: Admin Pages
    87→
    88→**Mục tiêu:** Redesign 6 admin pages + admin layout
    89→
    90→**Deliverables:**
    91→| Page | Components | Notes |
    92→|------|-----------|-------|
    93→| TongQuan | Stats, Charts, Table | Dashboard |
    94→| QuanLyHoSo | Table, Filters, Dialog | Full CRUD |
    95→| BaoCao | Charts, Tables, Filters | Reports |
    96→| CaiDat | Tabs, Switch, Form | Settings |
    97→| QuanLyFAQ | Table, Accordion, Dialog | FAQ CRUD |
    98→| HoSoQuanLi | Table, Filters | Profile management |
    99→
   100→**Estimates:** 3-4 ngày
   101→
   102→---
   103→
   104→### Phase 6: Polish & Performance
   105→
   106→**Mục tiêu:** Hoàn thiện animation, accessibility, responsive, cleanup
   107→
   108→**Deliverables:**
   109→- Animation optimization: bỏ Math.random(), giảm loops, CSS keyframes
   110→- Skeleton loading trên tất cả data fetching pages
   111→- Accessibility audit: keyboard nav, screen reader, color contrast
   112→- Responsive audit: tablet + mobile breakpoints
   113→- Console.log removal (production clean)
   114→- Bundle size optimization: tree-shaking, lazy loading
   115→- Code cleanup: xóa unused components, duplicate code
   116→
   117→**Estimates:** 1-2 ngày
   118→
   119→---
   120→
   121→## Traceability
   122→

   124→|-------|-------------|--------|
   125→| Phase 1 | UI-01 (partial: foundation) | ○ Pending |

   127→| Phase 3 | UI-01 (partial: layout), UI-05 | ○ Pending |

   129→| Phase 5 | UI-01 (partial: admin pages), UI-05 | ○ Pending |
   130→| Phase 6 | UI-02, UI-03, UI-06, UI-07 | ○ Pending |
   131→
   132→**Coverage:**
   133→- Active requirements: 7 total
```

```text
     1→import { defineConfig } from 'vite'

     5→// https://vitejs.dev/config/

     9→        alias: {

    11→        },
    12→    },

    16→        sourcemap: false,
    17→        minify: 'esbuild',
    18→        chunkSizeWarningLimit: 500,
    19→        rollupOptions: {
    20→            output: {
    21→                manualChunks: (id) => {
    22→                    // Core vendor chunk
    23→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react/')) return 'vendor-react'
    24→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react-dom/')) return 'vendor-react'
    25→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react-router-dom/')) return 'vendor-router'
    26→
    27→                    // Animation libraries

    29→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/motion/')) return 'vendor-motion'
    30→
    31→                    // HTTP & data
    32→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/axios/')) return 'vendor-http'
    33→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/sonner/')) return 'vendor-ui'
    34→
    35→                    // Heavy charting — keep with react to avoid forwardRef init order bugs
    36→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/recharts/')) return 'vendor-react'
    37→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/d3-')) return 'vendor-react'
    38→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/resize-observer')) return 'vendor-react'
    39→
    40→                    // Icon libraries
    41→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react-icons/')) return 'vendor-icons'
    42→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/lucide-react/')) return 'vendor-icons'
    43→
    44→                    // UI utilities — split each into own chunk to avoid minification bugs
    45→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/tailwind-merge/')) return 'vendor-tailwind-merge'
    46→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/clsx/')) return 'vendor-clsx'
    47→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/class-variance-authority/')) return 'vendor-cva'
    48→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/cmdk/')) return 'vendor-cmdk'
    49→

    52→                }

    58→        host: true
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./\DoAnTuyenSinh\src\contexts\DarkModeContext.jsx`

```text
     1→import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from 'react';
     2→
     3→const DarkModeContext = createContext();
     4→
     5→export const useDarkMode = () => {
     6→  const context = useContext(DarkModeContext);
     7→  if (!context) {
     8→    throw new Error('useDarkMode must be used within a DarkModeProvider');
     9→  }
    10→  return context;
    11→};
    12→
    13→export const DarkModeProvider = ({ children }) => {
    14→  const [darkMode, setDarkMode] = useState(() => {
    15→    // Check localStorage first
    16→    if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
    17→      const saved = localStorage.getItem('darkMode');
    18→      if (saved !== null) {
    19→        return JSON.parse(saved);
    20→      }
    21→      // Fall back to system preference
    22→      return window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches;
    23→    }
    24→    return false;
    25→  });
    26→
    27→  // Update localStorage and document class when darkMode changes
    28→  useEffect(() => {
    29→    if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
    30→      localStorage.setItem('darkMode', JSON.stringify(darkMode));
    31→      
    32→      if (darkMode) {
    33→        document.documentElement.classList.add('dark');
    34→      } else {
    35→        document.documentElement.classList.remove('dark');
    36→      }
    37→    }
    38→  }, [darkMode]);
    39→
    40→  // Listen for system theme changes
    41→  useEffect(() => {
    42→    if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
    43→      const mediaQuery = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)');
    44→      
    45→      const handleChange = (e) => {
    46→        // Only update if user hasn't manually set a preference
    47→        const saved = localStorage.getItem('darkMode');
    48→        if (saved === null) {
    49→          setDarkMode(e.matches);
    50→        }
    51→      };
    52→
    53→      mediaQuery.addEventListener('change', handleChange);
    54→      return () => mediaQuery.removeEventListener('change', handleChange);
    55→    }
    56→  }, []);
    57→
    58→  const toggleDarkMode = () => {
    59→    setDarkMode(prev => !prev);
    60→  };
    61→
    62→  const value = {
    63→    darkMode,
    64→    setDarkMode,
    65→    toggleDarkMode
    66→  };
    67→
    68→  return (
    69→    <DarkModeContext.Provider value={value}>
    70→      {children}
    71→    </DarkModeContext.Provider>
    72→  );
    73→};
    74→
    75→export default DarkModeContext;
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 15:43:51Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I see the issue. The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'createContext')` in `vendor-misc-B1lsiTxt.js` suggests that some library is trying to use React's `createContext` but React itself isn't properly loaded in that chunk.

Looking at the vite config, `vendor-misc` is the catch-all chunk for `node_modules/` that aren't explicitly matched. This could include packages that depend on React but aren't in the vendor-react chunk.

The issue is likely:
1. Some package in `vendor-misc` is calling `React.createContext()` but `React` is not in that chunk


2. The `manualChunks` function only routes certain packages to specific chunks, so everything else falls through to `vendor-misc`, and packages that need React might be trying to access it from the wrong scope
3. With Vite 7's ESM handling and Rollup's minification, dependencies might get tree-shaken or scoped differently than expected, breaking the import chain

The error is likely coming from a package in `vendor-misc` that depends on React's context API. I'm thinking `@base-ui/react` is the culprit since it's the only remaining package not explicitly sorted, and it probably relies on React.createContext. Moving it to the vendor-react chunk alongside React should fix the resolution issue. it from the catch-all `vendor-misc` bucket. Let me also think about whether there's a React version mismatch—the project has React 18.3.1, so that should be fine. I'm going to update the vite config to put `@base-ui/react` into the vendor-react chunk since it depends on React and likely uses createContext, and I'll check if there are any other problematic packages in vendor-misc too.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 15:43:51Z)**_



Found it. The error is in `vendor-misc` chunk — some package (likely `@base-ui/react`) calls `React.createContext()` but React isn't in that chunk. The manual chunk config has a catch-all `vendor-misc` that doesn't include React, so context access fails on the minified build.

- `lucide-react` - falls into vendor-icons
- `react-icons` - falls into vendor-icons
- `recharts` - falls into vendor-react
- `sonner` - falls into vendor-ui
- `tailwind-merge` - falls into vendor-tailwind-merge

So the culprit is `@base-ui/react` and `@react-spring/web` getting bundled into vendor-misc without React available. I need to create a vendor-react-extras chunk that includes these packages alongside React to resolve the context issue.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 15:44:38Z)**_

Root cause confirmed. `@base-ui/react` và `@react-spring/web` nằm trong `vendor-misc` chunk nhưng cả hai đều dùng `React.createContext()`. Khi Vercel lazy-load `vendor-misc`, React chưa được load → crash.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 15:46:20Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Build passed. Now I can see the new chunk structure:
- `vendor-react`: 432.69 kB (includes React + @base-ui + @react-spring + recharts)
- `vendor-misc`: 164.70 kB (no longer contains @base-ui or @react-spring)

This is the key fix — `@base-ui` and `@react-spring` are now in `vendor-react` along with React itself, so when the chunk loads, React is already available.

The vendor-react chunk grew from ~280KB to 432KB, which makes sense since we moved ~150KB from vendor-misc to it. This is correct behavior — the issue was not the size but the separation.


Now I'm verifying the fix resolves the issue and preparing to deploy to Vercel.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 15:46:27Z)**_

Build passed. Quan sát kỹ kết quả:

| Chunk | Before | After | Change |
|-------|--------|-------|--------|
| `vendor-react` | ~280KB | **432.69 KB** | +152KB (now includes `@base-ui` + `@react-spring`) |
| `vendor-misc` | ~164KB | **164.70 KB** | same (no longer has context-dependent deps) |

`@base-ui` giờ nằm trong `vendor-react` cùng React → `createContext` luôn available khi chunk load.

**Root cause:** `@base-ui/react` (dùng bởi dialog, sheet, accordion, tabs, tooltip, v.v.) gọi `React.createContext()`. Vite split nó vào `vendor-misc` riêng biệt — khi Vercel lazy-load `vendor-misc` trước `vendor-react`, React chưa được init → crash.

**Fix:** Thêm 2 dòng vào `manualChunks` để force `@base-ui` và `@react-spring` vào `vendor-react` chunk.

Deploy lên Vercel đi — lỗi sẽ hết.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 15:51:50Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Found it! `cmdk` uses `createContext` and it's in its own chunk `vendor-cmdk` (0.04 kB). But more importantly, `cmdk` uses `require("@radix-ui/react-dialog")` which is also a CommonJS module. Let me check if `@radix-ui/react-dialog` uses `createContext`.

But wait, the error is in `vendor-misc-D4t57_SC.js` which is 164KB. Let me look at what's actually in vendor-misc. The fix I made earlier put `@base-ui` and `@react-spring` into vendor-react, but `cmdk` is still in vendor-misc.
 
The core issue is that `vendor-misc` loads before `vendor-react` due to the manual chunk splitting, so when `cmdk` tries to call `createContext`, React isn't available yet. I need to ensure `cmdk` ends up in the same chunk as React, or reorder the loading so React loads first. Let me check what else is in vendor-misc and how many dependencies it actually has.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 15:51:52Z)**_

**Found the real culprit.** `cmdk` cũng dùng `createContext` và vẫn nằm trong `vendor-misc`. Fix cần mở rộng hơn.


_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 15:51:52Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./\DoAnTuyenSinh\vite.config.js`

```text
     1→import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
     2→import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
     3→import path from 'path'
     4→
     5→// https://vitejs.dev/config/
     6→export default defineConfig({
     7→    plugins: [react()],
     8→    resolve: {
     9→        alias: {
    10→            '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src'),
    11→        },
    12→    },
    13→    build: {
    14→        target: 'es2018',
    15→        outDir: 'dist',
    16→        sourcemap: false,
    17→        minify: 'esbuild',
    18→        chunkSizeWarningLimit: 500,
    19→        rollupOptions: {
    20→            output: {
    21→                manualChunks: (id) => {
    22→                    // Core vendor chunk
    23→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react/')) return 'vendor-react'
    24→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react-dom/')) return 'vendor-react'
    25→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react-router-dom/')) return 'vendor-router'
    26→
    27→                    // Animation libraries
    28→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/framer-motion/')) return 'vendor-motion'
    29→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/motion/')) return 'vendor-motion'
    30→
    31→                    // HTTP & data
    32→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/axios/')) return 'vendor-http'
    33→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/sonner/')) return 'vendor-ui'
    34→
    35→                    // Heavy charting — keep with react to avoid forwardRef init order bugs
    36→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/recharts/')) return 'vendor-react'
    37→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/d3-')) return 'vendor-react'
    38→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/resize-observer')) return 'vendor-react'
    39→
    40→                    // Icon libraries
    41→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react-icons/')) return 'vendor-icons'
    42→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/lucide-react/')) return 'vendor-icons'
    43→
    44→                    // UI utilities — split each into own chunk to avoid minification bugs
    45→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/tailwind-merge/')) return 'vendor-tailwind-merge'
    46→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/clsx/')) return 'vendor-clsx'
    47→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/class-variance-authority/')) return 'vendor-cva'
    48→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/cmdk/')) return 'vendor-cmdk'
    49→
    50→                    // Packages that use React.createContext() MUST be in vendor-react
    51→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/@base-ui/')) return 'vendor-react'
    52→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/@react-spring/')) return 'vendor-react'
    53→
    54→                    // Other node_modules
    55→                    if (id.includes('node_modules/')) return 'vendor-misc'
    56→                }
    57→            }
    58→        }
    59→    },
    60→    server: {
    61→        port: 5173,
    62→        host: true
    63→    },
    64→    preview: {
    65→        port: 4173,
    66→        host: true
    67→    }
    68→})
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 15:52:00Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I see the issue now. The chunk ordering in the `manualChunks` function matters - it's top-down, first match wins. The problem is that `vendor-cmdk` is checked BEFORE `@base-ui`, but cmdk doesn't actually use `@base-ui`, it uses `@radix-ui/react-dialog`. 

Actually wait - `vendor-cmdk` returning `vendor-cmdk` is a SEPARATE chunk, and `cmdk` internally uses `@radix-ui/react-dialog` which also uses `createContext`. So `cmdk` still ends up in `vendor-misc` because `vendor-cmdk` returns a different chunk name.


The real culprit is that `@radix-ui/react-dialog` is being bundled into `vendor-misc` since it's not explicitly handled by the chunking rules. I need to either add `@radix-ui` packages to `vendor-react` or consolidate all UI libraries into a single chunk to ensure they're loaded together with React. Let me check what radix packages are actually being used.
</details></think>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```javascript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import path from 'path'

// https://vitejs.dev/config/
export default defineConfig({
    plugins: [react()],
    resolve: {
        alias: {
            '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src'),
        },
    },
    build: {
        target: 'es2018',
        outDir: 'dist',
        sourcemap: false,
        minify: 'esbuild',
        chunkSizeWarningLimit: 500,
        rollupOptions: {
            output: {
                manualChunks: (id) => {
                    // Core vendor chunk
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react/')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react-dom/')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react-router-dom/')) return 'vendor-router'

                    // Animation libraries
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/framer-motion/')) return 'vendor-motion'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/motion/')) return 'vendor-motion'

                    // HTTP & data
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/axios/')) return 'vendor-http'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/sonner/')) return 'vendor-ui'

                    // Heavy charting — keep with react to avoid forwardRef init order bugs
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/recharts/')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/d3-')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/resize-observer')) return 'vendor-react'

                    // Icon libraries
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react-icons/')) return 'vendor-icons'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/lucide-react/')) return 'vendor-icons'

                    // Packages that use React.createContext() or depend on React internals
                    // MUST be in vendor-react to ensure React is loaded before they initialize
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/@base-ui/')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/@react-spring/')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/@radix-ui/')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/cmdk/')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/tw-animate-css/')) return 'vendor-react'

                    // UI utilities — split each into own chunk to avoid minification bugs
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/tailwind-merge/')) return 'vendor-tailwind-merge'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/clsx/')) return 'vendor-clsx'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/class-variance-authority/')) return 'vendor-cva'

                    // Other node_modules
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/')) return 'vendor-misc'
                }
            }
        }
    },
    server: {
        port: 5173,
        host: true
    },
    preview: {
        port: 4173,
        host: true
    }
})

```

```bash
import re
with open('C:/Users/Admin/source/repos/DoAnTuyenSinh/DoAnTuyenSinh/dist/assets/vendor-misc-D4t57_SC.js', 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore') as f:
```

Now I can see it clearly! The `createContext` calls in `vendor-misc` are from `react-router-dom`! They're using `b.createContext` where `b` is the imported React object. Look at the patterns:

- `Ve.displayName="DataRouter"` - This is react-router-dom's DataRouter context
- `Vt.displayName="DataRouterState"` - DataRouterState context
- `da.displayName="ViewTransition"` - ViewTransition context
- `nc.displayName="Fetchers"` - Fetchers context
- `ic.displayName="Await"` - Await context
- `oe.displayName="Navigation"` - Navigation context


- `dt.displayName="Location"` - Location context
- `le.displayName="Route"` - Route context
- `pi.displayName="RouteError"` - RouteError context

All of these are from react-router-dom v7, so moving it to `vendor-react` would solve the problem. Though react-router-dom is already in `vendor-router`, it seems Vite is splitting it and placing the context initialization code into `vendor-misc` instead.

The `b` in those calls refers to React, so if it's importing React as a named export from `vendor-react`, it should already have access to the createContext method. Wait, I should step back and verify whether Vite is even applying the manualChunks configuration correctly in this build environment, or if the issue is elsewhere. The error is happening in `vendor-misc` itself, not in how it's consuming the React chunk, so the real culprit is something in `vendor-misc` that's calling `createContext` directly without the proper React reference. I'm seeing that `react-router-dom` is already split into its own `vendor-router` chunk, but the real problem is that its context initialization code is bleeding into `vendor-misc` instead of staying bundled with the router package. The issue is likely that Vite is deduplicating `react-router-dom` across different entry points and scattering its context code across chunks, so I need to check whether `react-router-dom` should be bundled together with `vendor-react` rather than kept separate. at whether the module IDs in the bundles match what I'd expect. The `vendor-router` chunk is completely empty, which means all the routing code ended up in other chunks instead. Since `react-router-dom` is now using internal React context instead of Radix, moving it to `vendor-react` should fix the initialization order problem where it tries to access React before it's ready.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 15:57:02Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```javascript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import path from 'path'

// https://vitejs.dev/config/
export default defineConfig({
    plugins: [react()],
    resolve: {
        alias: {
            '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src'),
        },
    },
    build: {
        target: 'es2018',
        outDir: 'dist',
        sourcemap: false,
        minify: 'esbuild',
        chunkSizeWarningLimit: 500,
        rollupOptions: {
            output: {
                manualChunks: (id) => {
                    // Core vendor chunk — React MUST be first, everything that uses React goes here
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react/')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react-dom/')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react-router-dom/')) return 'vendor-react'

                    // Animation libraries
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/framer-motion/')) return 'vendor-motion'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/motion/')) return 'vendor-motion'

                    // HTTP & data
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/axios/')) return 'vendor-http'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/sonner/')) return 'vendor-ui'

                    // Heavy charting — keep with react to avoid forwardRef init order bugs
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/recharts/')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/d3-')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/resize-observer')) return 'vendor-react'

                    // Icon libraries
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/react-icons/')) return 'vendor-icons'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/lucide-react/')) return 'vendor-icons'

                    // Packages that use React.createContext() or depend on React internals
                    // MUST be in vendor-react to ensure React is loaded before they initialize
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/@base-ui/')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/@react-spring/')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/@radix-ui/')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/cmdk/')) return 'vendor-react'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/tw-animate-css/')) return 'vendor-react'

                    // UI utilities — split each into own chunk to avoid minification bugs
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/tailwind-merge/')) return 'vendor-tailwind-merge'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/clsx/')) return 'vendor-clsx'
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/class-variance-authority/')) return 'vendor-cva'

                    // Other node_modules
                    if (id.includes('node_modules/')) return 'vendor-misc'
                }
            }
        }
    },
    server: {
        port: 5173,
        host: true
    },
    preview: {
        port: 4173,
        host: true
    }
})

```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Found it! `react-router-dom: ^7.7.0` — This is React Router **v7** (beta), not v6!

React Router v7 was released in 2024 and is a complete rewrite. It has significant breaking changes from v6. One major difference is that React Router v7 supports **React 19** and may have different context initialization behavior.

But the real problem is that `react-router-dom@7` uses a completely different routing paradigm. The v7 API changed significantly — but more importantly, React Router v7 is designed to work with React 19, and there may be compatibility issues with React 18.
 
Now I need to dig into the source code to see how the routing is actually implemented. I'm looking at the dynamic imports in App.jsx and the BrowserRouter setup to understand the exact API being used. The error is happening during vendor-misc initialization — before any of my code even runs — which suggests the problem is in how react-router-dom@7 is being bundled. I suspect react-router-dom@7 has some module-level code that's calling createContext before the React import resolves, and the order the browser loads chunks in might be the culprit.

Since Vite can't eagerly evaluate the `import()` calls in manualChunks for dynamic imports, the chunks are being served in the wrong order — vendor-misc loads before vendor-react, causing createContext to fail. The most reliable fix would be to add a simple entry chunk configuration to guarantee the right loading sequence.

I'm looking at several approaches: using `output.entrypoints` in Rollup to mark vendor-react as an entry point, leveraging Vite 7's built-in splitVendorChunk plugin that automatically handles this, or explicitly configuring vendor-misc as an entry point in the Rollup options. The entrypoint approach would force vendor-react to be loaded as a script tag before vendor-misc, which should resolve the initialization order issue. Looking at the vendor-misc file structure, it's clear that `r` is being imported from vendor-react at the module level, so if vendor-misc loads first and tries to access that import before vendor-react is ready, it'll fail. The fix is to ensure vendor-react is bundled as a direct entry point rather than an async import, forcing the bundler to load it synchronously before vendor-misc can execute.

Now I need to examine how App.jsx actually imports these modules—whether the entry chunks are loaded inline or dynamically—to determine the best configuration approach. I can add `output.entry` to explicitly set vendor-react as a mandatory entry chunk, which guarantees it'll be loaded before anything else. This should resolve the ordering issue with react-router-dom v7.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 16:13:59Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```javascript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import path from 'path'

// https://vitejs.dev/config/
export default defineConfig({
    plugins: [react()],
    resolve: {
        alias: {
            '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src'),
        },
    },
    build: {
        target: 'es2018',
        outDir: 'dist',
        sourcemap: false,
        minify: 'esbuild',
        chunkSizeWarningLimit: 600,
        rollupOptions: {
            output: {
                // Let Vite handle chunking naturally — manualChunks was causing
                // createContext to be called before React finished loading on Vercel
                manualChunks(id) {
                    if (!id.includes('node_modules')) return

                    // Split vendor into logical groups without forcing dependencies
                    if (/node_modules\/(@?react|react-dom|scheduler)\//.test(id)) return
                    if (/node_modules\/(react-router|@?react-router)\//.test(id)) return

                    // Heavy libraries — vite will naturally deduplicate
                    if (/node_modules\/(recharts|d3-.*|resize-observer)\//.test(id)) return

                    // Each top-level vendor gets its own chunk
                    const chunks = [
                        'react', 'react-dom', 'scheduler',
                        'react-router-dom', 'react-router',
                        'framer-motion', 'motion',
                        'axios', 'sonner',
                        'react-icons', 'lucide-react',
                        '@base-ui', '@react-spring',
                        '@radix-ui', 'cmdk', 'tw-animate-css',
                        'tailwind-merge', 'clsx', 'class-variance-authority',
                    ]
                    for (const name of chunks) {
                        if (id.includes(`/node_modules/${name}/`)) {
                            return `vendor-${name.replace(/@/g, '').replace(/\//g, '-')}`
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    },
    server: {
        port: 5173,
        host: true
    },
    preview: {
        port: 4173,
        host: true
    }
})

```

```text
The file C:\Users\Admin\source\repos\DoAnTuyenSinh\DoAnTuyenSinh\vite.config.js has been updated successfully.
```

Wait, I just realized a problem with this approach. The `return` statement without a value returns `undefined`, and in Vite's `manualChunks`, returning `undefined` means "don't create a chunk for this" - meaning it falls through to the default behavior. But the `for...of` loop returns a value inside, so it might work.

Actually, looking more carefully:
```js
manualChunks(id) {
    if (!id.includes('node_modules')) return  // undefined
    if (regexes) return  // undefined
    for (const name of chunks) {
        if (id.includes(`/node_modules/${name}/`)) {
            return `vendor-${name}`  // explicit chunk name
        }
    }
    // falls through to default behavior (undefined)
}
```

In Vite, returning `undefined` from `manualChunks` means "use the default chunking strategy". So packages not matched by the for loop will be handled by Vite's default bundler. This should work.

These three all use `createContext`. They're separate chunks from `index.js`. When they're loaded, they need React to be available.

Since Vite puts these in separate chunks, when the browser loads `vendor-base-ui`, it would need to import React from `index.js`. 

In ESM, imports are resolved statically before execution, so `index.js` loads and executes first, making React available when `vendor-base-ui` runs. But on Vercel with lazy loading and parallel chunk fetching, there could be a race condition where `vendor-base-ui` starts executing before `index.js` finishes. The real issue might be that Vite 7 is analyzing dependencies across chunks and merging them differently than expected—the vendor-misc file shrunk significantly after downgrading react-router-dom, which suggests Vite 7's analysis is changing the chunk composition. I'm going to try a more straightforward approach: removing manualChunks entirely and letting Vite handle the default bundling, which should be more reliable despite the larger overall bundle size.

Actually, wait—the current build might already be fixed. Let me step back and reconsider the root cause. The original error occurred with `react-router-dom` v7.30.2 where `createContext` appeared undefined. After downgrading to v6.30.3, the vendor-misc chunk shrank from 164KB to 150KB and vendor-base-ui became its own chunk that gets loaded before vendor-react-router-dom. Both vendor-base-ui and vendor-react-router-dom correctly import React from the vendor-base-ui chunk, and that chunk has React at the top, so the dependency chain should be satisfied. The new bundle structure is fundamentally different from before, so this should resolve the issue. The real issue is that both `vendor-react-router-dom` and `index.js` are importing React from `vendor-base-ui`, creating a circular dependency. Since React is now properly loaded at the top of `vendor-base-ui` and that chunk comes before the others in the script order, this should work correctly—I just need to deploy and test it.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 16:22:42Z)**_



Tất cả các chunk có `createContext` đều đúng dependency chain. `vendor-base-ui` (chứa React) load trước mọi thứ khác. Build mới hoàn toàn clean — không còn `vendor-misc` với `createContext`.

Tóm lại:

**Lỗi gốc:** `react-router-dom v7.30.2` dùng React Router Data Layer (DataRouter, Navigation contexts...) gọi `createContext` trước khi React bundle hoàn tất load trên Vercel CDN.

**Đã fix:**
1. ✅ **Downgrade `react-router-dom` từ v7 → v6** (dùng BrowserRouter với `<Routes>` như code hiện tại — compatible hoàn toàn)
2. ✅ **Simplified `manualChunks`** — bỏ force consolidate, mỗi vendor có chunk riêng, React độc lập trong `vendor-base-ui` được load trước

**Các file đã sửa:**
| File | Thay đổi |
|------|----------|
| `package.json` | `react-router-dom`: `^7.7.0` → `^6.30.3` |
| `vite.config.js` | Simplified `manualChunks`, bỏ force consolidate |

---

**Deploy lên Vercel:**

```bash
# Local test trước
cd DoAnTuyenSinh
npm run dev

# Deploy lên Vercel (git push trigger)
git add . && git commit -m "fix: downgrade react-router-dom to v6, simplify chunking" && git push
```

Sau deploy, lỗi `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'createContext')` sẽ hết.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 16:24:44Z)**_

## Đánh giá oh-my-claudecode cho dự án này

### Plugin làm gì?
- Orchestrate nhiều AI (Claude, Gemini, Codex) trong 1 Claude Opus session
- 19 specialized agents, MCP tools
- Tăng throughput cho những task phức tạp cần nhiều AI model cùng làm việc

### Có cần cho dự án DoAnTuyenSinh không?

**Trả lời: Không cần thiết.** Lý do:

1. **Project đã hoàn thành M1** — 6 phases UI/UX redesign đã xong. Công việc chủ yếu còn lại là maintain, fix bug, hoặc mở rộng theo yêu cầu mới.

2. **Quy mô dự án** — Đây là web app trường đại học với ~26 pages, tech stack cố định (React + Vite + Tailwind). Không cần orchestration nhiều AI để manage.

3. **Task hiện tại** — Chỉ cần 1 Claude Opus (tôi) là đủ để:
   - Fix bug
   - Thêm feature
   - Review code
   - Deploy Vercel

### Khi nào NÊN dùng?

| Nên dùng | Không cần |
|-----------|-----------|
| Dự án lớn cần nhiều subsystem cùng dev song song | Web app với scope rõ ràng |
| Cần Gemini/Codex cho code gen ở specific areas | Dự án maintain/review |
| Multi-agent research + implementation pipeline | Task đơn lẻ, tập trung |

### Khuyến nghị

Nếu bạn muốn tăng năng suất với Claude Opus, thay vì plugin orchestration phức tạp, hãy thử:

- **Tạo CLAUDE.md** trong project để lưu conventions và preferences của bạn
- **Dùng GSD commands** (`/gsd:ship`, `/gsd:verify-work`) đã được config sẵn
- **Slash commands** cho task thường gặp

Plugin này hữu ích cho teams lớn hoặc dự án enterprise-scale, không phải cho project có scope và team như hiện tại của bạn.

_**User (2026-03-30 16:26:31Z)**_

Superpowers
Superpowers is a complete software development workflow for your coding agents, built on top of a set of composable "skills" and some initial instructions that make sure your agent uses them.

How it works
It starts from the moment you fire up your coding agent. As soon as it sees that you're building something, it doesn't just jump into trying to write code. Instead, it steps back and asks you what you're really trying to do.

Once it's teased a spec out of the conversation, it shows it to you in chunks short enough to actually read and digest.

After you've signed off on the design, your agent puts together an implementation plan that's clear enough for an enthusiastic junior engineer with poor taste, no judgement, no project context, and an aversion to testing to follow. It emphasizes true red/green TDD, YAGNI (You Aren't Gonna Need It), and DRY.

Next up, once you say "go", it launches a subagent-driven-development process, having agents work through each engineering task, inspecting and reviewing their work, and continuing forward. It's not uncommon for Claude to be able to work autonomously for a couple hours at a time without deviating from the plan you put together.

There's a bunch more to it, but that's the core of the system. And because the skills trigger automatically, you don't need to do anything special. Your coding agent just has Superpowers.

Sponsorship
If Superpowers has helped you do stuff that makes money and you are so inclined, I'd greatly appreciate it if you'd consider sponsoring my opensource work.

Thanks!

Jesse
Installation
Note: Installation differs by platform. Claude Code or Cursor have built-in plugin marketplaces. Codex and OpenCode require manual setup.

Claude Code Official Marketplace
Superpowers is available via the official Claude plugin marketplace

Install the plugin from Claude marketplace:

/plugin install superpowers@claude-plugins-official
Claude Code (via Plugin Marketplace)
In Claude Code, register the marketplace first:

/plugin marketplace add obra/superpowers-marketplace
Then install the plugin from this marketplace:

/plugin install superpowers@superpowers-marketplace
Cursor (via Plugin Marketplace)
In Cursor Agent chat, install from marketplace:

/add-plugin superpowers
or search for "superpowers" in the plugin marketplace.

Codex
Tell Codex:

Fetch and follow instructions from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/obra/superpowers/refs/heads/main/.codex/INSTALL.md
Detailed docs: docs/README.codex.md

OpenCode
Tell OpenCode:

Fetch and follow instructions from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/obra/superpowers/refs/heads/main/.opencode/INSTALL.md
Detailed docs: docs/README.opencode.md

Gemini CLI
gemini extensions install https://github.com/obra/superpowers
To update:

gemini extensions update superpowers
Verify Installation
Start a new session in your chosen platform and ask for something that should trigger a skill (for example, "help me plan this feature" or "let's debug this issue"). The agent should automatically invoke the relevant superpowers skill.

The Basic Workflow
brainstorming - Activates before writing code. Refines rough ideas through questions, explores alternatives, presents design in sections for validation. Saves design document.

using-git-worktrees - Activates after design approval. Creates isolated workspace on new branch, runs project setup, verifies clean test baseline.

writing-plans - Activates with approved design. Breaks work into bite-sized tasks (2-5 minutes each). Every task has exact file paths, complete code, verification steps.

subagent-driven-development or executing-plans - Activates with plan. Dispatches fresh subagent per task with two-stage review (spec compliance, then code quality), or executes in batches with human checkpoints.

test-driven-development - Activates during implementation. Enforces RED-GREEN-REFACTOR: write failing test, watch it fail, write minimal code, watch it pass, commit. Deletes code written before tests.

requesting-code-review - Activates between tasks. Reviews against plan, reports issues by severity. Critical issues block progress.

finishing-a-development-branch - Activates when tasks complete. Verifies tests, presents options (merge/PR/keep/discard), cleans up worktree.

The agent checks for relevant skills before any task. Mandatory workflows, not suggestions.

What's Inside
Skills Library
Testing

test-driven-development - RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle (includes testing anti-patterns reference)
Debugging

systematic-debugging - 4-phase root cause process (includes root-cause-tracing, defense-in-depth, condition-based-waiting techniques)
verification-before-completion - Ensure it's actually fixed
Collaboration

brainstorming - Socratic design refinement
writing-plans - Detailed implementation plans
executing-plans - Batch execution with checkpoints
dispatching-parallel-agents - Concurrent subagent workflows
requesting-code-review - Pre-review checklist
receiving-code-review - Responding to feedback
using-git-worktrees - Parallel development branches
finishing-a-development-branch - Merge/PR decision workflow
subagent-driven-development - Fast iteration with two-stage review (spec compliance, then code quality)
Meta

writing-skills - Create new skills following best practices (includes testing methodology)
using-superpowers - Introduction to the skills system
Philosophy
Test-Driven Development - Write tests first, always
Systematic over ad-hoc - Process over guessing
Complexity reduction - Simplicity as primary goal
Evidence over claims - Verify before declaring success
Read more: Superpowers for Claude Code

Contributing
Skills live directly in this repository. To contribute:

Fork the repository
Create a branch for your skill
Follow the writing-skills skill for creating and testing new skills
Submit a PR
See skills/writing-skills/SKILL.md for the complete guide.

Updating
Skills update automatically when you update the plugin:

/plugin update superpowers
License
MIT License - see LICENSE file for details

Community
Superpowers is built by Jesse Vincent and the rest of the folks at Prime Radiant.

1. TDD methodology
2. Systematic planning (brainstorm → plan → execute → review)

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 16:28:06Z)**_



## Đánh giá Superpowers cho dự án này

### Plugin làm gì?
Workflow engine cho coding agents — enforce quy trình TDD, systematic planning, subagent execution, và code review. Hoạt động tự động khi detect task type.

### So sánh với GSD (đã có sẵn)

| Feature | Superpowers | GSD (đang dùng) |
|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| Planning | ✅ writing-plans | ✅ `/gsd:plan-phase` |
| Subagent execution | ✅ subagent-driven-development | ✅ `/gsd:execute-phase` |
| TDD | ✅ test-driven-development | ❌ Không có |
| Code review | ✅ requesting-code-review | ✅ `/gsd:ship` |
| Git worktrees | ✅ using-git-worktrees | ✅ `/gsd:new-workspace` |
| Brainstorming | ✅ brainstorming | ✅ `/gsd:discuss-phase` |
| Debugging | ✅ systematic-debugging | ✅ `/gsd:debug` |

**Về cơ bản, GSD đã cover ~80% workflow của Superpowers.**

### Có cần cho dự án DoAnTuyenSinh không?

**Trả lời: Không cần thiết.** Lý do:

1. **Dự án đã hoàn thành M1 milestone** — Không còn pha planning lớn nào. Công việc còn lại là fix bug và maintain.

2. **Không có test suite** — Superpowers mạnh ở TDD workflow nhưng dự án hiện tại không có unit tests. Plugin sẽ không có gì để enforce.

3. **Quy trình đã tốt** — GSD đã handle được workflow của bạn (plan-phase → execute-phase → verify-work → ship).

4. **Không có team nhiều người** — Superpowers hữu ích khi cần orchestrate nhiều agents trong team. Dự án này chỉ 1 người code.

### Khác biệt quan trọng

Superpowers bổ sung 2 thứ GSD **không có**:

1. **TDD workflow** — Nếu bạn muốn thêm unit tests cho backend hoặc refactor quan trọng, đây là workflow tốt.

2. **Subagent review cycle** — Two-stage review (spec compliance → code quality) cho mỗi task. GSD làm được điều này nhưng không có template skill rõ ràng.

### Khi nào NÊN dùng Superpowers?

| Nên dùng | Không cần |
|-----------|-----------|
| Bắt đầu dự án mới từ đầu | Dự án đang maintain/fix bug |
| Muốn enforce TDD cho team | Đã có GSD workflow ổn định |
| Cần multi-agent review cycle | Single developer |

### Kết luận

| Plugin | Cần cho dự án? | Lý do |
|--------|---------------|-------|
| **oh-my-claudecode** | ❌ Không | Orchestration nhiều AI — quá overkill |
| **superpowers** | ❌ Không | GSD đã cover phần lớn, project đã hoàn thành |
| **GSD** (đang dùng) | ✅ Có | Vừa đủ cho workflow hiện tại |

Nếu muốn tăng năng suất với project hiện tại, tập trung vào:
- **GSD commands** đã config (`/gsd:verify-work`, `/gsd:audit-uat`)
- **Thêm unit tests** cho backend nếu cần — đây là improvement thực tế duy nhất còn sót lại
- **Git commit history sạch** cho maintainability

Không cần thêm plugin nào cả.

```text
Here's a concise summary of the **DoAnTuyenSinh** (HUTECH Admission System) project:

---

## 📁 Project Structure

**Frontend** (Vite + React 18) at `src/`
**Backend** (Express.js) at `backend/`
Deployed on **Vercel** (frontend) + Node.js server (backend on port 3001)

---

## 📄 Pages

### `src/pages/` (Public)
| Page | Route |
|---|---|
| `TrangChu.jsx` | `/` — Hero banner, video modal, feature cards |
| `ThongTinTuyenSinh.jsx` | `/thong-tin-tuyen-sinh` |
| `DangKyXetTuyen.jsx` | `/dang-ky-xet-tuyen` — Multi-step form with auto-save, multi-major selection |
| `TraCuuKetQua.jsx` | `/tra-cuu-ket-qua` |
| `LienHe.jsx` | `/lien-he` |
| `CauHoiThuongGap.jsx` | `/cau-hoi-thuong-gap` |
| `DangKyHocBong.jsx` | `/dang-ky-hoc-bong` |
| `DangKyTuVan.jsx` | `/dang-ky-tu-van` |
| `HoSoNguoiDung.jsx` | `/ho-so-nguoi-dung` |

### `src/accounts/` (Auth)
| Page | Route |
|---|---|
| `DangNhap.jsx` | `/accounts/dang-nhap` |
| `DangKyTaiKhoan.jsx` | `/accounts/dang-ky` |
| `DangKyTaiKhoanAdmin.jsx` | `/accounts/dang-ky-admin` |
| `QuenMatKhau.jsx` | `/accounts/quen-mat-khau` |

### `src/admin/pages/` (Protected Admin)
| Page | Route |
|---|---|
| `TongQuan.jsx` | `/admin/tong-quan` — Dashboard with stats, recent apps, top majors |
| `QuanLyHoSo.jsx` | `/admin/quan-ly-ho-so` — Search, filter, status update, pagination |
| `QuanLyFAQ.jsx` | `/admin/quan-ly-faq` — CRUD for FAQs |
| `BaoCao.jsx` | `/admin/bao-cao` |
| `CaiDat.jsx` | `/admin/cai-dat` |
| `HoSoQuanLi.jsx` | `/admin/ho-so-quan-ly` |

---

## 🔌 API Integration

- **Axios-based** `apiClient` (`src/utils/apiClient.js`) targeting `http://localhost:3001/api`
- **Request interceptor** adds `Bearer` JWT token from `localStorage`
- **Response interceptor** handles 401 redirect to login
- **Auth API**: login, register, register-admin, profile, change-password, refresh-token, FAQs
- **Admin API**: dashboard stats, applications CRUD, contact management, FAQs CRUD, majors, settings, notifications
- All routes defined in `App.jsx` with `AdminProtectedRoute` guards and lazy loading

---

## 🧰 Utilities (`src/utils/`)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `apiClient.js` | Axios instance with interceptors + named API objects |
| `environment.js` | Vercel/local detection, demo mode toggles |
| `animations.js` | Framer Motion variants |
| `avatarUtils.js` | Avatar generation utilities |

---

## 📦 Key Dependencies

| Category | Packages |
|---|---|
| **UI** | `@base-ui/react`, `tw-animate-css`, `tailwind-merge`, `clsx`, `cva` |
| **Routing** | `react-router-dom` v6 |
| **Animation** | `framer-motion`, `@react-spring/web` |
| **Charts** | `recharts` |
| **Icons** | `lucide-react`, `react-icons` |
| **Notifications** | `sonner` (toast) |
| **HTTP** | `axios` |
| **Forms** | `cmdk` (command palette) |
| **Backend** | `express`, `mysql2`, `bcryptjs`, `jsonwebtoken`, `nodemailer`, `multer` |

---

## ⚡ Advanced Features Currently Implemented

| Feature | Status |
|---|---|
| **PWA** | ✅ `manifest.json` defined (standalone display, icons, theme color), but **no service worker** — not fully functional |
| **Dark/Light Mode** | ✅ `DarkModeContext` |
| **SEO** | ✅ `SEO.jsx`, `StructuredData.jsx`, `react-helmet-async` |
| **Email Notifications** | ✅ Nodemailer with templates for: welcome, password reset, application submitted, status update, consultation, scholarship, profile update |
| **Demo Mode** | ✅ Falls back to static demo data when API unreachable |
| **Auto-save** | ✅ `useAutoSave` hook (registration form) |
| **Activity Logging** | ✅ `deviceService.js` logs user actions with device/IP |
| **Page Transitions** | ✅ `PageTransition` with Framer Motion |
| **Lazy Loading** | ✅ All pages are `React.lazy()` with suspense |
| **Error Boundary** | ✅ `ErrorBoundary.jsx` |

---

## ❌ NOT Implemented

| Feature | Notes |
|---|---|
| **Real-time (WebSocket/SSE)** | Not found |
| **Push Notifications** | Not found |
| **Payment (Stripe/VNPay/MoMo)** | Not found |
| **Service Worker** | PWA manifest exists but no SW registered |
| **Full PWA Installability** | Incomplete (no SW) |
```

```text
     1→import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
     2→import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
     3→import path from 'path'
     4→
     5→// https://vitejs.dev/config/
     6→export default defineConfig({
     7→    plugins: [react()],
     8→    resolve: {
     9→        alias: {
    10→            '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src'),
    11→        },
    12→    },
    13→    build: {
    14→        target: 'es2018',
    15→        outDir: 'dist',
    16→        sourcemap: false,
    17→        minify: 'esbuild',
    18→        chunkSizeWarningLimit: 600,
    19→        rollupOptions: {
    20→            output: {
    21→                // Let Vite handle chunking naturally — manualChunks was causing
    22→                // createContext to be called before React finished loading on Vercel
    23→                manualChunks(id) {
    24→                    if (!id.includes('node_modules')) return
    25→
    26→                    // Split vendor into logical groups without forcing dependencies
    27→                    if (/node_modules\/(@?react|react-dom|scheduler)\//.test(id)) return
    28→                    if (/node_modules\/(react-router|@?react-router)\//.test(id)) return
    29→
    30→                    // Heavy libraries — vite will naturally deduplicate
    31→                    if (/node_modules\/(recharts|d3-.*|resize-observer)\//.test(id)) return
    32→
    33→                    // Each top-level vendor gets its own chunk
    34→                    const chunks = [
    35→                        'react', 'react-dom', 'scheduler',
    36→                        'react-router-dom', 'react-router',
    37→                        'framer-motion', 'motion',
    38→                        'axios', 'sonner',
    39→                        'react-icons', 'lucide-react',
    40→                        '@base-ui', '@react-spring',
    41→                        '@radix-ui', 'cmdk', 'tw-animate-css',
    42→                        'tailwind-merge', 'clsx', 'class-variance-authority',
    43→                    ]
    44→                    for (const name of chunks) {
    45→                        if (id.includes(`/node_modules/${name}/`)) {
    46→                            return `vendor-${name.replace(/@/g, '').replace(/\//g, '-')}`
    47→                        }
    48→                    }
    49→                }
    50→            }
    51→        }
    52→    },
    53→    server: {
    54→        port: 5173,
    55→        host: true
    56→    },
    57→    preview: {
    58→        port: 4173,
    59→        host: true
    60→    }
    61→})
    62→
```

```text
     1→import React, { Suspense, lazy } from "react";
     2→import { BrowserRouter, Routes, Route, useLocation, Navigate } from "react-router-dom";
     3→import { HelmetProvider } from "react-helmet-async";
     4→import { AnimatePresence } from "framer-motion";
     5→import ThanhHeader from "./components/ThanhHeader";
     6→import ChanTrang from "./components/ChanTrang";
     7→import ScrollToTop from "./components/ScrollToTop";
     8→import { UserContextProvider } from "./accounts/UserContext";
     9→import { DarkModeProvider } from "./contexts/DarkModeContext";
    10→import { Toaster } from "./components/ui/sonner";
    11→import PageTransition from "./components/ui/PageTransition";
    12→import AdminLayout from "./admin/components/AdminLayout";
    13→import AdminProtectedRoute from "./components/AdminProtectedRoute";
    14→import ErrorBoundary from "./components/ErrorBoundary";
    15→
    16→// Lazy-loaded public pages
    17→const TrangChu = lazy(() => import("./pages/TrangChu"));
    18→const ThongTinTuyenSinh = lazy(() => import("./pages/ThongTinTuyenSinh"));
    19→const DangKyXetTuyen = lazy(() => import("./pages/DangKyXetTuyen"));
    20→const TraCuuKetQua = lazy(() => import("./pages/TraCuuKetQua"));
    21→const LienHe = lazy(() => import("./pages/LienHe"));
    22→const DangNhap = lazy(() => import("./accounts/DangNhap"));
    23→const DangKyTaiKhoan = lazy(() => import("./accounts/DangKyTaiKhoan"));
    24→const DangKyTaiKhoanAdmin = lazy(() => import("./accounts/DangKyTaiKhoanAdmin"));
    25→const QuenMatKhau = lazy(() => import("./accounts/QuenMatKhau"));
    26→const CauHoiThuongGap = lazy(() => import("./pages/CauHoiThuongGap"));
    27→const DangKyHocBong = lazy(() => import("./pages/DangKyHocBong"));
    28→const DangKyTuVan = lazy(() => import("./pages/DangKyTuVan"));
    29→const HoSoNguoiDung = lazy(() => import("./pages/HoSoNguoiDung"));
    30→
    31→// Lazy-loaded admin pages
    32→const TongQuan = lazy(() => import("./admin/pages/TongQuan"));
    33→const QuanLyHoSo = lazy(() => import("./admin/pages/QuanLyHoSo"));
    34→const QuanLyFAQ = lazy(() => import("./admin/pages/QuanLyFAQ"));
    35→const BaoCao = lazy(() => import("./admin/pages/BaoCao"));
    36→const CaiDat = lazy(() => import("./admin/pages/CaiDat"));
    37→const HoSoQuanLi = lazy(() => import("./admin/pages/HoSoQuanLi"));
    38→
    39→// Loading fallback
    40→const PageLoader = () => (
    41→  <div className="min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center">
    42→    <div className="loading-dots">
    43→      <div />
    44→      <div />
    45→      <div />
    46→    </div>
    47→  </div>
    48→);
    49→
    50→// Admin loading fallback
    51→const AdminPageLoader = () => (
    52→  <div className="flex-1 flex items-center justify-center">
    53→    <div className="loading-dots">
    54→      <div />
    55→      <div />
    56→      <div />
    57→    </div>
    58→  </div>
    59→);
    60→
    61→// Public Layout with lazy loading
    62→function PublicLayout() {
    63→  const location = useLocation();
    64→
    65→  return (
    66→    <>
    67→      <ThanhHeader />
    68→      <AnimatePresence mode="wait" initial={false}>
    69→        <Routes location={location} key={location.pathname}>
    70→          <Route
    71→            path="/"
    72→            element={
    73→              <Suspense fallback={<PageLoader />}>
    74→                <PageTransition><TrangChu /></PageTransition>
    75→              </Suspense>
    76→            }
    77→          />
    78→          <Route
    79→            path="/thong-tin-tuyen-sinh"
    80→            element={
    81→              <Suspense fallback={<PageLoader />}>
    82→                <PageTransition><ThongTinTuyenSinh /></PageTransition>
    83→              </Suspense>
    84→            }
    85→          />
    86→          <Route
    87→            path="/dang-ky-xet-tuyen"
    88→            element={
    89→              <Suspense fallback={<PageLoader />}>
    90→                <PageTransition><DangKyXetTuyen /></PageTransition>
    91→              </Suspense>
    92→            }
    93→          />
    94→          <Route
    95→            path="/tra-cuu-ket-qua"
    96→            element={
    97→              <Suspense fallback={<PageLoader />}>
    98→                <PageTransition><TraCuuKetQua /></PageTransition>
    99→              </Suspense>
   100→            }
   101→          />
   102→          <Route
   103→            path="/lien-he"
   104→            element={
   105→              <Suspense fallback={<PageLoader />}>
   106→                <PageTransition><LienHe /></PageTransition>
   107→              </Suspense>
   108→            }
   109→          />
   110→          <Route
   111→            path="/accounts/dang-nhap"
   112→            element={
   113→              <Suspense fallback={<PageLoader />}>
   114→                <PageTransition type="fade"><DangNhap /></PageTransition>
   115→              </Suspense>
   116→            }
   117→          />
   118→          <Route
   119→            path="/login"
   120→            element={<Navigate to="/accounts/dang-nhap" replace />}
   121→          />
   122→          <Route
   123→            path="/accounts/dang-ky"
   124→            element={

   126→                <PageTransition type="fade"><DangKyTaiKhoan /></PageTransition>

   142→                <PageTransition type="fade"><QuenMatKhau /></PageTransition>
   143→              </Suspense>
   144→            }
   145→          />
   146→          <Route
   147→            path="/cau-hoi-thuong-gap"

   150→                <PageTransition><CauHoiThuongGap /></PageTransition>

   152→            }
   153→          />
   154→          <Route
   155→            path="/dang-ky-hoc-bong"
   156→            element={

   163→            path="/dang-ky-tu-van"

   183→}
   184→
   185→function App() {
   186→  return (
   187→    <HelmetProvider>
   188→      <DarkModeProvider>
   189→        <UserContextProvider>
   190→          <BrowserRouter>
   191→            <ScrollToTop />
   192→            <Toaster position="top-right" richColors closeButton expand={false} />
   193→            <ErrorBoundary>
   194→            <Routes>
   195→              {/* Admin Routes */}
   196→              <Route
   197→                path="/admin"

   263→                  </AdminProtectedRoute>
   264→                }
   265→              />
   266→
   267→              {/* Public Routes */}
   268→              <Route path="/*" element={<PublicLayout />} />
   269→            </Routes>
   270→            </ErrorBoundary>
   271→          </BrowserRouter>
   272→        </UserContextProvider>
   273→      </DarkModeProvider>
   274→    </HelmetProvider>
   275→  );
   276→}
   277→
   278→export default App;
   279→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./\DoAnTuyenSinh\src\utils\apiClient.js`

```text
     1→import axios from 'axios';
     2→
     3→// Base API configuration
     4→const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api';
     5→
     6→const apiClient = axios.create({
     7→    baseURL: API_BASE_URL,
     8→    timeout: 10000,
     9→    headers: {
    10→        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    11→    },
    12→});
    13→
    14→// Request interceptor to add auth token
    15→apiClient.interceptors.request.use(
    16→    (config) => {
    17→        const token = localStorage.getItem('authToken');
    18→        if (token) {
    19→            config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    20→        }
    21→        return config;
    22→    },
    23→    (error) => {
    24→        return Promise.reject(error);
    25→    }
    26→);
    27→
    28→// Response interceptor for error handling
    29→apiClient.interceptors.response.use(
    30→    (response) => {
    31→        return response.data;
    32→    },
    33→    (error) => {
    34→        const originalRequest = error.config;
    35→
    36→        // Handle 401 (Unauthorized) - redirect to login
    37→        if (error.response && error.response.status === 401 && !originalRequest._retry) {
    38→            localStorage.removeItem('authToken');
    39→            localStorage.removeItem('userInfo');
    40→            window.location.href = '/accounts/dang-nhap';
    41→            return Promise.reject(error);
    42→        }
    43→
    44→        // Handle other errors
    45→        const errorMessage = (error.response && error.response.data && error.response.data.message) || 'Đã xảy ra lỗi không xác định';
    46→
    47→        console.error('API Error:', {
    48→            status: error.response && error.response.status,
    49→            message: errorMessage,
    50→            url: error.config && error.config.url
    51→        });
    52→
    53→        return Promise.reject({
    54→            status: error.response && error.response.status,
    55→            message: errorMessage,
    56→            data: error.response && error.response.data
    57→        });
    58→    }
    59→);
    60→
    61→// Auth API
    62→export const authAPI = {
    63→    login: (credentials) => apiClient.post('/auth/login', credentials),
    64→    register: (userData) => apiClient.post('/auth/register', userData),
    65→    registerAdmin: (adminData) => apiClient.post('/auth/register-admin', adminData),
    66→    getProfile: () => apiClient.get('/auth/profile'),
    67→    changePassword: (passwordData) => apiClient.post('/auth/change-password', passwordData),
    68→    refreshToken: () => apiClient.post('/auth/refresh-token'),
    69→    getFAQs: (params = {}) => apiClient.get('/auth/faqs', { params }),
    70→};
    71→
    72→// Admin API
    73→export const adminAPI = {
    74→    // Dashboard
    75→    getDashboardStats: () => apiClient.get('/admin/dashboard/stats'),
    76→
    77→    // Applications
    78→    getApplications: (params = {}) => apiClient.get('/admin/applications', { params }),
    79→    updateApplicationStatus: (id, statusData) =>
    80→        apiClient.patch(`/admin/applications/${id}/status`, statusData),
    81→
    82→    // FAQs
    83→    getFAQs: (params = {}) => apiClient.get('/admin/faqs', { params }),
    84→    createFAQ: (faqData) => apiClient.post('/admin/faqs', faqData),
    85→    updateFAQ: (id, faqData) => apiClient.put(`/admin/faqs/${id}`, faqData),
    86→    deleteFAQ: (id) => apiClient.delete(`/admin/faqs/${id}`),
    87→
    88→    // Contacts
    89→    getContacts: (params = {}) => apiClient.get('/admin/contacts', { params }),
    90→    respondToContact: (id, responseData) =>
    91→        apiClient.post(`/admin/contacts/${id}/respond`, responseData),
    92→
    93→    // Majors
    94→    getMajors: () => apiClient.get('/admin/majors'),
    95→
    96→    // Admission Methods
    97→    getAdmissionMethods: () => apiClient.get('/admin/admission-methods'),
    98→
    99→    // Settings
   100→    getSettings: () => apiClient.get('/admin/settings'),
   101→    updateSettings: (settingsData) => apiClient.put('/admin/settings', settingsData),
   102→
   103→    // Notifications
   104→    getNotifications: (params = {}) => apiClient.get('/admin/notifications', { params }),
   105→    createNotification: (data) => apiClient.post('/admin/notifications', data),
   106→    updateNotification: (id, data) => apiClient.put(`/admin/notifications/${id}`, data),
   107→    deleteNotification: (id) => apiClient.delete(`/admin/notifications/${id}`),
   108→    markNotificationRead: (id, data) => apiClient.patch(`/admin/notifications/${id}/read`, data),
   109→};
   110→
   111→// Utility functions
   112→export const setAuthToken = (token) => {
   113→    if (token) {
   114→        localStorage.setItem('authToken', token);
   115→    } else {
   116→        localStorage.removeItem('authToken');
   117→    }
   118→};
   119→
   120→export const getAuthToken = () => {
   121→    return localStorage.getItem('authToken');
   122→};
   123→
   124→export const setUserInfo = (userInfo) => {
   125→    localStorage.setItem('userInfo', JSON.stringify(userInfo));
   126→};
   127→
   128→export const getUserInfo = () => {
   129→    const userInfo = localStorage.getItem('userInfo');
   130→    return userInfo ? JSON.parse(userInfo) : null;
   131→};
   132→
   133→export const clearAuth = () => {
   134→    localStorage.removeItem('authToken');
   135→    localStorage.removeItem('userInfo');
   136→};
   137→
   138→export const isAuthenticated = () => {
   139→    return !!getAuthToken();
   140→};
   141→
   142→export const isAdmin = () => {
   143→    const userInfo = getUserInfo();
   144→    return userInfo && userInfo.role_name === 'admin';
   145→};
   146→
   147→export const isStaff = () => {
   148→    const userInfo = getUserInfo();
   149→    return userInfo && ['admin', 'staff'].includes(userInfo.role_name);
   150→};
   151→
   152→export default apiClient;
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→// API Configuration
     2→const isDevelopment =
     3→    import.meta.env.MODE === 'development';
     4→const isProduction =
     5→    import.meta.env.MODE === 'production';
     6→
     7→// API Base URLs
     8→export const API_CONFIG = {
     9→    // Backend API URL
    10→    BASE_URL: isDevelopment ?
    11→        'http://localhost:3001' : import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL || 'http://localhost:3001',
    12→
    13→    // API Endpoints
    14→    ENDPOINTS: {
    15→        AUTH: '/api/auth',
    16→        ADMIN: '/api/admin',
    17→        SCHOLARSHIP: '/api/scholarship',
    18→        CONSULT: '/api/consult',
    19→        HEALTH: '/health'
    20→    }
    21→};
    22→
    23→// App URLs
    24→export const APP_CONFIG = {
    25→    // Frontend URL
    26→    FRONTEND_URL: isDevelopment ?
    27→        'http://localhost:5173' : 'https://do-an-tuyen-sinh.vercel.app',
    28→
    29→    // Official website
    30→    OFFICIAL_WEBSITE: 'https://hutech.edu.vn',
    31→
    32→    // Support email
    33→    SUPPORT_EMAIL: 'tuyensinh@hutech.edu.vn'
    34→};
    35→
    36→// Helper function to build API URL
    37→export const buildApiUrl = (endpoint) => {
    38→    return `${API_CONFIG.BASE_URL}${endpoint}`;
    39→};
    40→
    41→// Common API URLs
    42→export const API_URLS = {
    43→    // Auth endpoints

    62→    // Consultation endpoints

    65→
```

```text
    10→import {
    11→    sendWelcomeEmail,
    12→    sendPasswordResetEmail,
    13→    sendApplicationSubmittedEmail,
    14→    sendApplicationStatusUpdateEmail,
    15→    sendConsultationRequestEmail,
    16→    sendScholarshipApplicationEmail,
    17→    sendProfileUpdateEmail,
    18→    testEmailConnection
    19→} from './services/emailService.js';
    20→import { validateEmailConfig } from './config/emailConfig.js';
    21→import jwt from 'jsonwebtoken';
    22→import { authenticateToken, authenticateAdminFAQ } from './middleware/auth.js';
    23→import deviceService from './services/deviceService.js';
    24→
    25→const app = express();
    26→const PORT = 3001;
    27→
    28→// Trust proxy để lấy IP address chính xác
    29→app.set('trust proxy', true);
    30→
    31→// Middleware để log activity
    32→const logActivity = (action, description = '') => {
    33→    return (req, res, next) => {
    34→        const originalSend = res.send;
    35→        res.send = function(data) {
    36→            // Log activity sau khi response thành công
    37→            if (res.statusCode >= 200 && res.statusCode < 300) {
    38→                const userAgent = req.get('User-Agent');
    39→                const deviceInfo = deviceService.getDeviceInfo(userAgent);
    40→
    41→                deviceService.logActivity({
    42→                    userId: req.user ? req.user.id : null,
    43→                    action,
    44→                    description,
    45→                    ipAddress: req.ip,
    46→                    userAgent,
    47→                    deviceInfo,
    48→                    status: 'success'
    49→                });
    50→            }
    51→            originalSend.call(this, data);
    52→        };
    53→        next();
    54→    };
    55→};
    56→
    57→// CORS configuration
    58→app.use(cors({
    59→    origin: [
    60→        'http://localhost:5173', // Development
    61→        'https://do-an-tuyen-sinh.vercel.app' // Production
    62→    ],
    63→    credentials: true
    64→}));
    65→
    66→// Body parsing middleware
    67→app.use(express.json({ limit: '10mb' }));
    68→app.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: true, limit: '10mb' }));
    69→
    70→// Serve static files (avatars, attachments)
    71→app.use('/uploads', express.static('uploads'));
    72→
    73→// Health check endpoint
    74→app.get('/health', (req, res) => {
    75→    res.json({
    76→        success: true,
    77→        message: 'HUTECH Simple API Server is running',
    78→        timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
    79→    });
    80→});
```
