<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./\DoAnTuyenSinh\src\utils\environment.js`

```text
     1→// Environment detection utilities
     2→
     3→/**
     4→ * Check if the app is running on Vercel deployment
     5→ * @returns {boolean} True if running on Vercel
     6→ */
     7→export const isVercelDeployment = () => {
     8→    if (typeof window === 'undefined') return false;
     9→
    10→    const hostname = window.location.hostname;
    11→    const protocol = window.location.protocol;
    12→
    13→    // Check for Vercel domains
    14→    const isVercelDomain = hostname.includes('vercel.app') || hostname.includes('vercel.com');
    15→
    16→    // Check for HTTPS production (excluding localhost HTTPS)
    17→    const isProductionHTTPS = protocol === 'https:' && !hostname.includes('localhost');
    18→
    19→    const isVercel = isVercelDomain || isProductionHTTPS;
    20→
    21→    // Debug logging in development
    22→    if (hostname.includes('localhost')) {
    23→        console.log('Environment Detection:', {
    24→            hostname,
    25→            protocol,
    26→            isVercelDomain,
    27→            isProductionHTTPS,
    28→            isVercel,
    29→            isDevelopment: true
    30→        });
    31→    }
    32→
    33→    return isVercel;
    34→};
    35→
    36→/**
    37→ * Check if the app is running in development mode
    38→ * @returns {boolean} True if in development
    39→ */
    40→export const isDevelopment = () => {
    41→    if (typeof window === 'undefined') return false;
    42→
    43→    const hostname = window.location.hostname;
    44→    return hostname.includes('localhost') || hostname.includes('127.0.0.1');
    45→};
    46→
    47→/**
    48→ * Check if the app is running in production mode
    49→ * @returns {boolean} True if in production
    50→ */
    51→export const isProduction = () => {
    52→    return !isDevelopment();
    53→};
    54→
    55→/**
    56→ * Get current environment name
    57→ * @returns {string} 'development', 'vercel', or 'production'
    58→ */
    59→export const getEnvironment = () => {
    60→    if (isDevelopment()) return 'development';
    61→    if (isVercelDeployment()) return 'vercel';
    62→    return 'production';
    63→};
    64→
    65→/**
    66→ * Check if demo mode should be available
    67→ * Demo mode should be available on Vercel deployment and localhost for testing
    68→ * @returns {boolean} True if demo mode should be shown
    69→ */
    70→export const shouldShowDemoMode = () => {
    71→    return isVercelDeployment() || isDevelopment();
    72→};
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 16:34:09Z)**_



Here's a concise summary of the **DoAnTuyenSinh** (HUTECH Admission System) project:

---

## 📁 Project Structure

**Frontend** (Vite + React 18) at `src/`
**Backend** (Express.js) at `backend/`
Deployed on **Vercel** (frontend) + Node.js server (backend on port 3001)

---

## 📄 Pages

### `src/pages/` (Public)
| Page | Route |
|---|---|
| `TrangChu.jsx` | `/` — Hero banner, video modal, feature cards |
| `ThongTinTuyenSinh.jsx` | `/thong-tin-tuyen-sinh` |
| `DangKyXetTuyen.jsx` | `/dang-ky-xet-tuyen` — Multi-step form with auto-save, multi-major selection |
| `TraCuuKetQua.jsx` | `/tra-cuu-ket-qua` |
| `LienHe.jsx` | `/lien-he` |
| `CauHoiThuongGap.jsx` | `/cau-hoi-thuong-gap` |
| `DangKyHocBong.jsx` | `/dang-ky-hoc-bong` |
| `DangKyTuVan.jsx` | `/dang-ky-tu-van` |
| `HoSoNguoiDung.jsx` | `/ho-so-nguoi-dung` |

### `src/accounts/` (Auth)
| Page | Route |
|---|---|
| `DangNhap.jsx` | `/accounts/dang-nhap` |
| `DangKyTaiKhoan.jsx` | `/accounts/dang-ky` |
| `DangKyTaiKhoanAdmin.jsx` | `/accounts/dang-ky-admin` |
| `QuenMatKhau.jsx` | `/accounts/quen-mat-khau` |

### `src/admin/pages/` (Protected Admin)
| Page | Route |
|---|---|
| `TongQuan.jsx` | `/admin/tong-quan` — Dashboard with stats, recent apps, top majors |
| `QuanLyHoSo.jsx` | `/admin/quan-ly-ho-so` — Search, filter, status update, pagination |
| `QuanLyFAQ.jsx` | `/admin/quan-ly-faq` — CRUD for FAQs |
| `BaoCao.jsx` | `/admin/bao-cao` |
| `CaiDat.jsx` | `/admin/cai-dat` |
| `HoSoQuanLi.jsx` | `/admin/ho-so-quan-ly` |

---

## 🔌 API Integration

- **Axios-based** `apiClient` (`src/utils/apiClient.js`) targeting `http://localhost:3001/api`
- **Request interceptor** adds `Bearer` JWT token from `localStorage`
- **Response interceptor** handles 401 redirect to login
- **Auth API**: login, register, register-admin, profile, change-password, refresh-token, FAQs
- **Admin API**: dashboard stats, applications CRUD, contact management, FAQs CRUD, majors, settings, notifications
- All routes defined in `App.jsx` with `AdminProtectedRoute` guards and lazy loading

---

## 🧰 Utilities (`src/utils/`)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `apiClient.js` | Axios instance with interceptors + named API objects |
| `environment.js` | Vercel/local detection, demo mode toggles |
| `animations.js` | Framer Motion variants |
| `avatarUtils.js` | Avatar generation utilities |

---

## 📦 Key Dependencies

| Category | Packages |
|---|---|
| **UI** | `@base-ui/react`, `tw-animate-css`, `tailwind-merge`, `clsx`, `cva` |
| **Routing** | `react-router-dom` v6 |
| **Animation** | `framer-motion`, `@react-spring/web` |
| **Charts** | `recharts` |
| **Icons** | `lucide-react`, `react-icons` |
| **Notifications** | `sonner` (toast) |
| **HTTP** | `axios` |
| **Forms** | `cmdk` (command palette) |
| **Backend** | `express`, `mysql2`, `bcryptjs`, `jsonwebtoken`, `nodemailer`, `multer` |

---

## ⚡ Advanced Features Currently Implemented

| Feature | Status |
|---|---|
| **PWA** | ✅ `manifest.json` defined (standalone display, icons, theme color), but **no service worker** — not fully functional |
| **Dark/Light Mode** | ✅ `DarkModeContext` |
| **SEO** | ✅ `SEO.jsx`, `StructuredData.jsx`, `react-helmet-async` |
| **Email Notifications** | ✅ Nodemailer with templates for: welcome, password reset, application submitted, status update, consultation, scholarship, profile update |
| **Demo Mode** | ✅ Falls back to static demo data when API unreachable |
| **Auto-save** | ✅ `useAutoSave` hook (registration form) |
| **Activity Logging** | ✅ `deviceService.js` logs user actions with device/IP |
| **Page Transitions** | ✅ `PageTransition` with Framer Motion |
| **Lazy Loading** | ✅ All pages are `React.lazy()` with suspense |
| **Error Boundary** | ✅ `ErrorBoundary.jsx` |

---

## ❌ NOT Implemented

| Feature | Notes |
|---|---|
| **Real-time (WebSocket/SSE)** | Not found |
| **Push Notifications** | Not found |
| **Payment (Stripe/VNPay/MoMo)** | Not found |
| **Service Worker** | PWA manifest exists but no SW registered |
| **Full PWA Installability** | Incomplete (no SW) |

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 16:34:27Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants advanced features to add to the project. Based on the audit, here's what I know:

Current state:
- React 18 + Vite frontend with 9 public pages + 9 admin pages
- Express.js backend with MySQL
- Auth, forms, dashboard, admin management
- Demo mode, auto-save, SEO, dark mode
- Partial PWA (manifest only, no service worker)
- Email notifications via nodemailer
- Activity logging

NOT implemented:
1. Real-time (WebSocket/SSE)
2. Push Notifications
3. Payment (Stripe/VNPay/MoMo)


4. Full PWA with service worker
5. TypeScript migration
6. Advanced search with filters
7. Analytics tracking
8. Multi-language/i18n
9. File upload with preview
10. Password strength indicator
11. Advanced form validation
12. Mobile app (React Native / Expo)

Now I'm thinking through which features would be realistic to add. PWA with a service worker makes the most sense—it would improve offline support, caching, and installability without major rework. Push notifications would be the next logical step to boost engagement, though that requires backend setup. Real-time updates via WebSockets or SSE could work for notifications, but it's more involved. I should avoid payment processing, full TypeScript migration, and mobile app development since those are either too complex or outside the scope of a Vercel-deployed web app.

I also need to check what's actually possible given the hosting constraints—Vercel's free tier has limitations on background jobs and server-side operations, so features like scheduled notifications or SSR wouldn't fit well. Let me focus on presenting the realistic options first and letting the user choose what makes sense for their situation.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 16:34:48Z)**_

Dựa trên audit, đây là các features nâng cao có thể thêm, chia theo mức độ ưu tiên thực tế:

---

## Các features nâng cao có thể thêm

### 🔴 Cao ưu tiên (đáng giá nhất)

| # | Feature | Mô tả | Effort |
|---|---------|--------|--------|
| **1** | **PWA hoàn chỉnh** | Service Worker + offline support + install prompt + push notifications | Medium |
| **2** | **Real-time notifications** | WebSocket/SSE cho admin thấy application mới ngay lập tức, thí sinh thấy kết quả cập nhật | Medium |
| **3** | **i18n (Đa ngôn ngữ)** | Tiếng Việt + Tiếng Anh, lazy-load locale files | Medium |

### 🟡 Trung ưu tiên (cải thiện UX)

| # | Feature | Mô tả | Effort |
|---|---------|--------|--------|
| **4** | **Advanced search** | Full-text search với debounce, fuzzy matching, filter chips | Low-Medium |
| **5** | **File upload với preview** | Ảnh CMND, bằng cấp — drag-drop, preview, compress trước upload | Medium |
| **6** | **Form validation nâng cao** | Real-time validation, password strength meter, smart error messages | Low |
| **7** | **Analytics dashboard** | Google Analytics / Plausible integration, track user funnel | Low |

### 🟢 Low ưu tiên (nice-to-have)

| # | Feature | Mô tả | Effort |
|---|---------|--------|--------|
| **8** | **Payment gateway** | VNPay / MoMo / Stripe cho phí xét tuyển | High (backend + legal) |
| **9** | **TypeScript migration** | Migrate dần từ JS → TS | High (long-term) |
| **10** | **Mobile app** | React Native / Expo wrapper | High |
| **11** | **Email marketing** | Newsletter, reminder emails, drip campaigns | Medium (backend) |

---

## Đề xuất của tôi

### Option A: PWA hoàn chỉnh (Recommended)
```
- Service Worker caching strategies (cache-first cho assets, network-first cho API)
- Offline page khi mất kết nối
- Install prompt ("Cài đặt ứng dụng")
- Push notifications (FCM) khi có cập nhật hồ sơ
- Background sync cho form submissions
```
**Lý do:** Không cần backend thay đổi nhiều, tăng UX đáng kể, tăng engagement.

### Option B: Real-time + Notifications
```
- SSE (Server-Sent Events) cho real-time updates
- Admin dashboard: notification bell + live counter
- Email notification khi trạng thái hồ sơ thay đổi
```
**Lý do:** Phù hợp hệ thống tuyển sinh — thí sinh cần biết kết quả nhanh.

### Option C: i18n (Đa ngôn ngữ)
```
- vi.json / en.json locale files
- react-i18next integration
- Language switcher trong header
- SEO meta tags per locale
```
**Lý do:** HUTECH có thể có thí sinh nước ngoài, tuyển sinh quốc tế.

---

## Bạn muốn đi theo hướng nào?

Tôi recommend **Option A (PWA hoàn chỉnh)** vì:
1. Effort vừa phải
2. Không cần backend change
3. Impact lớn cho UX (install app, offline, push)
4. Đã có manifest.json sẵn — chỉ cần thêm Service Worker
5. Vercel hỗ trợ tốt cho PWAs

Hoặc bạn muốn **kết hợp nhiều options**? Cho tôi biết, tôi sẽ lên plan cụ thể.

---

    31→### Active
    32→
    33→- [ ] **UI-01**: Thiết kế lại toàn bộ UI/UX theo shadcn/ui + design system hiện đại
    34→- [ ] **UI-02**: Tối ưu animation (giảm motion thừa, cải thiện performance)

    38→- [ ] **UI-06**: Cải thiện responsive design toàn trang

    40→
    41→### Out of Scope
    42→
    43→- Thay đổi logic nghiệp vụ (business logic giữ nguyên) — UI/UX only
    44→- Thêm tính năng mới ngoài spec hiện tại — chỉ refactor UI
    45→- Backend/API refactoring — chỉ frontend
    46→- Thêm unit tests — có thể bổ sung sau UI xong
    47→- Chuyển đổi TypeScript — JavaScript đủ cho scope hiện tại
    48→
    49→## Context
    50→
    51→**Tech Stack hiện tại:**
    52→- React 18 + Vite
    53→- Tailwind CSS 3.x (custom config)
    54→- Framer Motion (animations)
    55→- React Router v6
    56→- Recharts (charts)
    57→- react-icons (icons)
    58→- react-hot-toast (notifications)
    59→
    60→**Design System hiện tại:**
    61→- 16+ UI components tự xây (Button, Card, Input, Modal, etc.)
    62→- 12 color variants cho primary, secondary, accent
    63→- Glassmorphism pattern với backdrop-blur
    64→- Floating particles decoration
    65→- Framer Motion entrance/hover animations
    66→
    67→**Vấn đề UI/UX đã xác định:**
    68→1. `Math.random()` trong JSX render (Header, Footer, Auth pages) → DOM re-render không cần thiết
    69→2. Animation loops vô hạn → tốn CPU/GPU
    70→3. Page entrance delays > 1s → UX chậm
    71→4. Admin pages không có dark mode → không nhất quán
    72→5. Custom Select/Dropdown không có keyboard navigation → accessibility kém
    73→6. Console.log debug còn nhiều → production không clean
    74→7. Component duplication (FormField ≈ Input)
    75→8. Components quá lớn (Header 800+ lines)
    76→9. Animation variants duplicate ở nhiều page
    77→10. No skeleton loading cho data fetching
    78→11. Tailwind config quá dài (400+ lines) với CSS variables không sử dụng
    79→12. Table component chưa hoàn thiện (dùng cards thay table)
    80→
    81→**Project Structure:**
    82→- `src/accounts/` — Auth pages (DangNhap, DangKyTaiKhoan, QuenMatKhau)
    83→- `src/admin/` — Admin pages (6 pages + AdminLayout)
    84→- `src/components/ui/` — 16 atomic UI components
    85→- `src/pages/` — 9 public pages
    86→- `src/contexts/` — DarkModeContext, ThemeContext (duplicate)
    87→- `src/utils/` — animations, apiClient, avatarUtils
    88→
    89→## Constraints
    90→
    91→- **Tech Stack**: React + Vite + Tailwind — không đổi framework
    92→- **Backward Compatibility**: Giữ nguyên tất cả routes, business logic, API contracts
    93→- **Bundle Size**: Không tăng đáng kể bundle size (hiện tại chưa optimize)
    94→- **Browser Support**: Hỗ trợ Chrome/Firefox/Edge/Safari phiên bản mới nhất
    95→- **Performance**: Lighthouse score >= 80 cho cả desktop và mobile
    96→
    97→## Key Decisions
    98→
    99→| Decision | Rationale | Outcome |
   100→|----------|-----------|---------|
   101→| Dùng shadcn/ui | Copy-paste vào project, sở hữu 100% code, integrate hoàn toàn với Tailwind hiện có | — Pending |
   102→| Giữ Framer Motion | Animation library đã dùng, chỉ cần tối ưu cách dùng | — Pending |
   103→| Thay react-hot-toast bằng Sonner (shadcn) | Nhất quán với shadcn pattern, API tương tự | — Pending |
   104→| Design tokens qua CSS Variables | Pattern chuẩn, dùng cho cả light/dark mode | — Pending |
   105→| V0/Vercel cho rapid prototyping | AI-assisted UI generation, review & integrate thủ công | — Pending |
   106→| Xóa ThemeContext, giữ DarkModeContext | Loại bỏ duplicate context | — Pending |

 GSD ► MILESTONE SUMMARY

**Milestone: M2 — Advanced Features**

**Goal:** Thêm 3 tính năng nâng cao giúp trang tuyển sinh chuyên nghiệp hơn: PWA offline-capable, real-time notifications, và đa ngôn ngữ.

**Target features:**
- **Option A — PWA hoàn chỉnh:** Service Worker + offline support + install prompt + push notifications
- **Option B — Real-time notifications:** SSE cho admin thấy hồ sơ mới, thí sinh thấy cập nhật kết quả
- **Option C — i18n (Đa ngôn ngữ):** Tiếng Việt + Tiếng Anh, lazy-load locale files

**Key context:**
- Tech stack: React 18 + Vite + Tailwind + react-router-dom v6
- Backend: Express.js + MySQL (cần thêm SSE endpoint)
- Deploy: Vercel (frontend) + Node server (backend)
- Không có test suite hiện tại
- Không thay đổi business logic hiện tại

**Dependency order:** PWA (frontend-only, dễ) → i18n (frontend-only, dễ) → Real-time (cần backend change, phức tạp hơn)

```md
# DoAnTuyenSinh - Website Tuyển Sinh HUTECH

## What This Is

Website tuyển sinh trực tuyến của Trường Đại học HUTECH — cho phép thí sinh đăng ký xét tuyển, tra cứu kết quả, đăng ký tư vấn, và quản lý hồ sơ tuyển sinh. Hệ thống bao gồm trang công khai (public) và trang quản trị (admin) riêng biệt.

## Core Value

Thí sinh có thể hoàn tất toàn bộ quy trình tuyển sinh từ đăng ký đến tra cứu kết quả trên nền tảng web hiện đại, chuyên nghiệp, nhanh chóng.

## Current Milestone: M2 — Advanced Features

**Goal:** Thêm 3 tính năng nâng cao: PWA hoàn chỉnh, real-time notifications, và đa ngôn ngữ (i18n).

**Target features:**
- Option A — PWA: Service Worker + offline support + install prompt + push notifications
- Option B — Real-time: SSE cho admin thấy hồ sơ mới, thí sinh thấy cập nhật kết quả
- Option C — i18n: Tiếng Việt + Tiếng Anh, lazy-load locale files

## Requirements

### Validated (M1)

- ✓ Đăng ký / Đăng nhập tài khoản — v1
- ✓ Dark mode toàn trang — v1
- ✓ Header sticky navigation — v1
- ✓ Footer với thông tin liên hệ — v1
- ✓ Trang chủ với hero + stats — v1
- ✓ Trang đăng ký xét tuyển — v1
- ✓ Trang tra cứu kết quả — v1
- ✓ Trang thông tin tuyển sinh — v1
- ✓ Trang liên hệ — v1
- ✓ Trang FAQ — v1
- ✓ Trang hồ sơ người dùng — v1
- ✓ Trang đăng ký tư vấn — v1
- ✓ Dashboard admin quản lý hồ sơ — v1
- ✓ Báo cáo thống kê admin — v1
- ✓ Cài đặt & FAQ quản lý admin — v1
- ✓ UI/UX Redesign hoàn chỉnh (shadcn/ui, dark mode, responsive) — M1
- ✓ Code splitting + bundle optimization — M1
- ✓ Toast migration (react-hot-toast → sonner) — M1

### Active (M2)

- [ ] **PWA-01**: Service Worker với caching strategies
- [ ] **PWA-02**: Offline page khi mất kết nối
- [ ] **PWA-03**: Install prompt để cài đặt ứng dụng
- [ ] **PWA-04**: Push notifications (FCM) khi có cập nhật hồ sơ
- [ ] **RT-01**: SSE endpoint cho real-time updates
- [ ] **RT-02**: Admin notification bell với live counter
- [ ] **RT-03**: Thí sinh nhận thông báo khi kết quả được cập nhật
- [ ] **I18N-01**: i18n infrastructure (react-i18next)
- [ ] **I18N-02**: Language switcher trong header
- [ ] **I18N-03**: Locale files tiếng Việt + tiếng Anh

### Out of Scope

- Thay đổi logic nghiệp vụ (business logic giữ nguyên) — chỉ thêm features
- Backend/API refactoring — chỉ thêm SSE endpoint
- TypeScript migration — JavaScript đủ cho scope hiện tại
- Payment gateway — không trong scope M2

## Context

**Tech Stack hiện tại (sau M1):**
- React 18 + Vite 7
- Tailwind CSS 3.x (custom config, CSS variables)
- Framer Motion (animations)
- React Router v6.30
- Recharts (charts)
- react-icons (icons)
- sonner (toast notifications)
- axios (HTTP client)
- @base-ui/react (shadcn-style components)

**Backend:**
- Express.js + MySQL
- JWT authentication
- Nodemailer email templates

**Deployment:**
- Vercel (frontend, automatic deploy)
- Node.js server (backend on port 3001)

**PWA state hiện tại:**
- manifest.json đã có (standalone display, icons, theme color)
- Không có Service Worker

**i18n state hiện tại:**
- Hoàn toàn chưa có — hardcoded tiếng Việt

## Constraints

- **Tech Stack**: React + Vite + Tailwind — không đổi framework
- **Backward Compatibility**: Giữ nguyên tất cả routes, business logic, API contracts
- **Bundle Size**: Không tăng quá 50KB sau khi thêm PWA + i18n
- **Browser Support**: Hỗ trợ Chrome/Firefox/Edge/Safari phiên bản mới nhất
- **PWA**: Service Worker phải work offline (cache-first cho assets, network-first cho API)
- **Real-time**: Dùng SSE (Server-Sent Events) thay vì WebSocket — đơn giản hơn cho unidirectional updates

## Key Decisions

| Decision | Rationale | Outcome |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| PWA với Workbox | Workbox là thư viện chuẩn, hỗ trợ nhiều caching strategies, được Google maintain | — Pending |
| SSE thay vì WebSocket | Unidirectional (server → client), đơn giản hơn, work được với Vercel edge functions | — Pending |
| react-i18next | Thư viện i18n phổ biến nhất cho React, lazy-load locale files | — Pending |
| FCM cho push notifications | Firebase Cloud Messaging là giải pháp push notification phổ biến nhất | — Pending |
| react-router-dom v6 | Ổn định, tương thích với React 18, API đơn giản | — Pending |

## Evolution

This document evolves at phase transitions and milestone boundaries.

**After each phase transition** (via `/gsd:transition`):
1. Requirements invalidated? → Move to Out of Scope with reason
2. Requirements validated? → Move to Validated with phase reference
3. New requirements emerged? → Add to Active
4. Decisions to log? → Add to Key Decisions
5. "What This Is" still accurate? → Update if drifted

**After each milestone** (via `/gsd:complete-milestone`):
1. Full review of all sections
2. Core Value check — still the right priority?
3. Audit Out of Scope — reasons still valid?
4. Update Context with current state

---
*Last updated: 2026-03-30 after M2 milestone initialization*

```text


Written to `.planning/research/STACK.md`. Here's a summary of the findings:

---

## Stack Additions for M2

### PWA — 2 packages

| Library | Version | Why |
|---------|---------|-----|
| **vite-plugin-pwa** | 1.2.0 | Official Vite plugin — wraps Workbox, injects SW at build time, zero-config defaults. No manual SW files needed. |
| **workbox-window** | 7.4.0 | Bundled by vite-plugin-pwa; gives `updateSW()`, install prompt, offline-ready events on the client. |

**Why not manual Workbox?** vite-plugin-pwa generates and registers the SW automatically. Manual Workbox adds significant complexity for no benefit — all PWA-01 through PWA-04 can be configured declaratively in `vite.config.js`.

---

### Real-time — 1 package

| Library | Version | Why |
|---------|---------|-----|
| **express-sse** | 1.0.0 | Handles `Content-Type: text/event-stream`, keep-alive, and heartbeat cleanup. Project constraint mandates SSE over WebSocket. |

**No frontend package needed** — the browser's native `EventSource` API handles the client side. SSE streams over axios REST calls, no conflict.

---

### i18n — 2 packages

| Library | Version | Why |
|---------|---------|-----|
| **i18next** | 26.0.2 | Core engine (translation loading, interpolation, namespaces) |
| **react-i18next** | 17.0.1 | React bindings — `useTranslation` hook, Suspense support, lazy namespace loading built-in |

**Why not lingui?** Requires a macro/compile step. For a 2-language VI+EN app, react-i18next is simpler and sufficient.

---

### Push Notifications — 1 package

| Library | Version | Why |
|---------|---------|-----|
| **firebase** | 12.11.0 | Modular SDK — import only `getMessaging` + `getToken` from `firebase/messaging`. Tree-shakeable, ~15-20KB. FCM handles token management, foreground/background push, payloads. |

---

### Bundle impact

| Addition | Est. gzipped |
|----------|-------------|
| vite-plugin-pwa + workbox-window | ~15-25KB |
| firebase (messaging only, modular) | ~15-20KB |
| i18next + react-i18next | ~10-15KB |
| **Total** | **~40-60KB** |

⚠️ Closely near the PROJECT.md 50KB budget constraint — use modular imports and strict lazy loading on locale files.

Features research: PWA + real-time + i18n

```text
## RESEARCH COMPLETE

**Project:** DoAnTuyenSinh (HUTECH Admissions Platform)
**Mode:** Ecosystem
**Confidence:** MEDIUM

### Key Findings

- **PWA**: manifest.json and icons already exist. The hard part is Service Worker (Workbox/vite-plugin-pwa), offline page, and install prompt -- all medium-to-low complexity. Push notifications (FCM) should be deferred to P3 due to Firebase setup overhead and low permission acceptance rates.
- **SSE**: Backend SSE endpoint (RT-01) is the critical path -- it enables both RT-02 and RT-03. **Key warning**: Vercel serverless functions have a 30s timeout, requiring reconnection logic or a separate Node.js server for SSE. This is an architectural decision that affects the roadmap.
- **i18n**: Infrastructure is straightforward with react-i18next, but the real effort is the content migration -- wrapping ~24 page components with `t()` keys. This should be done page-by-page incrementally.

### Files Created

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `.planning/research/FEATURES.md` | Complete feature landscape with table stakes, differentiators, anti-features, dependencies, MVP definition, prioritization matrix |

### Key Recommendations

1. **P1 for M2**: PWA-01/02/03 (SW + offline + install prompt), I18N-01/02 (infrastructure + switcher), RT-01 (SSE backend endpoint)
2. **P2**: RT-02/03 (frontend notification bell + student updates) -- require RT-01 first
3. **P3**: PWA-04 (push notifications) and full I18N-03 (complete locale coverage) -- defer to after launch

### Open Questions

- **Vercel + SSE architecture**: Should SSE endpoint run on the existing Express server (port 3001, not Vercel) or handle reconnection on Vercel? This affects deployment architecture and should be resolved before RT-01 implementation.
- **Firebase account**: Does the team have a Firebase project set up for FCM? Without this, PWA-04 cannot proceed.
- **i18n scope for v1**: Should ALL pages be translated in M2, or only high-traffic pages (TrangChu, DangKyXetTuyen, TraCuuKetQua)? Full coverage is high effort.

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="Agent"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Agent**</summary>
Architecture research: PWA + real-time + i18n

```text
## RESEARCH COMPLETE

**Project:** DoAnTuyenSinh (HUTECH Admissions Website)
**Mode:** Architecture Integration Research
**Confidence:** MEDIUM

### Key Findings

- **PWA via vite-plugin-pwa:** Generates Workbox SW at build time, auto-injects into `index.html`. Configure existing `manifest.json` by adding `"id": "sw-base"`. API routes use `NetworkFirst` (not `CacheFirst`) to avoid stale admissions data.
- **SSE in Express:** Requires a singleton `sseService.js` holding connected client `res` objects. Heartbeat every 30s is mandatory to prevent proxy timeouts. SSE **cannot** run on Vercel serverless -- must connect to backend on port 3001 directly.
- **i18n lazy-loading:** Locale files go in `public/locales/{lang}/{ns}.json`, fetched at runtime by `i18next-http-backend`. Only the i18next library (~23KB gzipped) hits the JS bundle. App must be wrapped with `<Suspense>` because first load fetches locale JSON async.
- **New providers stack:** `App.jsx` needs nested wrapping: `HelmetProvider > DarkModeProvider > UserContextProvider > NotificationProvider > BrowserRouter`. The `NotificationContext` reads `userId` from `UserContext`, manages SSE connection lifecycle, and pipes events into sonner.
- **Build dependency order:** i18n infrastructure first (no dependencies), then PWA (configures vite), then SSE (needs existing auth). Or PWA first to avoid SW conflicts with new API routes.

### Files Created

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `.planning/research/ARCHITECTURE.md` | Full architecture with system overview, patterns, data flows, integration points, anti-patterns |

### Confidence Assessment

| Area | Level | Reason |
|------|-------|--------|
| Stack | MEDIUM | Existing code confirmed via file reads; vite-plugin-pwa/vite 7 compatibility uncertain (pre-release), verified via ecosystem search |
| Features | HIGH | All three features (PWA/SSE/i18n) are well-established patterns with ample documentation |
| Architecture | MEDIUM | Integration patterns confirmed from docs; SSE/Vercel limitation verified (Vercel serverless cannot host SSE) |
| Pitfalls | MEDIUM | Anti-patterns documented from community experience; SSE memory scaling at 500+ is theoretical for this project's size |

### Roadmap Implications

1. **Phase 1 (i18n):** Create `src/i18n/`, `public/locales/`, `LanguageSwitcher`. No backend changes. Lowest risk, validates the provider wrapping pattern.
2. **Phase 2 (PWA):** Configure `vite-plugin-pwa` in `vite.config.js`, add `public/locales/` to build output, create `OfflineBoundary`, `InstallPrompt`. Modifies the build pipeline.
3. **Phase 3 (SSE):** Add `sseService.js`, SSE route in `backend/index.js`, `NotificationContext`, `useSSE` hook, `NotificationBell`. Backend-only + new frontend context. Requires careful reconnection logic.

### Open Questions

- **Vite 7 compatibility:** vite-plugin-pWA version compatibility with Vite 7 (which appears to be pre-release as of March 2026). Should verify exact package version before build.
- **Vercel SW serving:** Need to confirm Vercel serves `sw.js` from the static `dist/` directory correctly (may need `vercel.json` adjustment).
- **FCM implementation scope:** Push notifications via FCM require a Firebase project. If out of scope, fall back to in-app only notifications via SSE.
</parameter></li>
agentId: aefb4b34adcdcd043 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="Agent"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Agent**</summary>
Pitfalls research: PWA + real-time + i18n

```text
## RESEARCH COMPLETE

**Project:** DoAnTuyenSinh (HUTECH Admissions Website) -- M2 Advanced Features
**Mode:** Pitfalls / Domain Research
**Confidence:** MEDIUM-HIGH (based on web search + community sources, not verified against live implementations)

### Key Findings

- **PWA SW timing** is the first pitfall to prevent -- assets can load before the SW activates, causing blank screens. Must add a render-guard or use `waitOnLocalhost` strategy.
- **SSE connection leaks** are the most dangerous silent killer -- `EventSource` never sends a "goodbye" signal on tab close. Every page navigation in React Router creates a new connection. Prevention: global SSE context at app root + `req.on('close')` server-side + `beforeunload` handler.
- **Vercel edge caching conflicts with both PWA and SSE** -- this project deploys frontend to Vercel but the backend runs on Express port 3001. The SSE endpoint MUST stay on Express (serverless functions can't hold long SSE connections). API responses need `Cache-Control: no-store` headers to prevent edge caching interference.
- **FCM push notifications and Workbox caching SW will conflict** if two separate SW files are registered. Must consolidate into one unified SW that handles both Workbox caching and FCM messaging.
- **i18n "looks done" but isn't** -- missing translation keys silently fall back to the source language in dev. Must fail the build if keys are missing, not just warn.

### Files Created

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `.planning/research/PITFALLS.md` | Comprehensive pitfalls catalog across PWA, SSE, and i18n domains |

### Confidence Assessment

| Area | Level | Reason |
|------|-------|--------|
| PWA Pitfalls | MEDIUM | Based on vite-plugin-pwa docs + community discussions, not tested on this exact Vite 7 + Vercel setup |
| SSE Pitfalls | MEDIUM-HIGH | SSE API is stable, Express cleanup patterns are well-documented |
| i18n Pitfalls | MEDIUM | react-i18next patterns are standard, but RTL + SEO integration depends on project-specific implementation |
| Vercel-specific issues | MEDIUM | Vercel caching behavior is documented, but edge function limits for SSE are not tested |

### Roadmap Implications

**Phase ordering should reflect these dependencies:**

1. **Phase 1 (PWA infrastructure)**: Set up vite-plugin-pwa with correct caching strategies AND handle Vercel cache headers in `vercel.json`. If you skip the Vercel config, every other phase's SSE and API calls will be affected. Also unify the FCM SW with the Workbox SW here, not later.

2. **Phase 2 (SSE endpoint)**: Build on Express port 3001 (NOT Vercel). Implement global SSE context at app root (not per-component) to prevent connection multiplication on React Router navigation. Add exponential backoff from day one.

3. **Phase 3 (i18n infrastructure)**: Set up react-i18next with translation key extraction in CI/CD. Add SEO meta tags (via react-helmet-async) alongside the locale scaffold, not as a separate phase. RTL direction handling is a watch item for future, not M2 priority.

### Open Questions

- Whether to use `vite-plugin-pwa`'s `injectManifest` (for custom SW) or `generateSW` (Workbox-generated) strategy -- affects how FCM is integrated. `generateSW` is simpler but less flexible for custom FCM logic.
- Whether FCM push notifications should be part of M2 scope or deferred (requires Firebase project setup + VAPID key management + backend FCM server integration).
- Exact SSE event format (JSON structure) for admission result updates -- needs alignment with the existing Express backend data models.

Research what stack additions/changes are needed for PWA, Real-time notifications, and i18n in a React 18 + Vite project.

Context:
- Existing: React 18, Vite 7, Tailwind CSS 3.x, Framer Motion, react-router-dom v6, axios, @base-ui/react, sonner
- Backend: Express.js + MySQL on port 3001
- Deployment: Vercel (frontend) + Node server

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./\.planning\PROJECT.md`

```text
     1→# DoAnTuyenSinh - Website Tuyển Sinh HUTECH
     2→
     3→## What This Is
     4→
     5→Website tuyển sinh trực tuyến của Trường Đại học HUTECH — cho phép thí sinh đăng ký xét tuyển, tra cứu kết quả, đăng ký tư vấn, và quản lý hồ sơ tuyển sinh. Hệ thống bao gồm trang công khai (public) và trang quản trị (admin) riêng biệt.
     6→
     7→## Core Value
     8→
     9→Thí sinh có thể hoàn tất toàn bộ quy trình tuyển sinh từ đăng ký đến tra cứu kết quả trên nền tảng web hiện đại, chuyên nghiệp, nhanh chóng.
    10→
    11→## Current Milestone: M2 — Advanced Features
    12→
    13→**Goal:** Thêm 3 tính năng nâng cao: PWA hoàn chỉnh, real-time notifications, và đa ngôn ngữ (i18n).
    14→
    15→**Target features:**
    16→- Option A — PWA: Service Worker + offline support + install prompt + push notifications
    17→- Option B — Real-time: SSE cho admin thấy hồ sơ mới, thí sinh thấy cập nhật kết quả
    18→- Option C — i18n: Tiếng Việt + Tiếng Anh, lazy-load locale files
    19→
    20→## Requirements
    21→
    22→### Validated (M1)
    23→
    24→- ✓ Đăng ký / Đăng nhập tài khoản — v1
    25→- ✓ Dark mode toàn trang — v1
    26→- ✓ Header sticky navigation — v1
    27→- ✓ Footer với thông tin liên hệ — v1
    28→- ✓ Trang chủ với hero + stats — v1
    29→- ✓ Trang đăng ký xét tuyển — v1
    30→- ✓ Trang tra cứu kết quả — v1
    31→- ✓ Trang thông tin tuyển sinh — v1
    32→- ✓ Trang liên hệ — v1
    33→- ✓ Trang FAQ — v1
    34→- ✓ Trang hồ sơ người dùng — v1
    35→- ✓ Trang đăng ký tư vấn — v1
    36→- ✓ Dashboard admin quản lý hồ sơ — v1
    37→- ✓ Báo cáo thống kê admin — v1
    38→- ✓ Cài đặt & FAQ quản lý admin — v1
    39→- ✓ UI/UX Redesign hoàn chỉnh (shadcn/ui, dark mode, responsive) — M1
    40→- ✓ Code splitting + bundle optimization — M1
    41→- ✓ Toast migration (react-hot-toast → sonner) — M1
    42→
    43→### Active (M2)
    44→
    45→- [ ] **PWA-01**: Service Worker với caching strategies
    46→- [ ] **PWA-02**: Offline page khi mất kết nối
    47→- [ ] **PWA-03**: Install prompt để cài đặt ứng dụng
    48→- [ ] **PWA-04**: Push notifications (FCM) khi có cập nhật hồ sơ
    49→- [ ] **RT-01**: SSE endpoint cho real-time updates
    50→- [ ] **RT-02**: Admin notification bell với live counter
    51→- [ ] **RT-03**: Thí sinh nhận thông báo khi kết quả được cập nhật
    52→- [ ] **I18N-01**: i18n infrastructure (react-i18next)
    53→- [ ] **I18N-02**: Language switcher trong header
    54→- [ ] **I18N-03**: Locale files tiếng Việt + tiếng Anh
    55→
    56→### Out of Scope
    57→
    58→- Thay đổi logic nghiệp vụ (business logic giữ nguyên) — chỉ thêm features
    59→- Backend/API refactoring — chỉ thêm SSE endpoint
    60→- TypeScript migration — JavaScript đủ cho scope hiện tại
    61→- Payment gateway — không trong scope M2
    62→
    63→## Context
    64→
    65→**Tech Stack hiện tại (sau M1):**
    66→- React 18 + Vite 7
    67→- Tailwind CSS 3.x (custom config, CSS variables)
    68→- Framer Motion (animations)
    69→- React Router v6.30
    70→- Recharts (charts)
    71→- react-icons (icons)
    72→- sonner (toast notifications)
    73→- axios (HTTP client)
    74→- @base-ui/react (shadcn-style components)
    75→
    76→**Backend:**
    77→- Express.js + MySQL
    78→- JWT authentication
    79→- Nodemailer email templates
    80→
    81→**Deployment:**
    82→- Vercel (frontend, automatic deploy)
    83→- Node.js server (backend on port 3001)
    84→
    85→**PWA state hiện tại:**
    86→- manifest.json đã có (standalone display, icons, theme color)
    87→- Không có Service Worker
    88→
    89→**i18n state hiện tại:**
    90→- Hoàn toàn chưa có — hardcoded tiếng Việt
    91→
    92→## Constraints
    93→
    94→- **Tech Stack**: React + Vite + Tailwind — không đổi framework
    95→- **Backward Compatibility**: Giữ nguyên tất cả routes, business logic, API contracts
    96→- **Bundle Size**: Không tăng quá 50KB sau khi thêm PWA + i18n
    97→- **Browser Support**: Hỗ trợ Chrome/Firefox/Edge/Safari phiên bản mới nhất
    98→- **PWA**: Service Worker phải work offline (cache-first cho assets, network-first cho API)
    99→- **Real-time**: Dùng SSE (Server-Sent Events) thay vì WebSocket — đơn giản hơn cho unidirectional updates
   100→
   101→## Key Decisions
   102→
   103→| Decision | Rationale | Outcome |
   104→|----------|-----------|---------|
   105→| PWA với Workbox | Workbox là thư viện chuẩn, hỗ trợ nhiều caching strategies, được Google maintain | — Pending |
   106→| SSE thay vì WebSocket | Unidirectional (server → client), đơn giản hơn, work được với Vercel edge functions | — Pending |
   107→| react-i18next | Thư viện i18n phổ biến nhất cho React, lazy-load locale files | — Pending |
   108→| FCM cho push notifications | Firebase Cloud Messaging là giải pháp push notification phổ biến nhất | — Pending |
   109→| react-router-dom v6 | Ổn định, tương thích với React 18, API đơn giản | — Pending |
   110→
   111→## Evolution
   112→
   113→This document evolves at phase transitions and milestone boundaries.
   114→
   115→**After each phase transition** (via `/gsd:transition`):
   116→1. Requirements invalidated? → Move to Out of Scope with reason
   117→2. Requirements validated? → Move to Validated with phase reference
   118→3. New requirements emerged? → Add to Active
   119→4. Decisions to log? → Add to Key Decisions
   120→5. "What This Is" still accurate? → Update if drifted
   121→
   122→**After each milestone** (via `/gsd:complete-milestone`):
   123→1. Full review of all sections
   124→2. Core Value check — still the right priority?
   125→3. Audit Out of Scope — reasons still valid?
   126→4. Update Context with current state
   127→
   128→---
   129→*Last updated: 2026-03-30 after M2 milestone initialization*
   130→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→# Stack Research Template
     2→
     3→Template for `.planning/research/STACK.md` — recommended technologies for the project domain.
     4→
     5→<template>
     6→
     7→```markdown
     8→# Stack Research
     9→
    10→**Domain:** [domain type]
    11→**Researched:** [date]
    12→**Confidence:** [HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW]
    13→
    14→## Recommended Stack
    15→
    16→### Core Technologies
    17→
    18→| Technology | Version | Purpose | Why Recommended |
    19→|------------|---------|---------|-----------------|
    20→| [name] | [version] | [what it does] | [why experts use it for this domain] |
    21→| [name] | [version] | [what it does] | [why experts use it for this domain] |
    22→| [name] | [version] | [what it does] | [why experts use it for this domain] |
    23→
    24→### Supporting Libraries
    25→
    26→| Library | Version | Purpose | When to Use |
    27→|---------|---------|---------|-------------|
    28→| [name] | [version] | [what it does] | [specific use case] |
    29→| [name] | [version] | [what it does] | [specific use case] |
    30→| [name] | [version] | [what it does] | [specific use case] |
    31→
    32→### Development Tools
    33→
    34→| Tool | Purpose | Notes |
    35→|------|---------|-------|
    36→| [name] | [what it does] | [configuration tips] |
    37→| [name] | [what it does] | [configuration tips] |
    38→
    39→## Installation
    40→
    41→```bash
    42→# Core
    43→npm install [packages]
    44→
    45→# Supporting
    46→npm install [packages]
    47→
    48→# Dev dependencies
    49→npm install -D [packages]
    50→```
    51→
    52→## Alternatives Considered
    53→
    54→| Recommended | Alternative | When to Use Alternative |
    55→|-------------|-------------|-------------------------|
    56→| [our choice] | [other option] | [conditions where alternative is better] |
    57→| [our choice] | [other option] | [conditions where alternative is better] |
    58→
    59→## What NOT to Use
    60→
    61→| Avoid | Why | Use Instead |
    62→|-------|-----|-------------|
    63→| [technology] | [specific problem] | [recommended alternative] |
    64→| [technology] | [specific problem] | [recommended alternative] |
    65→
    66→## Stack Patterns by Variant
    67→
    68→**If [condition]:**
    69→- Use [variation]
    70→- Because [reason]
    71→
    72→**If [condition]:**
    73→- Use [variation]
    74→- Because [reason]
    75→
    76→## Version Compatibility
    77→
    78→| Package A | Compatible With | Notes |
    79→|-----------|-----------------|-------|
    80→| [package@version] | [package@version] | [compatibility notes] |
    81→
    82→## Sources
    83→
    84→- [Context7 library ID] — [topics fetched]
    85→- [Official docs URL] — [what was verified]
    86→- [Other source] — [confidence level]
    87→
    88→---
    89→*Stack research for: [domain]*
    90→*Researched: [date]*
    91→```
    92→
    93→</template>
    94→
    95→<guidelines>
    96→
    97→**Core Technologies:**
    98→- Include specific version numbers
    99→- Explain why this is the standard choice, not just what it does
   100→- Focus on technologies that affect architecture decisions
   101→
   102→**Supporting Libraries:**
   103→- Include libraries commonly needed for this domain
   104→- Note when each is needed (not all projects need all libraries)
   105→
   106→**Alternatives:**
   107→- Don't just dismiss alternatives
   108→- Explain when alternatives make sense
   109→- Helps user make informed decisions if they disagree
   110→
   111→**What NOT to Use:**
   112→- Actively warn against outdated or problematic choices
   113→- Explain the specific problem, not just "it's old"
   114→- Provide the recommended alternative
   115→
   116→**Version Compatibility:**
   117→- Note any known compatibility issues
   118→- Critical for avoiding debugging time later
   119→
   120→</guidelines>
   121→
```
</details></tool-use>

     7→## Core Value
     8→
     9→Thí sinh có thể hoàn tất toàn bộ quy trình tuyển sinh từ đăng ký đến tra cứu kết quả trên nền tảng web hiện đại, chuyên nghiệp, nhanh chóng.
    10→
    11→## Current Milestone: M2 — Advanced Features
    12→
    13→**Goal:** Thêm 3 tính năng nâng cao: PWA hoàn chỉnh, real-time notifications, và đa ngôn ngữ (i18n).
    14→
    15→**Target features:**
    16→- Option A — PWA: Service Worker + offline support + install prompt + push notifications
    17→- Option B — Real-time: SSE cho admin thấy hồ sơ mới, thí sinh thấy cập nhật kết quả
    18→- Option C — i18n: Tiếng Việt + Tiếng Anh, lazy-load locale files
    19→
    20→## Requirements
    21→
    22→### Validated (M1)
    23→
    24→- ✓ Đăng ký / Đăng nhập tài khoản — v1
    25→- ✓ Dark mode toàn trang — v1
    26→- ✓ Header sticky navigation — v1
    27→- ✓ Footer với thông tin liên hệ — v1
    28→- ✓ Trang chủ với hero + stats — v1
    29→- ✓ Trang đăng ký xét tuyển — v1
    30→- ✓ Trang tra cứu kết quả — v1
    31→- ✓ Trang thông tin tuyển sinh — v1
    32→- ✓ Trang liên hệ — v1
    33→- ✓ Trang FAQ — v1
    34→- ✓ Trang hồ sơ người dùng — v1
    35→- ✓ Trang đăng ký tư vấn — v1
    36→- ✓ Dashboard admin quản lý hồ sơ — v1
    37→- ✓ Báo cáo thống kê admin — v1
    38→- ✓ Cài đặt & FAQ quản lý admin — v1
    39→- ✓ UI/UX Redesign hoàn chỉnh (shadcn/ui, dark mode, responsive) — M1
    40→- ✓ Code splitting + bundle optimization — M1
    41→- ✓ Toast migration (react-hot-toast → sonner) — M1
    42→
    43→### Active (M2)
    44→
    45→- [ ] **PWA-01**: Service Worker với caching strategies
    46→- [ ] **PWA-02**: Offline page khi mất kết nối
    47→- [ ] **PWA-03**: Install prompt để cài đặt ứng dụng
    48→- [ ] **PWA-04**: Push notifications (FCM) khi có cập nhật hồ sơ
    49→- [ ] **RT-01**: SSE endpoint cho real-time updates
    50→- [ ] **RT-02**: Admin notification bell với live counter
    51→- [ ] **RT-03**: Thí sinh nhận thông báo khi kết quả được cập nhật
    52→- [ ] **I18N-01**: i18n infrastructure (react-i18next)
    53→- [ ] **I18N-02**: Language switcher trong header
    54→- [ ] **I18N-03**: Locale files tiếng Việt + tiếng Anh
    55→
    56→### Out of Scope
    57→
    58→- Thay đổi logic nghiệp vụ (business logic giữ nguyên) — chỉ thêm features
    59→- Backend/API refactoring — chỉ thêm SSE endpoint
    60→- TypeScript migration — JavaScript đủ cho scope hiện tại
    61→- Payment gateway — không trong scope M2
    62→
    63→## Context
    64→
    65→**Tech Stack hiện tại (sau M1):**
    66→- React 18 + Vite 7
    67→- Tailwind CSS 3.x (custom config, CSS variables)
    68→- Framer Motion (animations)
    69→- React Router v6.30
    70→- Recharts (charts)
    71→- react-icons (icons)
    72→- sonner (toast notifications)
    73→- axios (HTTP client)
    74→- @base-ui/react (shadcn-style components)
    75→
    76→**Backend:**
    77→- Express.js + MySQL
    78→- JWT authentication
    79→- Nodemailer email templates
    80→
    81→**Deployment:**
    82→- Vercel (frontend, automatic deploy)
    83→- Node.js server (backend on port 3001)
    84→
    85→**PWA state hiện tại:**
    86→- manifest.json đã có (standalone display, icons, theme color)
    87→- Không có Service Worker
    88→
    89→**i18n state hiện tại:**
    90→- Hoàn toàn chưa có — hardcoded tiếng Việt
    91→
    92→## Constraints
    93→
    94→- **Tech Stack**: React + Vite + Tailwind — không đổi framework
    95→- **Backward Compatibility**: Giữ nguyên tất cả routes, business logic, API contracts
    96→- **Bundle Size**: Không tăng quá 50KB sau khi thêm PWA + i18n
    97→- **Browser Support**: Hỗ trợ Chrome/Firefox/Edge/Safari phiên bản mới nhất
    98→- **PWA**: Service Worker phải work offline (cache-first cho assets, network-first cho API)
    99→- **Real-time**: Dùng SSE (Server-Sent Events) thay vì WebSocket — đơn giản hơn cho unidirectional updates
   100→
   101→## Key Decisions
   102→
   103→| Decision | Rationale | Outcome |
   104→|----------|-----------|---------|
   105→| PWA với Workbox | Workbox là thư viện chuẩn, hỗ trợ nhiều caching strategies, được Google maintain | — Pending |
   106→| SSE thay vì WebSocket | Unidirectional (server → client), đơn giản hơn, work được với Vercel edge functions | — Pending |
   107→| react-i18next | Thư viện i18n phổ biến nhất cho React, lazy-load locale files | — Pending |
   108→| FCM cho push notifications | Firebase Cloud Messaging là giải pháp push notification phổ biến nhất | — Pending |
   109→| react-router-dom v6 | Ổn định, tương thích với React 18, API đơn giản | — Pending |
   110→
   111→## Evolution
   112→
   113→This document evolves at phase transitions and milestone boundaries.
   114→
   115→**After each phase transition** (via `/gsd:transition`):
   116→1. Requirements invalidated? → Move to Out of Scope with reason
   117→2. Requirements validated? → Move to Validated with phase reference
   118→3. New requirements emerged? → Add to Active
   119→4. Decisions to log? → Add to Key Decisions
   120→5. "What This Is" still accurate? → Update if drifted
   121→
   122→**After each milestone** (via `/gsd:complete-milestone`):
   123→1. Full review of all sections
   124→2. Core Value check — still the right priority?
   125→3. Audit Out of Scope — reasons still valid?
   126→4. Update Context with current state
   127→
   128→---
   129→*Last updated: 2026-03-30 after M2 milestone initialization*
   130→
```
</details></tool-use>

     8→# Architecture Research
     9→
    10→**Domain:** [domain type]
    11→**Researched:** [date]
    12→**Confidence:** [HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW]
    13→
    14→## Standard Architecture
    15→
    16→### System Overview
    17→
    18→```
    19→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    20→│                        [Layer Name]                          │
    21→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    22→│  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐        │
    23→│  │ [Comp]  │  │ [Comp]  │  │ [Comp]  │  │ [Comp]  │        │
    24→│  └────┬────┘  └────┬────┘  └────┬────┘  └────┬────┘        │
    25→│       │            │            │            │              │
    26→├───────┴────────────┴────────────┴────────────┴──────────────┤
    27→│                        [Layer Name]                          │
    28→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    29→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
    30→│  │                    [Component]                       │    │
    31→│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
    32→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    33→│                        [Layer Name]                          │
    34→│  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐                   │
    35→│  │ [Store]  │  │ [Store]  │  │ [Store]  │                   │
    36→│  └──────────┘  └──────────┘  └──────────┘                   │
    37→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    38→```
    39→
    40→### Component Responsibilities
    41→
    42→| Component | Responsibility | Typical Implementation |
    43→|-----------|----------------|------------------------|
    44→| [name] | [what it owns] | [how it's usually built] |
    45→| [name] | [what it owns] | [how it's usually built] |
    46→| [name] | [what it owns] | [how it's usually built] |
    47→
    48→## Recommended Project Structure
    49→
    50→```
    51→src/
    52→├── [folder]/           # [purpose]
    53→│   ├── [subfolder]/    # [purpose]
    54→│   └── [file].ts       # [purpose]
    55→├── [folder]/           # [purpose]
    56→│   ├── [subfolder]/    # [purpose]
    57→│   └── [file].ts       # [purpose]
    58→├── [folder]/           # [purpose]
    59→└── [folder]/           # [purpose]
    60→```
    61→
    62→### Structure Rationale
    63→
    64→- **[folder]/:** [why organized this way]
    65→- **[folder]/:** [why organized this way]
    66→
    67→## Architectural Patterns
    68→
    69→### Pattern 1: [Pattern Name]
    70→
    71→**What:** [description]
    72→**When to use:** [conditions]
    73→**Trade-offs:** [pros and cons]
    74→
    75→**Example:**
    76→```typescript
    77→// [Brief code example showing the pattern]
    78→```
    79→
    80→### Pattern 2: [Pattern Name]
    81→
    82→**What:** [description]
    83→**When to use:** [conditions]
    84→**Trade-offs:** [pros and cons]
    85→
    86→**Example:**
    87→```typescript
    88→// [Brief code example showing the pattern]
    89→```
    90→
    91→### Pattern 3: [Pattern Name]
    92→
    93→**What:** [description]
    94→**When to use:** [conditions]
    95→**Trade-offs:** [pros and cons]
    96→
    97→## Data Flow
    98→
    99→### Request Flow
   100→
   101→```
   102→[User Action]
   103→    ↓
   104→[Component] → [Handler] → [Service] → [Data Store]
   105→    ↓              ↓           ↓            ↓
   106→[Response] ← [Transform] ← [Query] ← [Database]
   107→```
   108→
   109→### State Management
   110→
   111→```
   112→[State Store]
   113→    ↓ (subscribe)
   114→[Components] ←→ [Actions] → [Reducers/Mutations] → [State Store]
   115→```
   116→
   117→### Key Data Flows
   118→
   119→1. **[Flow name]:** [description of how data moves]
   120→2. **[Flow name]:** [description of how data moves]
   121→
   122→## Scaling Considerations
   123→
   124→| Scale | Architecture Adjustments |
   125→|-------|--------------------------|
   126→| 0-1k users | [approach — usually monolith is fine] |
   127→| 1k-100k users | [approach — what to optimize first] |
   128→| 100k+ users | [approach — when to consider splitting] |
   129→
   130→### Scaling Priorities
   131→
   132→1. **First bottleneck:** [what breaks first, how to fix]
   133→2. **Second bottleneck:** [what breaks next, how to fix]
   134→
   135→## Anti-Patterns
   136→
   137→### Anti-Pattern 1: [Name]
   138→
   139→**What people do:** [the mistake]
   140→**Why it's wrong:** [the problem it causes]
   141→**Do this instead:** [the correct approach]
   142→
   143→### Anti-Pattern 2: [Name]
   144→
   145→**What people do:** [the mistake]
   146→**Why it's wrong:** [the problem it causes]
   147→**Do this instead:** [the correct approach]
   148→
   149→## Integration Points
   150→
   151→### External Services
   152→
   153→| Service | Integration Pattern | Notes |
   154→|---------|---------------------|-------|
   155→| [service] | [how to connect] | [gotchas] |
   156→| [service] | [how to connect] | [gotchas] |
   157→
   158→### Internal Boundaries
   159→
   160→| Boundary | Communication | Notes |
   161→|----------|---------------|-------|
   162→| [module A ↔ module B] | [API/events/direct] | [considerations] |
   163→
   164→## Sources
   165→
   166→- [Architecture references]
   167→- [Official documentation]
   168→- [Case studies]
   169→
   170→---
   171→*Architecture research for: [domain]*
   172→*Researched: [date]*
   173→```
   174→
   175→</template>
   176→
   177→<guidelines>
   178→
   179→**System Overview:**
   180→- Use ASCII box-drawing diagrams for clarity (├── └── │ ─ for structure visualization only)
   181→- Show major components and their relationships
   182→- Don't over-detail — this is conceptual, not implementation
   183→
   184→**Project Structure:**
   185→- Be specific about folder organization
   186→- Explain the rationale for grouping
   187→- Match conventions of the chosen stack
   188→
   189→**Patterns:**
   190→- Include code examples where helpful
   191→- Explain trade-offs honestly
   192→- Note when patterns are overkill for small projects
   193→
   194→**Scaling Considerations:**
   195→- Be realistic — most projects don't need to scale to millions
   196→- Focus on "what breaks first" not theoretical limits
   197→- Avoid premature optimization recommendations
   198→
   199→**Anti-Patterns:**
   200→- Specific to this domain
   201→- Include what to do instead
   202→- Helps prevent common mistakes during implementation
   203→
   204→</guidelines>
   205→
```

 `./\.claude\get-shit-done\templates\research-project\FEATURES.md`

```text
     1→# Features Research Template
     2→
     3→Template for `.planning/research/FEATURES.md` — feature landscape for the project domain.
     4→
     5→<template>
     6→
     7→```markdown
     8→# Feature Research
     9→
    10→**Domain:** [domain type]
    11→**Researched:** [date]
    12→**Confidence:** [HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW]
    13→
    14→## Feature Landscape
    15→
    16→### Table Stakes (Users Expect These)
    17→
    18→Features users assume exist. Missing these = product feels incomplete.
    19→
    20→| Feature | Why Expected | Complexity | Notes |
    21→|---------|--------------|------------|-------|
    22→| [feature] | [user expectation] | LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH | [implementation notes] |
    23→| [feature] | [user expectation] | LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH | [implementation notes] |
    24→| [feature] | [user expectation] | LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH | [implementation notes] |
    25→
    26→### Differentiators (Competitive Advantage)
    27→
    28→Features that set the product apart. Not required, but valuable.
    29→
    30→| Feature | Value Proposition | Complexity | Notes |
    31→|---------|-------------------|------------|-------|
    32→| [feature] | [why it matters] | LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH | [implementation notes] |
    33→| [feature] | [why it matters] | LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH | [implementation notes] |
    34→| [feature] | [why it matters] | LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH | [implementation notes] |
    35→
    36→### Anti-Features (Commonly Requested, Often Problematic)
    37→
    38→Features that seem good but create problems.
    39→
    40→| Feature | Why Requested | Why Problematic | Alternative |
    41→|---------|---------------|-----------------|-------------|
    42→| [feature] | [surface appeal] | [actual problems] | [better approach] |
    43→| [feature] | [surface appeal] | [actual problems] | [better approach] |
    44→
    45→## Feature Dependencies
    46→
    47→```
    48→[Feature A]
    49→    └──requires──> [Feature B]
    50→                       └──requires──> [Feature C]
    51→
    52→[Feature D] ──enhances──> [Feature A]
    53→
    54→[Feature E] ──conflicts──> [Feature F]
    55→```
    56→
    57→### Dependency Notes
    58→
    59→- **[Feature A] requires [Feature B]:** [why the dependency exists]
    60→- **[Feature D] enhances [Feature A]:** [how they work together]
    61→- **[Feature E] conflicts with [Feature F]:** [why they're incompatible]
    62→
    63→## MVP Definition
    64→
    65→### Launch With (v1)
    66→
    67→Minimum viable product — what's needed to validate the concept.
    68→
    69→- [ ] [Feature] — [why essential]
    70→- [ ] [Feature] — [why essential]
    71→- [ ] [Feature] — [why essential]
    72→
    73→### Add After Validation (v1.x)
    74→
    75→Features to add once core is working.
    76→
    77→- [ ] [Feature] — [trigger for adding]
    78→- [ ] [Feature] — [trigger for adding]
    79→
    80→### Future Consideration (v2+)
    81→
    82→Features to defer until product-market fit is established.
    83→
    84→- [ ] [Feature] — [why defer]
    85→- [ ] [Feature] — [why defer]
    86→
    87→## Feature Prioritization Matrix
    88→
    89→| Feature | User Value | Implementation Cost | Priority |
    90→|---------|------------|---------------------|----------|
    91→| [feature] | HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW | HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW | P1/P2/P3 |
    92→| [feature] | HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW | HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW | P1/P2/P3 |
    93→| [feature] | HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW | HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW | P1/P2/P3 |
    94→
    95→**Priority key:**
    96→- P1: Must have for launch
    97→- P2: Should have, add when possible
    98→- P3: Nice to have, future consideration
    99→
   100→## Competitor Feature Analysis
   101→
   102→| Feature | Competitor A | Competitor B | Our Approach |
   103→|---------|--------------|--------------|--------------|
   104→| [feature] | [how they do it] | [how they do it] | [our plan] |
   105→| [feature] | [how they do it] | [how they do it] | [our plan] |
   106→
   107→## Sources
   108→
   109→- [Competitor products analyzed]
   110→- [User research or feedback sources]
   111→- [Industry standards referenced]
   112→
   113→---
   114→*Feature research for: [domain]*
   115→*Researched: [date]*
   116→```
   117→
   118→</template>
   119→
   120→<guidelines>
   121→
   122→**Table Stakes:**
   123→- These are non-negotiable for launch
   124→- Users don't give credit for having them, but penalize for missing them
   125→- Example: A community platform without user profiles is broken
   126→
   127→**Differentiators:**
   128→- These are where you compete
   129→- Should align with the Core Value from PROJECT.md
   130→- Don't try to differentiate on everything
   131→
   132→**Anti-Features:**
   133→- Prevent scope creep by documenting what seems good but isn't
   134→- Include the alternative approach
   135→- Example: "Real-time everything" often creates complexity without value
   136→
   137→**Feature Dependencies:**
   138→- Critical for roadmap phase ordering
   139→- If A requires B, B must be in an earlier phase
   140→- Conflicts inform what NOT to combine in same phase
   141→
   142→**MVP Definition:**
   143→- Be ruthless about what's truly minimum
   144→- "Nice to have" is not MVP
   145→- Launch with less, validate, then expand
   146→
   147→</guidelines>
   148→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→# DoAnTuyenSinh - Website Tuyển Sinh HUTECH
     2→
     3→## What This Is
     4→
     5→Website tuyển sinh trực tuyến của Trường Đại học HUTECH — cho phép thí sinh đăng ký xét tuyển, tra cứu kết quả, đăng ký tư vấn, và quản lý hồ sơ tuyển sinh. Hệ thống bao gồm trang công khai (public) và trang quản trị (admin) riêng biệt.
     6→
     7→## Core Value
     8→
     9→Thí sinh có thể hoàn tất toàn bộ quy trình tuyển sinh từ đăng ký đến tra cứu kết quả trên nền tảng web hiện đại, chuyên nghiệp, nhanh chóng.
    10→
    11→## Current Milestone: M2 — Advanced Features
    12→
    13→**Goal:** Thêm 3 tính năng nâng cao: PWA hoàn chỉnh, real-time notifications, và đa ngôn ngữ (i18n).
    14→
    15→**Target features:**
    16→- Option A — PWA: Service Worker + offline support + install prompt + push notifications
    17→- Option B — Real-time: SSE cho admin thấy hồ sơ mới, thí sinh thấy cập nhật kết quả
    18→- Option C — i18n: Tiếng Việt + Tiếng Anh, lazy-load locale files
    19→
    20→## Requirements
    21→
    22→### Validated (M1)
    23→
    24→- ✓ Đăng ký / Đăng nhập tài khoản — v1
    25→- ✓ Dark mode toàn trang — v1
    26→- ✓ Header sticky navigation — v1
    27→- ✓ Footer với thông tin liên hệ — v1
    28→- ✓ Trang chủ với hero + stats — v1
    29→- ✓ Trang đăng ký xét tuyển — v1
    30→- ✓ Trang tra cứu kết quả — v1
    31→- ✓ Trang thông tin tuyển sinh — v1
    32→- ✓ Trang liên hệ — v1
    33→- ✓ Trang FAQ — v1
    34→- ✓ Trang hồ sơ người dùng — v1
    35→- ✓ Trang đăng ký tư vấn — v1
    36→- ✓ Dashboard admin quản lý hồ sơ — v1
    37→- ✓ Báo cáo thống kê admin — v1
    38→- ✓ Cài đặt & FAQ quản lý admin — v1
    39→- ✓ UI/UX Redesign hoàn chỉnh (shadcn/ui, dark mode, responsive) — M1
    40→- ✓ Code splitting + bundle optimization — M1
    41→- ✓ Toast migration (react-hot-toast → sonner) — M1
    42→
    43→### Active (M2)
    44→
    45→- [ ] **PWA-01**: Service Worker với caching strategies
    46→- [ ] **PWA-02**: Offline page khi mất kết nối
    47→- [ ] **PWA-03**: Install prompt để cài đặt ứng dụng
    48→- [ ] **PWA-04**: Push notifications (FCM) khi có cập nhật hồ sơ
    49→- [ ] **RT-01**: SSE endpoint cho real-time updates
    50→- [ ] **RT-02**: Admin notification bell với live counter
    51→- [ ] **RT-03**: Thí sinh nhận thông báo khi kết quả được cập nhật
    52→- [ ] **I18N-01**: i18n infrastructure (react-i18next)
    53→- [ ] **I18N-02**: Language switcher trong header
    54→- [ ] **I18N-03**: Locale files tiếng Việt + tiếng Anh
    55→
    56→### Out of Scope
    57→
    58→- Thay đổi logic nghiệp vụ (business logic giữ nguyên) — chỉ thêm features
    59→- Backend/API refactoring — chỉ thêm SSE endpoint
    60→- TypeScript migration — JavaScript đủ cho scope hiện tại
    61→- Payment gateway — không trong scope M2
    62→
    63→## Context
    64→
    65→**Tech Stack hiện tại (sau M1):**
    66→- React 18 + Vite 7
    67→- Tailwind CSS 3.x (custom config, CSS variables)
    68→- Framer Motion (animations)
    69→- React Router v6.30
    70→- Recharts (charts)
    71→- react-icons (icons)
    72→- sonner (toast notifications)
    73→- axios (HTTP client)
    74→- @base-ui/react (shadcn-style components)
    75→
    76→**Backend:**
    77→- Express.js + MySQL
    78→- JWT authentication
    79→- Nodemailer email templates
    80→
    81→**Deployment:**
    82→- Vercel (frontend, automatic deploy)
    83→- Node.js server (backend on port 3001)
    84→
    85→**PWA state hiện tại:**
    86→- manifest.json đã có (standalone display, icons, theme color)
    87→- Không có Service Worker
    88→
    89→**i18n state hiện tại:**
    90→- Hoàn toàn chưa có — hardcoded tiếng Việt
    91→
    92→## Constraints
    93→
    94→- **Tech Stack**: React + Vite + Tailwind — không đổi framework
    95→- **Backward Compatibility**: Giữ nguyên tất cả routes, business logic, API contracts
    96→- **Bundle Size**: Không tăng quá 50KB sau khi thêm PWA + i18n
    97→- **Browser Support**: Hỗ trợ Chrome/Firefox/Edge/Safari phiên bản mới nhất
    98→- **PWA**: Service Worker phải work offline (cache-first cho assets, network-first cho API)
    99→- **Real-time**: Dùng SSE (Server-Sent Events) thay vì WebSocket — đơn giản hơn cho unidirectional updates
   100→
   101→## Key Decisions
   102→
   103→| Decision | Rationale | Outcome |
   104→|----------|-----------|---------|
   105→| PWA với Workbox | Workbox là thư viện chuẩn, hỗ trợ nhiều caching strategies, được Google maintain | — Pending |
   106→| SSE thay vì WebSocket | Unidirectional (server → client), đơn giản hơn, work được với Vercel edge functions | — Pending |
   107→| react-i18next | Thư viện i18n phổ biến nhất cho React, lazy-load locale files | — Pending |
   108→| FCM cho push notifications | Firebase Cloud Messaging là giải pháp push notification phổ biến nhất | — Pending |
   109→| react-router-dom v6 | Ổn định, tương thích với React 18, API đơn giản | — Pending |
   110→
   111→## Evolution
   112→
   113→This document evolves at phase transitions and milestone boundaries.
   114→
   115→**After each phase transition** (via `/gsd:transition`):
   116→1. Requirements invalidated? → Move to Out of Scope with reason
   117→2. Requirements validated? → Move to Validated with phase reference
   118→3. New requirements emerged? → Add to Active
   119→4. Decisions to log? → Add to Key Decisions
   120→5. "What This Is" still accurate? → Update if drifted
   121→
   122→**After each milestone** (via `/gsd:complete-milestone`):
   123→1. Full review of all sections
   124→2. Core Value check — still the right priority?
   125→3. Audit Out of Scope — reasons still valid?
   126→4. Update Context with current state
   127→
   128→---
   129→*Last updated: 2026-03-30 after M2 milestone initialization*
   130→
```
</details></tool-use>

   169→
   170→**Critical Pitfalls:**
   171→- Focus on domain-specific issues, not generic mistakes
   172→- Include warning signs — early detection prevents disasters
   173→- Link to specific phases — makes pitfalls actionable
   174→
   175→**Technical Debt:**
   176→- Be realistic — some shortcuts are acceptable
   177→- Note when shortcuts are "never acceptable" vs. "only in MVP"
   178→- Include the long-term cost to inform tradeoff decisions
   179→
   180→**Performance Traps:**
   181→- Include scale thresholds ("breaks at 10k users")
   182→- Focus on what's relevant for this project's expected scale
   183→- Don't over-engineer for hypothetical scale
   184→
   185→**Security Mistakes:**
   186→- Beyond OWASP basics — domain-specific issues
   187→- Example: Community platforms have different security concerns than e-commerce
   188→- Include risk level to prioritize
   189→
   190→**"Looks Done But Isn't":**
   191→- Checklist format for verification during execution
   192→- Common in demos vs. production
   193→- Prevents "it works on my machine" issues
   194→
   195→**Pitfall-to-Phase Mapping:**
   196→- Critical for roadmap creation
   197→- Each pitfall should map to a phase that prevents it
   198→- Informs phase ordering and success criteria
   199→
   200→</guidelines>

I don't have the ability to perform web searches, as that capability isn't available to me. However, I can still try to help based on my training data.

**A few notes:**

1.  **Future Date:** Your query includes "2026", which is beyond my knowledge cutoff of April 2024. I cannot provide information about specific versions or updates that may have occurred in 2026.
2.  **Version Mismatch:** "Vite 7" doesn't exist yet. As of my last update, the latest major stable versions were Vite 4 and 5, with Vite 6 in beta or early release. "vite-plugin-pwa 2026" is also not information I have.

**What I can help with (based on data up to April 2024):**

For a React 18 + Vite project (versions like 4.x/5.x), a standard `vite-plugin-pwa` setup usually involves:

1.  **Installation:** `npm install vite-plugin-pwa workbox-window`
2.  **Vite Config (`vite.config.js/ts`):**
    ```javascript
    import { VitePWA } from 'vite-plugin-pwa'

    export default {
      plugins: [
        VitePWA({
          registerType: 'autoUpdate',
          includeAssets: ['favicon.ico', 'apple-touch-icon.png', 'mask-icon.svg'],
          manifest: {
            name: 'My App',
            short_name: 'App',
            description: 'My Progressive Web App',
            theme_color: '#ffffff',
            icons: [ /* your icons config */ ]
          },
          workbox: {
            globPatterns: ['**/*.{js,css,html,ico,png,svg}']
          }
        })
      ],
    }
    ```
3.  **Usage in `main.jsx`:**
    ```javascript
    import { registerSW } from 'virtual:pwa-register'

    const updateSW = registerSW({
      onNeedRefresh() {
        if (confirm('New content available. Reload?')) {
          updateSW()
        }
      },
      onOfflineReady() {
        console.log('App ready to work offline')
      },
    })
    ```

**To get the specific guidance you are looking for regarding 2026, Vite 7, and the latest configurations, I recommend:**
1.  Checking the official GitHub repository for `vite-plugin-pwa`.
2.  Searching on Google or a search engine for the specific query you provided.
3.  Checking the Vite official blog for version updates.

Is there anything else I can assist you with regarding PWA configurations or other topics?

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have a good understanding of both the project and the template. Let me research the specific pitfalls for PWA, Real-time notifications, and i18n in the context of React + Vite + Express.js, and then write the findings to the PITFALLS.md file.

Let me think through each area carefully:

### PWA Pitfalls:
1. **SW registration timing** - Registering too early/late, race conditions with dev tools
2. **Caching strategies** - Wrong strategies for different resource types, cache poisoning, stale caches
3. **Vercel edge deployment** - Vercel edge caching conflicts with SW, 100% cache-hit bypasses SW


4. **SSE connection handling** - Improper cleanup on unmount causing memory leaks, duplicate connections, CORS issues with Vercel
5. **Reconnection logic** - Exponential backoff traps, reconnecting too aggressively, missing cleanup on unmount
6. **Memory leaks** - Event listeners not cleaned up, SSE stream not properly aborted

### i18n Pitfalls:
1. **String extraction** - Missing keys during development, inconsistent naming conventions
2. **Lazy loading locale files** - Race conditions, wrong bundling, missing fallbacks
3. **RTL support** - CSS not handling text direction properly, mixed content issues
4. **SEO meta tags per locale** - Duplicate content issues, hreflang tags missing, meta tags not updating on language switch
5. **React-i18next namespace management** - Large bundle sizes if not lazy loaded

Now I'm going to search for current best practices and common mistakes in these areas to ground my findings in real-world experience.
</details></think>

     1→import React, { Suspense, lazy } from "react";
     2→import { BrowserRouter, Routes, Route, useLocation, Navigate } from "react-router-dom";
     3→import { HelmetProvider } from "react-helmet-async";
     4→import { AnimatePresence } from "framer-motion";
     5→import ThanhHeader from "./components/ThanhHeader";
     6→import ChanTrang from "./components/ChanTrang";
     7→import ScrollToTop from "./components/ScrollToTop";
     8→import { UserContextProvider } from "./accounts/UserContext";
     9→import { DarkModeProvider } from "./contexts/DarkModeContext";
    10→import { Toaster } from "./components/ui/sonner";
    11→import PageTransition from "./components/ui/PageTransition";
    12→import AdminLayout from "./admin/components/AdminLayout";
    13→import AdminProtectedRoute from "./components/AdminProtectedRoute";
    14→import ErrorBoundary from "./components/ErrorBoundary";
    15→
    16→// Lazy-loaded public pages
    17→const TrangChu = lazy(() => import("./pages/TrangChu"));
    18→const ThongTinTuyenSinh = lazy(() => import("./pages/ThongTinTuyenSinh"));
    19→const DangKyXetTuyen = lazy(() => import("./pages/DangKyXetTuyen"));
    20→const TraCuuKetQua = lazy(() => import("./pages/TraCuuKetQua"));
    21→const LienHe = lazy(() => import("./pages/LienHe"));
    22→const DangNhap = lazy(() => import("./accounts/DangNhap"));
    23→const DangKyTaiKhoan = lazy(() => import("./accounts/DangKyTaiKhoan"));
    24→const DangKyTaiKhoanAdmin = lazy(() => import("./accounts/DangKyTaiKhoanAdmin"));
    25→const QuenMatKhau = lazy(() => import("./accounts/QuenMatKhau"));
    26→const CauHoiThuongGap = lazy(() => import("./pages/CauHoiThuongGap"));
    27→const DangKyHocBong = lazy(() => import("./pages/DangKyHocBong"));
    28→const DangKyTuVan = lazy(() => import("./pages/DangKyTuVan"));
    29→const HoSoNguoiDung = lazy(() => import("./pages/HoSoNguoiDung"));
    30→
    31→// Lazy-loaded admin pages
    32→const TongQuan = lazy(() => import("./admin/pages/TongQuan"));
    33→const QuanLyHoSo = lazy(() => import("./admin/pages/QuanLyHoSo"));
    34→const QuanLyFAQ = lazy(() => import("./admin/pages/QuanLyFAQ"));
    35→const BaoCao = lazy(() => import("./admin/pages/BaoCao"));
    36→const CaiDat = lazy(() => import("./admin/pages/CaiDat"));
    37→const HoSoQuanLi = lazy(() => import("./admin/pages/HoSoQuanLi"));
    38→
    39→// Loading fallback
    40→const PageLoader = () => (
    41→  <div className="min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center">
    42→    <div className="loading-dots">
    43→      <div />
    44→      <div />
    45→      <div />
    46→    </div>
    47→  </div>
    48→);
    49→
    50→// Admin loading fallback
    51→const AdminPageLoader = () => (
    52→  <div className="flex-1 flex items-center justify-center">
    53→    <div className="loading-dots">
    54→      <div />
    55→      <div />
    56→      <div />
    57→    </div>
    58→  </div>
    59→);
    60→
    61→// Public Layout with lazy loading
    62→function PublicLayout() {
    63→  const location = useLocation();
    64→
    65→  return (
    66→    <>
    67→      <ThanhHeader />
    68→      <AnimatePresence mode="wait" initial={false}>
    69→        <Routes location={location} key={location.pathname}>
    70→          <Route
    71→            path="/"
    72→            element={
    73→              <Suspense fallback={<PageLoader />}>
    74→                <PageTransition><TrangChu /></PageTransition>
    75→              </Suspense>
    76→            }
    77→          />
    78→          <Route
    79→            path="/thong-tin-tuyen-sinh"
    80→            element={
    81→              <Suspense fallback={<PageLoader />}>
    82→                <PageTransition><ThongTinTuyenSinh /></PageTransition>
    83→              </Suspense>
    84→            }
    85→          />
    86→          <Route
    87→            path="/dang-ky-xet-tuyen"
    88→            element={
    89→              <Suspense fallback={<PageLoader />}>
    90→                <PageTransition><DangKyXetTuyen /></PageTransition>
    91→              </Suspense>
    92→            }
    93→          />
    94→          <Route
    95→            path="/tra-cuu-ket-qua"
    96→            element={
    97→              <Suspense fallback={<PageLoader />}>
    98→                <PageTransition><TraCuuKetQua /></PageTransition>
    99→              </Suspense>
   100→            }
   101→          />
   102→          <Route
   103→            path="/lien-he"
   104→            element={
   105→              <Suspense fallback={<PageLoader />}>
   106→                <PageTransition><LienHe /></PageTransition>
   107→              </Suspense>
   108→            }
   109→          />
   110→          <Route
   111→            path="/accounts/dang-nhap"
   112→            element={
   113→              <Suspense fallback={<PageLoader />}>
   114→                <PageTransition type="fade"><DangNhap /></PageTransition>
   115→              </Suspense>
   116→            }
   117→          />
   118→          <Route
   119→            path="/login"
   120→            element={<Navigate to="/accounts/dang-nhap" replace />}
   121→          />
   122→          <Route
   123→            path="/accounts/dang-ky"
   124→            element={

   126→                <PageTransition type="fade"><DangKyTaiKhoan /></PageTransition>
   127→              </Suspense>
   128→            }

   130→          <Route

   139→            path="/accounts/quen-mat-khau"

   142→                <PageTransition type="fade"><QuenMatKhau /></PageTransition>
   143→              </Suspense>
   144→            }
   145→          />
   146→          <Route
   147→            path="/cau-hoi-thuong-gap"
   148→            element={

   150→                <PageTransition><CauHoiThuongGap /></PageTransition>
   151→              </Suspense>
   152→            }
   153→          />
   154→          <Route
   155→            path="/dang-ky-hoc-bong"
   156→            element={
   157→              <Suspense fallback={<PageLoader />}>
   158→                <PageTransition><DangKyHocBong /></PageTransition>
   159→              </Suspense>
   160→            }
   161→          />
   162→          <Route
   163→            path="/dang-ky-tu-van"
   164→            element={

   166→                <PageTransition><DangKyTuVan /></PageTransition>
   167→              </Suspense>
   168→            }
   169→          />
   170→          <Route
   171→            path="/ho-so-nguoi-dung"

   178→        </Routes>
   179→      </AnimatePresence>
   180→      <ChanTrang />
   181→    </>
   182→  );
   183→}
   184→
   185→function App() {
   186→  return (
   187→    <HelmetProvider>
   188→      <DarkModeProvider>
   189→        <UserContextProvider>
   190→          <BrowserRouter>
   191→            <ScrollToTop />
   192→            <Toaster position="top-right" richColors closeButton expand={false} />
   193→            <ErrorBoundary>
   194→            <Routes>
   195→              {/* Admin Routes */}
   196→              <Route
   197→                path="/admin"
   198→                element={
   199→                  <AdminProtectedRoute>
   200→                    <AdminLayout currentPage="tong-quan">
   201→                      <Suspense fallback={<AdminPageLoader />}><TongQuan /></Suspense>
   202→                    </AdminLayout>
   203→                  </AdminProtectedRoute>
   204→                }
   205→              />
   206→              <Route
   207→                path="/admin/tong-quan"
   208→                element={
   209→                  <AdminProtectedRoute>
   210→                    <AdminLayout currentPage="tong-quan">
   211→                      <Suspense fallback={<AdminPageLoader />}><TongQuan /></Suspense>
   212→                    </AdminLayout>
   213→                  </AdminProtectedRoute>
   214→                }
   215→              />
   216→              <Route
   217→                path="/admin/quan-ly-ho-so"
   218→                element={
   219→                  <AdminProtectedRoute>
   220→                    <AdminLayout currentPage="quan-ly-ho-so">

   226→              <Route
