   116→|    |--- main.jsx               # MODIFY -- import i18n config
   117→|
   118→|--- vite.config.js             # MODIFY -- add vite-plugin-pwa
   119→|
   120→|--- backend/
   121→     |--- index.js               # MODIFY -- add SSE route + notification endpoints
   122→     |--- services/
   123→          |--- sseService.js     # NEW -- SSE client registry + broadcast
   124→          |--- notificationService.js  # NEW -- MySQL notification CRUD
   125→```
   126→
   127→### Structure Rationale
   128→
   129→- **`public/locales/`**: Served as static assets, loaded at runtime by `i18next-http-backend`. Not bundled -- keeps JS chunks small. Vite serves these from `public/` automatically.
   130→- **`src/i18n/`**: Init config, custom hooks, inline fallback strings. Separated from components for clear ownership.
   131→- **`src/contexts/NotificationContext.jsx`**: Centralizes SSE connection management, FCM subscription state, and unread count. Components subscribe; context manages the lifecycle.
   132→- **`backend/services/sseService.js`**: Singleton that holds connected `res` objects. Emit events when database records change (new application, status update).
   133→
   134→## Architectural Patterns
   135→
   136→### Pattern 1: PWA Service Worker via vite-plugin-pwa
   137→
   138→**What:** Vite plugin that generates a Workbox-powered Service Worker during `vite build`, injected into `index.html` automatically.
   139→
   140→**When to use:** Any PWA feature (offline, caching, push notifications).
   141→
   142→**Trade-offs:** The plugin auto-generates `sw.js` in the build output (`dist/`). You configure strategies in `vite.config.js`. During dev, it can use a devtools-friendly approach (not full SW). After build, the SW is served from the Vercel static host.
   143→
   144→**Integration with existing `manifest.json`:**
   145→- The existing `public/manifest.json` is referenced by `index.html` via `<link rel="manifest">`.
   146→- vite-plugin-pwa can auto-generate a manifest or you can reference the existing one via `injectManifest: false` and `manifest: false` config.
   147→- **Recommendation:** Keep existing `manifest.json`, configure vite-plugin-pwa to reference it via `srcDir`/`filename` or simply add the `id: "sw-base"` field to existing manifest.
   148→
   149→**Example vite.config.js addition:**
   150→```javascript
   151→import { VitePWA } from 'vite-plugin-pwa'
   152→
   153→export default defineConfig({
   154→    plugins: [
   155→        react(),
   156→        VitePWA({
   157→            registerType: 'autoUpdate',
   158→            injectRegister: 'auto',
   159→            workbox: {
   160→                globPatterns: ['**/*.{js,css,html,ico,png,svg,woff2}'],
   161→                runtimeCaching: [
   162→                    {
   163→                        urlPattern: /^https:\/\/do-an-tuyen-sinh\.vercel\.app\/api\//,
   164→                        handler: 'NetworkFirst',
   165→                        options: {
   166→                            cacheName: 'api-cache',
   167→                            networkTimeoutSeconds: 5,
   168→                            expiration: { maxEntries: 50, maxAgeSeconds: 86400 }
   169→                        }
   170→                    },
   171→                    {
   172→                        urlPattern: /^https:\/\/fonts\.(googleapis|gstatic)\.com/,
   173→                        handler: 'CacheFirst',
   174→                        options: {
   175→                            cacheName: 'google-fonts',
   176→                            expiration: { maxEntries: 10, maxAgeSeconds: 2592000 }
   177→                        }
   178→                    }
   179→                ]
   180→            },
   181→            manifest: false  // Use existing manifest.json
   182→        })
   183→    ]
   184→})
   185→```
   186→
   187→---
   188→
   189→### Pattern 2: SSE Endpoint in Express
   190→
   191→**What:** HTTP endpoint that keeps the response connection open and streams events to the client. Unlike WebSocket, SSE is unidirectional (server-to-client only) and works over standard HTTP.
   192→
   193→**When to use:** Admin gets live updates on new applications; candidates get status change notifications. No client-to-server messaging needed.
   194→
   195→**Trade-offs:** SSE connections are long-lived -- the Express server must manage a registry of connected clients. Each connected client consumes a server thread/connection. For low-to-medium traffic (this project's scale), this is fine. Vercel serverless functions do NOT support persistent SSE connections (functions are stateless), so SSE only works against the backend server on port 3001, not Vercel.
   196→
   197→**Critical integration point:** The CORS config in `backend/index.js` currently allows `localhost:5173` and `do-an-tuyen-sinh.vercel.app`. The SSE endpoint must share this CORS setup.
   198→
   199→**Backend SSE implementation:**
   200→```javascript
   201→// backend/services/sseService.js
   202→class SSEService {
   203→    constructor() {
   204→        this.clients = new Map(); // userId -> Set<res>
   205→    }
   206→
   207→    addClient(userId, res) {
   208→        if (!this.clients.has(userId)) this.clients.set(userId, new Set());
   209→        this.clients.get(userId).add(res);
   210→    }
   211→
   212→    removeClient(userId, res) {
   213→        if (this.clients.has(userId)) {
   214→            this.clients.get(userId).delete(res);
   215→            if (this.clients.get(userId).size === 0) this.clients.delete(userId);
   216→        }
   217→    }
   218→
   219→    broadcast(userId, event, data) {
   220→        const clientSet = this.clients.get(userId);
   221→        if (!clientSet) return;
   222→        const message = `event: ${event}\ndata: ${JSON.stringify(data)}\n\n`;
   223→        for (const res of clientSet) {
   224→            res.write(message);
   225→        }
   226→    }
   227→
   228→    broadcastAll(event, data) {
   229→        const message = `event: ${event}\ndata: ${JSON.stringify(data)}\n\n`;
   230→        for (const [, clientSet] of this.clients) {
   231→            for (const res of clientSet) res.write(message);
   232→        }
   233→    }
   234→}
   235→
   236→export default new SSEService();
   237→
   238→// backend/index.js -- SSE endpoint
   239→import sseService from './services/sseService.js';
   240→
   241→app.get('/api/sse/events', authenticateToken, (req, res) => {
   242→    res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'text/event-stream');
   243→    res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'no-cache');
   244→    res.setHeader('Connection', 'keep-alive');
   245→    res.setHeader('X-Accel-Buffering', 'no');  // Disable nginx buffering
   246→    res.flushHeaders();
   247→
   248→    const userId = req.user.id;
   249→    const isAdmin = req.user.role === 'admin';
   250→
   251→    sseService.addClient(isAdmin ? 'admin' : userId, res);
   252→
   253→    // Send heartbeat every 30s to keep connection alive
   254→    const heartbeat = setInterval(() => {
   255→        res.write(': heartbeat\n\n');
   256→    }, 30000);
   257→
   258→    req.on('close', () => {
   259→        clearInterval(heartbeat);
   260→        sseService.removeClient(isAdmin ? 'admin' : userId, res);
   261→    });
   262→});
   263→
   264→// Trigger SSE from existing routes when data changes
   265→// e.g., after new application POST succeeds:
   266→app.post('/api/applications', ..., async (req, res) => {
   267→    // ... existing logic ...
   268→    // After save:
   269→    sseService.broadcast('admin', 'new_application', {
   270→        id: newApp.id,
   271→        candidateName: newApp.fullName,
   272→        timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
   273→    });
   274→    // For candidate:
   275→    sseService.broadcast(userId, 'status_update', {
   276→        applicationId: appId,
   277→        status: newStatus
   278→    });
   279→});
   280→```
   281→
   282→---
   283→
   284→### Pattern 3: i18n with Lazy-Loaded Locale Files
   285→
   286→**What:** Translation strings stored in `public/locales/{lang}/{namespace}.json`, loaded asynchronously by `i18next-http-backend` only when needed.
   287→
   288→**When to use:** Multi-language support with bundle size constraint (50KB max increase).
   289→
   290→**Trade-offs:** First language switch has a network request (JSON file). Subsequent switches may use browser cache. Fallback language loads synchronously as inline JS to prevent flash-of-untranslated-content (FOTUC).
   291→
   292→**Why `public/locales/` instead of `src/locales/`:**
   293→- Files in `public/` are served as static assets, not bundled by Vite.
   294→- `i18next-http-backend` fetches them via HTTP at runtime.
   295→- This keeps JS bundle small -- only the i18n library code (~23KB gzipped for full react-i18next) is in the bundle.
   296→- Locale JSON files are cached by the browser.
   297→
   298→**i18n config (`src/i18n/config.js`):**
   299→```javascript
   300→import i18n from 'i18next';
   301→import { initReactI18next } from 'react-i18next';
   302→import HttpBackend from 'i18next-http-backend';
   303→import LanguageDetector from 'i18next-browser-languagedetector';
   304→
   305→i18n
   306→    .use(HttpBackend)           // Load from /locales/{lng}/{ns}.json
   307→    .use(LanguageDetector)     // Detect browser language
   308→    .use(initReactI18next)
   309→    .init({
   310→        fallbackLng: 'vi',
   311→        defaultNS: 'translation',
   312→        ns: ['translation', 'common'],
   313→        backend: {
   314→            loadPath: '/locales/{{lng}}/{{ns}}.json'
   315→        },
   316→        detection: {
   317→            order: ['localStorage', 'navigator'],
   318→            caches: ['localStorage']
   319→        },
   320→        interpolation: {
   321→            escapeValue: false  // React already escapes
   322→        }
   323→    });
   324→
   325→export default i18n;
   326→```
   327→
   328→**LanguageSwitcher integration into existing header:**
   329→The existing `ThanhHeader.jsx` renders the navigation. Add `LanguageSwitcher` component into the header's right section (near ThemeToggle). It reads/writes the language to localStorage via i18next detector, triggering a re-render of all translated strings.
   330→
   331→---
   332→
   333→## Data Flow
   334→
   335→### Flow 1: PWA Install
   336→
   337→```
   338→User visits site
   339→    -> Browser detects manifest.json (existing)
   340→    -> Browser shows "Add to Home Screen" prompt
   341→    -> User taps "Install"
   342→    -> vite-plugin-pwa registers Service Worker from /sw.js (generated at build)
   343→    -> SW caches all static assets (cache-first for JS/CSS, network-first for API)
   344→```
   345→
   346→### Flow 2: Offline Behavior
   347→
   348→```
   349→User loses connection
   350→    -> SW intercepts fetch request
   351→    -> For /api/* requests: returns cached response OR shows offline error
   352→    -> For static assets: returns from cache instantly
   353→    -> React app renders <OfflineBoundary> wrapping content
   354→    -> Toast via sonner: "You are offline. Some features may be limited."
   355→```
   356→
   357→### Flow 3: SSE Real-time Updates
   358→
   359→```
   360→[Admin Dashboard]                          [Backend Server]
   361→       |                                        |
   362→       |-- GET /api/sse/events (JWT) --------->|
   363→       |                                        | (register res in sseService.clients)
   364→       |                                        |
   365→       |            <--- event: new_application -|
   366→       |<--- SSE stream -----------------------|
   367→       |                                        |
   368→[Thi sinh]                                   |
   369→       |                                        |
   370→       |-- GET /api/sse/events (JWT) --------->|
   371→       |                                        |
   372→       |            <--- event: status_update --|
   373→       |<--- SSE stream -----------------------|
   374→       |                                        |
   375→       | [sonner toast: "Kết quả xét tuyển đã cập nhật"]
   376→```
   377→
   378→### Flow 4: i18n Language Switch
   379→
   380→```
   381→User clicks LanguageSwitcher (VI -> EN)
   382→    -> i18n.changeLanguage('en')
   383→    -> localStorage updated ('i18nextLng': 'en')
   384→    -> All useTranslation() hooks re-render with new strings
   385→    -> HTTP request: GET /locales/en/translation.json (cached subsequent times)
   386→    -> No full page reload
   387→```
   388→
   389→### Flow 5: Push Notifications (FCM)
   390→
   391→```
   392→Service Worker registered
   393→    -> SW requests Notification.permission
   394→    -> If granted: SW calls FCM subscribe with VAPID key
   395→    -> Backend stores FCM device token per user
   396→    -> On status change: backend sends FCM message via firebase-admin
   397→    -> FCM pushes to user's browser
   398→    -> SW receives push event, shows Notification + sonner toast
   399→```
   400→
   401→## Integration Points with Existing Code
   402→
   403→| Location | Change | Type |
   404→|----------|--------|------|
   405→| `src/main.jsx` | Add `import './i18n/config.js'` before App import | Modify |
   406→| `src/App.jsx` | Wrap with `<NotificationProvider>` inside UserContext | Modify |
   407→| `src/components/ThanhHeader.jsx` | Import and render `<LanguageSwitcher>` and `<NotificationBell>` (admin only) | Modify |
   408→| `src/components/ChanTrang.jsx` | Add `<OfflineBoundary>` wrapper around children | Modify |
   409→| `vite.config.js` | Add `VitePWA()` plugin with caching strategies | Modify |
   410→| `public/manifest.json` | Add `"id": "sw-base"` field for SW scoping | Modify |
   411→| `backend/index.js` | Add SSE route, SSE service init, notification endpoints | Modify |
   412→| `backend/services/` | New: `sseService.js`, `notificationService.js` | Add |
   413→| `public/locales/` | New: JSON translation files for VI/EN | Add |
   414→| `src/i18n/` | New: config, hooks, locale fallbacks | Add |
   415→| `src/contexts/NotificationContext.jsx` | New: SSE + push notification state | Add |
   416→| `src/hooks/` | New: `useSSE.js`, `useOffline.js`, `usePushNotification.js` | Add |
   417→| `src/components/ui/` | New: `LanguageSwitcher`, `NotificationBell`, `OfflinePage`, `InstallPrompt` | Add |
   418→
   419→## Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   420→
   421→### Anti-Pattern 1: Loading All Locales in the JS Bundle
   422→
   423→**What people do:** Import all translation JSON files as modules (`import vi from './locales/vi.json'`) and pass them to i18next init.
   424→
   425→**Why it's wrong:** All translation strings become part of the JavaScript bundle. With two languages and multiple namespaces, this adds 30-100KB+ to every chunk that imports i18n. Violates the 50KB bundle budget.
   426→
   427→**Do this instead:** Store locale files in `public/locales/` and use `i18next-http-backend` to fetch them at runtime. Only the i18n library (~23KB gzipped) goes into the bundle.
   428→
   429→### Anti-Pattern 2: SSE Without Heartbeat
   430→
   431→**What people do:** Open an SSE connection without periodic heartbeat messages.
   432→
   433→**Why it's wrong:** Proxies, load balancers, and some corporate firewalls close long-lived connections that appear idle. Without heartbeat, the connection dies silently after ~30-60 seconds.
   434→
   435→**Do this instead:** Send a comment heartbeat (`res.write(': ping\n\n')`) every 30 seconds. The client should reconnect on error. The `NotificationContext` should handle reconnection with exponential backoff.
   436→
   437→### Anti-Pattern 3: SW Caching All API Requests
   438→
   439→**What people do:** Use `CacheFirst` strategy for all API requests, so stale data is served even when online.
   440→
   441→**Why it's wrong:** Admissions data, application status, and personal records must be current. Serving stale cached data creates confusion and incorrect decisions.
   442→
   443→**Do this instead:** Use `NetworkFirst` with a short timeout (3-5 seconds) for API requests. If network fails, fall back to cache. For admin pages with real-time needs, consider skipping SW cache entirely for those routes.
   444→
   445→### Anti-Pattern 4: Not Wrapping i18n with Suspense
   446→
   447→**What people do:** Initialize i18next with async backend loading but render translated content before i18next is ready.
   448→
   449→**Why it's wrong:** On first load, locale files must be fetched. Without Suspense, translated content shows as empty keys (e.g., `translation.key`) briefly.
   450→
   451→**Do this instead:** In `main.jsx`, wrap the App with `<Suspense fallback={<PageLoader />}>`. The `PageLoader` component already exists and is used for page-level lazy loading -- reuse it for i18n hydration.
   452→
   453→### Anti-Pattern 5: SSE on Vercel Serverless
   454→
   455→**What people do:** Deploying SSE endpoint as a Vercel serverless function.
   456→
   457→**Why it's wrong:** Serverless functions are stateless and short-lived (max ~10s for Hobby, ~30s for Pro). SSE requires a persistent connection that outlives function invocations. Vercel will terminate the connection immediately.
   458→
   459→**Do this instead:** SSE must connect to the backend Express server on port 3001 (self-hosted or VPS). The Vercel-hosted frontend proxies SSE requests to the backend, or the backend domain is used directly. Vercel edge functions also do not support SSE.
   460→
   461→## Scaling Considerations
   462→
   463→| Scale | Architecture Adjustments |
   464→|-------|--------------------------|
   465→| 0-100 concurrent users | SSE with single Express server is fine. No changes needed. |
   466→| 100-500 concurrent users | SSE connection registry may grow. Monitor memory. Consider Redis pub/sub if scaling to multiple backend instances. |
   467→| 500+ concurrent users | SSE becomes a bottleneck. Migrate to a message broker (Redis/Socket.io) or use a dedicated real-time service (Pusher/Ably). |
   468→
   469→### Scaling Priorities
   470→
   471→1. **First bottleneck:** SSE connection memory on Express server. Each connection holds a `res` object. With 1000 concurrent users, ~1-2MB of memory. Manageable but monitor.
   472→2. **Second bottleneck:** SSE + MySQL on same server. Consider read replicas for MySQL if SSE broadcasts cause CPU spikes.
   473→
   474→## Sources
   475→
   476→- [vite-plugin-pwa official guide](https://vite-pwa.dev/) -- Service worker registration, build integration
   477→- [i18next react-i18next lazy loading](https://locize.com/blog/react-i18next/) -- Namespace pattern, Suspense requirement
   478→- [i18next-http-backend](https://github.com/i18next/i18next-http-backend) -- Loading translation files from public directory
   479→- [MDN: Using SSE](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events) -- SSE protocol details
   480→- [Vercel PWA deployment](https://vercel.com/docs/concepts/solutions/pwa) -- vite-plugin-pwa + Vercel compatibility notes
   481→
   482→---
   483→*Architecture research for: PWA + Real-time + i18n integration on existing React/Express stack*
   484→*Researched: 2026-03-30*
   485→
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./\.planning\research\STACK.md`

```text
     1→# Stack Research
     2→
     3→**Domain:** PWA + Real-time Notifications + i18n additions for React 18 + Vite 7 project
     4→**Researched:** 2026-03-30
     5→**Confidence:** MEDIUM (npm registry versions confirmed; ecosystem patterns from training data verified against current dates)
     6→
     7→## Recommended Stack
     8→
     9→### Core Technologies
    10→
    11→| Technology | Version | Purpose | Why Recommended |
    12→|------------|---------|---------|-----------------|
    13→| vite-plugin-pwa | 1.2.0 | PWA infrastructure (Service Worker + manifest) | Official Vite plugin; wraps Workbox with zero-config defaults; integrates at build time; compatible with Vite 7 |
    14→| workbox-window | 7.4.0 | PWA client-side utilities | Used by vite-plugin-pwa internally; provides `updateSW`, install prompt, offline ready events |
    15→| firebase | 12.11.0 | FCM push notifications | Industry standard for web push; handles token management, foreground/background messages, and payload delivery |
    16→| @firebase/messaging | (part of firebase) | FCM client SDK | Required for receiving push notifications in the browser |
    17→| i18next | 26.0.2 | Core i18n engine | Powers react-i18next; handles translation loading, interpolation, pluralization, namespaces |
    18→| react-i18next | 17.0.1 | React bindings for i18next | Standard choice for React projects; declarative API, hooks (useTranslation), suspense support |
    19→| express-sse | 1.0.0 | SSE helper for Express backend | Minimal, zero-dependency; simplifies the `WriteStream` + `flush()` pattern for SSE endpoints |
    20→
    21→### Supporting Libraries
    22→
    23→| Library | Version | Purpose | When to Use |
    24→|---------|---------|---------|-------------|
    25→| @vite-pwa/assets-generator | (dev, latest) | Auto-generate PWA icons from a source image | Only if you need to auto-generate icons; otherwise keep manual icons |
    26→
    27→### Development Tools
    28→
    29→| Tool | Purpose | Notes |
    30→|------|---------|-------|
    31→| vite-plugin-pwa | Build-time SW generation | Configure in `vite.config.js`; generates `dev-dist/sw.js` and injects into HTML |
    32→| Workbox via vite-plugin-pwa | Runtime caching strategies | Don't install Workbox separately -- vite-plugin-pwa bundles it; configure strategies via `workbox.runtimeCaching` |
    33→
    34→## Installation
    35→
    36→```bash
    37→# PWA
    38→npm install vite-plugin-pwa workbox-window
    39→
    40→# i18n
    41→npm install i18next react-i18next
    42→
    43→# Push notifications
    44→npm install firebase
    45→
    46→# Backend SSE helper
    47→npm install express-sse
    48→```
    49→
    50→## Alternatives Considered
    51→
    52→| Feature | Recommended | Alternative | When to Use Alternative |
    53→|---------|-------------|-------------|-------------------------|
    54→| PWA tooling | vite-plugin-pwa | Manual Workbox | Only if you need custom SW logic beyond caching strategies; adds significant complexity |
    55→| i18n | react-i18next | lingui | lingui requires a compile step (macro) and is better for large apps with hundreds of translation keys; react-i18next is simpler for 2-language apps |
    56→| i18n | react-i18next | next-intl | next-intl is tightly coupled to Next.js; not suitable for SPA/Vite |
    57→| Push notifications | FCM | Web Push API directly | FCM wraps the Web Push Protocol with token management, which is significantly more work to implement manually |
    58→| Real-time | SSE | WebSocket | Project constraint (PROJECT.md specifies SSE); WebSocket is bidirectional (overkill for server-to-client-only updates) |
    59→| Real-time | SSE | Long polling | SSE is simpler and has lower latency; polling is a last resort |
    60→| SSE on backend | express-sse | Manual response.flush() | express-sse handles the Content-Type, keep-alive, and heartbeat cleanup; manual is error-prone |
    61→
    62→## What NOT to Use
    63→
    64→| Avoid | Why | Use Instead |
    65→|-------|-----|-------------|
    66→| sw-precache or sw-toolbox (standalone) | Deprecated; Workbox replaced both | vite-plugin-pwa (bundles Workbox) |
    67→| react-intl (FormatJS) | Heavy, compile-step required, steeper learning curve | react-i18next |
    68→| Socket.IO | WebSocket wrapper; overkill for unidirectional SSE; adds ~40KB | express-sse |
    69→| polling | High server load, laggy updates, poor UX | SSE (low-latency, server-push) |
    70→| Firebase Messaging older SDK (@firebase/messaging standalone) | Now bundled inside firebase v9+ modular SDK | firebase (modular) |
    71→
    72→## Stack Patterns by Variant
    73→
    74→**If PWA with full offline support (cache-first for assets, network-first for API):**
    75→- Use vite-plugin-pwa with `strategy: 'generateSW'`
    76→- Configure `workbox.runtimeCaching` for API routes
    77→- Integrate workbox-window for update prompts
    78→
    79→**If PWA install prompt only (no offline needed):**
    80→- Use vite-plugin-pwa with minimal manifest and `registerType: 'autoUpdate'`
    81→- Skip custom runtimeCaching unless offline support is added later
    82→
    83→**If SSE for admin notifications only:**
    84→- Single SSE endpoint `/api/notifications/stream`
    85→- Express middleware attaches JWT verification to SSE stream
    86→- Frontend uses `EventSource` with auto-reconnect
    87→- No SSE for public pages (avoids unnecessary server load)
    88→
    89→**If i18n with 2 languages (VI + EN):**
    90→- Use i18next with `lazy: true` on namespaces
    91→- Load only the active language at startup
    92→- Default to Vietnamese, persist choice in localStorage
    93→
    94→## Version Compatibility
    95→
    96→| Package | Compatible With | Notes |
    97→|---------|-----------------|-------|
    98→| vite-plugin-pwa@1.2.0 | vite@7.x, workbox-window@7.x | Verified via npm peer deps; requires Vite 5+ |
    99→| workbox-window@7.4.0 | React 18.x | Browser-only; no React version dependency |
   100→| firebase@12.11.0 | React 18.x, Vite 7.x | Modular SDK (tree-shakeable); ESM + CJS |
   101→| i18next@26.0.2 | React 18.x | Works with all React versions; hooks-based |
   102→| react-i18next@17.0.1 | i18next@26.x | Peer dep is i18next >= 23; verified compatible |
   103→| express-sse@1.0.0 | Express 4.x | Minimal API; no breaking changes expected |
   104→| sonner@2.0.7 | React 18.x | Already in project; no conflict with new additions |
   105→
   106→## Bundle Size Impact
   107→
   108→| Addition | Estimated Added Size | Notes |
   109→|----------|---------------------|-------|
   110→| vite-plugin-pwa + workbox-window | ~15-25KB gzipped | SW runtime + window utilities |
   111→| firebase (FCM only, modular) | ~15-20KB gzipped | Modular import: `import { getMessaging, getToken } from 'firebase/messaging'` |
   112→| i18next + react-i18next | ~10-15KB gzipped | Core i18n engine; locale files loaded async |
   113→| **Total** | **~40-60KB gzipped** | Within the 50KB budget ( PROJECT.md constraint: "khong tang qua 50KB" ) -- note: this is close to the limit; use modular imports and lazy loading strictly |
   114→
   115→## Integration with Existing Stack
   116→
   117→### React 18 + Vite 7
   118→- vite-plugin-pwa adds a Vite plugin that injects Service Worker registration at build time
   119→- No changes to existing React component tree
   120→- `manifest.json` already exists -- vite-plugin-pwa will augment it automatically
   121→
   122→### Tailwind CSS 3.x
   123→- No conflicts; PWA icons and theme colors integrate via CSS variables already defined
   124→
   125→### Framer Motion
   126→- PWA install prompt can use Framer Motion for smooth slide-in animations
   127→- No direct integration needed
   128→
   129→### react-router-dom v6
   130→- SSE `EventSource` should be connected in a root-level component (e.g., `App` or a dedicated `NotificationsProvider`)
   131→- SSE should close on route unmount to avoid memory leaks
   132→
   133→### axios
   134→- SSE is independent of axios
   135→- PWA caching via Workbox can be configured to bypass axios and cache network requests directly via URL patterns
   136→
   137→### sonner
   138→- Push notifications from FCM can trigger sonner toasts when the app is in foreground
   139→- Background push notifications use the browser's native notification system (handled by Firebase SDK)
   140→
   141→### Backend: Express + MySQL on port 3001
   142→- New SSE endpoint: `GET /api/notifications/stream` -- uses express-sse
   143→- SSE does not conflict with existing REST routes
   144→- MySQL schema unchanged; SSE broadcasts when relevant DB events occur (new application, result updated)
   145→
   146→## Sources
   147→
   148→- npm registry -- version numbers confirmed (vite-plugin-pwa 1.2.0, workbox-window 7.4.0, firebase 12.11.0, i18next 26.0.2, react-i18next 17.0.1, express-sse 1.0.0)
   149→- Project context (.planning/PROJECT.md) -- existing stack, constraints, feature list confirmed
   150→- Training data (verified against npm dates) -- ecosystem patterns, library comparisons, integration guidance
   151→- Recommended stack decisions align with PROJECT.md Key Decisions section (PWA with Workbox, SSE over WebSocket, react-i18next, FCM)
   152→
   153→---
   154→*Stack research for: M2 PWA + Real-time + i18n additions*
   155→*Researched: 2026-03-30*
   156→
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./\.planning\research\PITFALLS.md`

```text
     1→# Pitfalls Research
     2→
     3→**Domain:** PWA + Real-time Notifications + i18n on React 18 + Vite 7 + Express.js
     4→**Researched:** 2026-03-30
     5→**Confidence:** MEDIUM-HIGH
     6→
     7→## Critical Pitfalls
     8→
     9→### Pitfall 1: SW Registration Timing -- Assets Load Before Cache Is Ready
    10→
    11→**What goes wrong:**
    12→The SPA loads and renders before the Service Worker finishes installing and activating. Users see a partially cached or uncached app on first visit, causing blank screens, missing assets, or broken layouts.
    13→
    14→**Why it happens:**
    15→`vite-plugin-pwa` registers the SW via a `<script>` injected into `index.html`. If the SW installation takes time (pre-caching a large bundle), the app may mount with `navigator.serviceWorker` still `undefined` or in `installing` state. The app then makes network requests that should be intercepted, but the SW is not yet active.
    16→
    17→**How to avoid:**
    18→1. Use `vite-plugin-pwa`'s `registerType: 'autoUpdate'` (default). This handles the update cycle correctly.
    19→2. Add a loading state during SW registration. Show a minimal shell or splash until the SW is `active`:
    20→
    21→```javascript
    22→// In your app entry, before rendering
    23→navigator.serviceWorker?.register('/sw.js').then(reg => {
    24→  if (reg.active) {
    25→    // SW already active, proceed
    26→    renderApp();
    27→  } else {
    28→    // Wait for activation
    29→    reg.installing?.addEventListener('statechange', (e) => {
    30→      if (e.target?.state === 'active') renderApp();
    31→    });
    32→  }
    33→});
    34→```
    35→
    36→3. Alternatively, use the `waitOnLocalhost` strategy (blocks rendering until SW is ready) if offline-first is critical for first load.
    37→4. Pre-cache the critical path (HTML shell + critical CSS) using Workbox's `ManifestTransformPlugin` to ensure minimal assets load instantly.
    38→
    39→**Warning signs:**
    40→- Lighthouse PWA audit shows "User will not be prompted to install the Web App" on fresh incognito visit
    41→- Console errors like "Cannot read property 'add' of undefined" from SW cache
    42→- Random blank pages on first PWA load, works on refresh
    43→
    44→**Phase to address:**
    45→Phase 1 (PWA infrastructure: SW + caching) -- this is a foundational setup issue.
    46→
    47→---
    48→
    49→### Pitfall 2: Cache Poisoning -- Old SW Serves Stale Assets After Deploy
    50→
    51→**What goes wrong:**
    52→After a new Vercel deployment, the old Service Worker still serves cached versions of JS/CSS bundles. Users run outdated code against a new API, causing runtime errors, broken UI, or data inconsistencies. The app looks broken until users manually unregister the SW.
    53→
    54→**Why it happens:**
    55→The SW caches assets with a long TTL or uses a "cache-first" strategy without a cache-busting mechanism tied to deployment. When Vercel deploys new assets (with new content-hashed filenames), the old SW's cache still holds the old assets. The browser serves the old SW because it was registered once and persists across deployments.
    56→
    57→**How to avoid:**
    58→1. **Always use content-hashed filenames** (Vite does this by default for production builds). The SW cache key should be the URL itself, so new hashes = new cache entries.
    59→2. Configure `vite-plugin-pwa` with `manifest: { publicExposed: [...] }` and use Workbox's `CacheExpirationPlugin` with a reasonable `maxAgeSeconds` (e.g., 1 day for assets, 1 week for fonts).
    60→3. Implement a "new version available" UX pattern: listen for `reg.update()` and prompt the user to reload when a new SW is waiting:
    61→
    62→```javascript
    63→// In SW registration
    64→navigator.serviceWorker?.register('/sw.js').then(reg => {
    65→  reg.addEventListener('updatefound', () => {
    66→    const newWorker = reg.installing;
    67→    newWorker?.addEventListener('statechange', () => {
    68→      if (newWorker.state === 'installed' && navigator.serviceWorker.controller) {
    69→        // New SW available, prompt user
    70→        showUpdatePrompt();
    71→      }
    72→    });
    73→  });
    74→});
    75→```
    76→
    77→4. For critical data (admission results), prefer **network-first** for API calls, not cache-first. This prevents stale data from being served as "truth."
    78→
    79→**Warning signs:**
    80→- Users reporting "the app looks different from what I submitted yesterday"
    81→- API responses don't match what the UI renders
    82→- Browser DevTools shows cached responses with old `content-length` on new deployments
    83→
    84→**Phase to address:**
    85→Phase 1 (PWA caching strategies) -- must be configured before going live.
    86→
    87→---
    88→
    89→### Pitfall 3: Vercel Edge Caching Overrides Service Worker Caching Headers
    90→
    91→**What goes wrong:**
    92→Vercel's CDN (edge network) caches responses at the edge level before they reach the browser or SW. Even with correct SW caching strategies, users get stale data cached by Vercel, and the SW never gets a chance to intercept or update the response.
    93→
    94→**Why it happens:**
    95→Vercel automatically caches static assets at the edge. By default, `Cache-Control` headers on your server responses may tell Vercel to cache for hours or days. The SW sits between the browser and the network, but Vercel's edge sits above the SW. If an API response is cached at the edge, the SW never sees a fresh request.
    96→
    97→**How to avoid:**
    98→1. Set `Cache-Control: public, max-age=0, must-revalidate` on API endpoints in Express. This tells Vercel not to cache at the edge:
    99→
   100→```javascript
   101→// In Express routes for API endpoints
   102→app.use('/api', (req, res, next) => {
   103→  res.set('Cache-Control', 'no-store');
   104→  next();
   105→});
   106→```
   107→
   108→2. For Vercel, use `vercel.json` to configure caching headers per route:
   109→
   110→```json
   111→{
   112→  "headers": [
   113→    {
   114→      "source": "/api/(.*)",
   115→      "headers": [{ "key": "Cache-Control", "value": "no-store" }]
   116→    },
   117→    {
   118→      "source": "/assets/(.*)",
   119→      "headers": [{ "key": "Cache-Control", "value": "public, max-age=31536000, immutable" }]
   120→    }
   121→  ]
   122→}
   123→```
   124→
   125→3. Static assets (JS/CSS/images) CAN be cached aggressively at the edge since they have content hashes. But HTML files should have short or no edge caching to allow SW updates to propagate quickly.
   126→4. For the SSE endpoint, explicitly disable all caching: `Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate`.
   127→
   128→**Warning signs:**
   129→- SSE events not reaching the browser (edge caching the connection)
   130→- API data doesn't update even after clearing browser cache
   131→- Lighthouse shows "Page load is not fast enough" despite optimized bundles
   132→
   133→**Phase to address:**
   134→Phase 1 (PWA infrastructure) combined with SSE endpoint setup. This is a cross-cutting concern.
   135→
   136→---
   137→
   138→### Pitfall 4: SSE Connection Never Closes on Browser Tab Close / Navigates Away
   139→
   140→**What goes wrong:**
   141→When a user navigates to another page or closes the tab, the `EventSource` connection remains open on the server. Over time, this accumulates open connections on the Express server, eventually exhausting server resources or hitting connection limits. The server continues sending events to dead connections.
   142→
   143→**Why it happens:**
   144→`EventSource` does not have a built-in mechanism to send a "close" signal to the server when the browser tab is closed. The TCP connection may stay in `TIME_WAIT` state briefly, but Express event emitters are not cleaned up. Each page navigation (e.g., going from `/` to `/result`) creates a new SSE connection without closing the old one, especially with React Router's client-side navigation.
   145→
   146→**How to avoid:**
   147→1. **Always close the EventSource in `useEffect` cleanup**:
   148→
   149→```javascript
   150→useEffect(() => {
   151→  const eventSource = new EventSource('/api/sse/notifications');
   152→
   153→  // Handle messages
   154→  eventSource.onmessage = (event) => { /* ... */ };
   155→
   156→  // Cleanup on unmount
   157→  return () => {
   158→    eventSource.close();
   159→  };
   160→}, []);
   161→```
   162→
   163→2. **Handle `beforeunload` for full page navigation**:
   164→
   165→```javascript
   166→useEffect(() => {
   167→  const eventSource = new EventSource('/api/sse/notifications');
   168→
   169→  const handleUnload = () => {
   170→    eventSource.close();
   171→    // Send a final message to the server so it can clean up
   172→    if (navigator.sendBeacon) {
   173→      navigator.sendBeacon('/api/sse/close');
   174→    }
   175→  };
   176→
   177→  window.addEventListener('beforeunload', handleUnload);
   178→  return () => {
   179→    eventSource.close();
   180→    window.removeEventListener('beforeunload', handleUnload);
   181→  };
   182→}, []);
   183→```
   184→
   185→3. **Server-side: detect closed connections**. In Express, use `req.on('close')` to detect when the client disconnects:
   186→
   187→```javascript
   188→app.get('/api/sse/notifications', (req, res) => {
   189→  res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'text/event-stream');
   190→  res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'no-cache');
   191→  res.setHeader('Connection', 'keep-alive');
   192→  res.flushHeaders();
   193→
   194→  const clientId = req.query.userId;
   195→
   196→  const sendUpdate = (data) => {
   197→    if (res.writableEnded) return; // Client disconnected
   198→    res.write(`data: ${JSON.stringify(data)}\n\n`);
   199→  };
   200→
   201→  // Register client
   202→  clientRegistry.add(clientId, sendUpdate);
   203→
   204→  // Clean up on close
   205→  req.on('close', () => {
   206→    clientRegistry.remove(clientId);
   207→  });
   208→});
   209→```
   210→
   211→4. **Set a `keep-alive` heartbeat** so intermediate proxies (nginx, load balancers) do not close the connection due to inactivity:
   212→
   213→```javascript
   214→// Server: send a comment line every 30 seconds
   215→const heartbeat = setInterval(() => {
   216→  if (!res.writableEnded) {
   217→    res.write(': heartbeat\n\n');
   218→  }
   219→}, 30000);
   220→req.on('close', () => clearInterval(heartbeat));
   221→```
   222→
   223→5. **React Router navigation**: Each route change that mounts an SSE component creates a new connection. Design SSE management as a global singleton (React context or a dedicated hook that lives at the app root level), not per-page. All pages share one SSE connection.
   224→
   225→**Warning signs:**
   226→- Express server `netstat` shows hundreds of open connections
   227→- Server memory grows linearly over time
   228→- SSE events are delayed or batched unexpectedly
   229→- `Error: Hub connection already exists` or similar from duplicate connection attempts
   230→
   231→**Phase to address:**
   232→Phase 2 (SSE endpoint + notification UI) -- this must be built correctly from day one or it will silently accumulate.
   233→
   234→---
   235→
   236→### Pitfall 5: No Exponential Backoff on SSE Reconnection
   237→
   238→**What goes wrong:**
   239→When the SSE connection drops (network blip, server restart), the `EventSource` automatically reconnects with a short delay. On repeated failures, the browser reconnects rapidly, flooding the server with connection requests and making the outage worse. The server may ban the client IP, or the browser tab becomes unresponsive.
   240→
   241→**Why it happens:**
   242→`EventSource` has built-in reconnection, but it uses a short delay that decreases on each attempt (standard behavior). After a server outage, all connected clients reconnect simultaneously, creating a "thundering herd" that overwhelms the recovering server.
   243→
   244→**How to avoid:**
   245→1. **Do not rely on EventSource's default reconnection** for critical notification flows. Implement manual reconnection with exponential backoff:
   246→
   247→```javascript
   248→function createSSEWithBackoff(url, options = {}) {
   249→  const { maxRetries = 10, baseDelay = 1000 } = options;
   250→  let retryCount = 0;
   251→  let eventSource = null;
   252→  let timeoutId = null;
   253→
   254→  function connect() {
   255→    eventSource = new EventSource(url);
   256→
   257→    eventSource.onopen = () => {
   258→      retryCount = 0;
   259→    };
   260→
   261→    eventSource.onerror = () => {
   262→      eventSource.close();
   263→      if (retryCount < maxRetries) {
   264→        const delay = Math.min(baseDelay * Math.pow(2, retryCount), 30000);
   265→        retryCount++;
   266→        timeoutId = setTimeout(connect, delay);
   267→      }
   268→    };
   269→
   270→    return eventSource;
   271→  }
   272→
   273→  return {
   274→    open: connect,
   275→    close: () => {
   276→      if (timeoutId) clearTimeout(timeoutId);
   277→      if (eventSource) eventSource.close();
   278→    }
   279→  };
   280→}
   281→```
   282→
   283→2. **Track connection state** in React with a dedicated hook. Show UI feedback (e.g., "Reconnecting..." badge) so users know the system is recovering.
   284→3. **Use `navigator.onLine` and `offline`/`online` events** to pause reconnection attempts when the device is genuinely offline, and resume when back online.
   285→4. Consider a maximum delay cap (e.g., 30 seconds) so users do not wait forever for reconnection.
   286→
   287→**Warning signs:**
   288→- Server logs show sudden spike in connection attempts after restart
   289→- Browser tab CPU usage spikes during network instability
   290→- "Too many connections" errors from Express or Vercel serverless functions
   291→
   292→**Phase to address:**
   293→Phase 2 (SSE endpoint + notification UI) -- must be implemented alongside the SSE hook.
   294→
   295→---
   296→
   297→### Pitfall 6: i18n Translation Keys Missing in Production (Ghost Strings)
   298→
   299→**What goes wrong:**
   300→After deployment, some UI strings appear as blank spaces, untranslated keys (e.g., `[common.welcome_message]`), or fallback language strings instead of the correct translation. This happens because translation keys were added after the last locale file was built, or keys were extracted from source but never added to translation files.
   301→
   302→**Why it happens:**
   303→1. `i18next-scanner` (or `vite-plugin-i18next`) runs at build time to extract keys. If a developer adds a new `t('key')` call after the last scan, it is missing from the built locale files.
   304→2. In development, `fallbackLng: 'vi'` kicks in and shows the Vietnamese string (or a placeholder), hiding the problem until production.
   305→3. Lazy-loaded locale files may not include all keys if the extraction only runs on the initially-loaded bundle.
   306→
   307→**How to avoid:**
   308→1. **Fail the build if missing keys are detected**: configure `i18next-scanner` with ` interpolation: { escapeValue: false }` and a `漠视` (ignore) list, then run a pre-production check:
   309→
   310→```javascript
   311→// In your i18n config
   312→i18next.use(Backend).init({
   313→  fallbackLng: 'vi',
   314→  debug: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development',
   315→  missingKeyHandler: (lng, ns, key, fallbackValue) => {
   316→    if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production') {
   317→      console.error(`MISSING KEY: ${ns}:${key}`);
   318→      // Or: throw new Error(`Missing translation: ${key}`);
   319→    }
   320→  },
   321→});
   322→```
   323→
   324→2. **Use `react-i18next`'s `useTranslation` with explicit namespace support** for lazy-loaded namespaces. Do not mix default namespace usage across the app without a clear convention.
   325→3. **Add a CI/CD check** that runs `i18next` extraction and compares against committed locale files, failing if new keys are detected without corresponding translations.
   326→4. **Use descriptive key names** rather than auto-generated hashes. Keys like `admission.form.step3.label.name` are self-documenting and easier to track than UUIDs.
   327→
   328→**Warning signs:**
   329→- Build logs show "missing key" warnings during development (these are the warning signs to catch)
   330→- Lighthouse accessibility audit flags empty `aria-label` or `alt` attributes on translated images
   331→- QA reports "some text is in English" on the Vietnamese site
   332→
   333→**Phase to address:**
   334→Phase 3 (i18n infrastructure) -- tooling must be set up correctly before any translation work begins.
   335→
   336→---
   337→
   338→### Pitfall 7: RTL Layout Breaks When Adding Arabic/Hebrew Support
   339→
   340→**What goes wrong:**
   341→The entire layout flips incorrectly when an RTL language is added. CSS properties that work for LTR (like `margin-left`, `padding-right`) remain directionally tied to the original LTR layout. The sidebar appears on the wrong side, text alignment is wrong, icons that indicate direction (arrows, chevrons) are not flipped, and the language switcher itself may disappear or be inaccessible.
   342→
   343→**Why it happens:**
   344→1. `react-i18next` does not handle RTL -- it only provides translated strings. Direction must be set on the `html` element.
   345→2. CSS that uses absolute directional values (`margin-left: 16px`) instead of logical properties (`margin-inline-start: 16px`) does not flip in RTL.
   346→3. Tailwind CSS 3.x defaults to LTR physical properties. The project needs explicit RTL-aware classes (`rtl:ml-4`, `ltr:ml-4`) or must migrate to logical properties.
   347→
   348→**How to avoid:**
   349→1. **Set direction on the document root** whenever the language changes:
   350→
   351→```javascript
   352→// In your language switcher handler
   353→import i18n from './i18n';
   354→
   355→const changeLanguage = (lng) => {
   356→  i18n.changeLanguage(lng);
   357→  document.documentElement.dir = lng === 'ar' || lng === 'he' ? 'rtl' : 'ltr';
   358→  document.documentElement.lang = lng;
   359→};
   360→```
   361→
   362→2. **Migrate CSS to logical properties** (Tailwind supports these natively):
   363→
   364→| Physical (LTR-only) | Logical (RTL-aware) |
   365→|---------------------|---------------------|
   366→| `ml-4`, `mr-4` | `ms-4`, `me-4` |
   367→| `pl-4`, `pr-4` | `ps-4`, `pe-4` |
   368→| `text-left`, `text-right` | `text-start`, `text-end` |
   369→| `float-left`, `float-right` | `float-start`, `float-end` |
   370→
   371→3. **Audit icons and visual indicators**: Use `react-icons` icons that are symmetric or explicitly handle direction. Arrows like `FaChevronRight` should become `FaChevronLeft` for RTL -- or better, use a component that auto-flips with CSS `transform: scaleX(-1)` in RTL context.
   372→4. **Test RTL early** -- do not design everything in LTR and plan to "fix RTL later." The project only needs Vietnamese and English (both LTR), so this is **low risk for M2**, but if any RTL language is ever considered, set up the direction infrastructure now.
   373→
   374→**Warning signs:**
   375→- Sidebar or navigation items on the wrong side after language change
   376→- Arrow icons pointing the wrong direction
   377→- Scroll bars on the wrong side
   378→
   379→**Phase to address:**
   380→Phase 3 (i18n + language switcher) -- if RTL languages are ever planned. For current scope (VI + EN), this is a low-priority watch item.
   381→
   382→---
   383→
   384→### Pitfall 8: SEO Meta Tags Are Not Translated and No hreflang Exists
   385→
   386→**What goes wrong:**
   387→Search engines index the Vietnamese version of page titles and meta descriptions for all language versions. English-speaking users searching in English do not find the English version of the site. Google may penalize for duplicate content across language variants if hreflang is missing.
   388→
   389→**Why it happens:**
   390→1. `react-i18next` only translates strings rendered in the DOM. `<title>` and `<meta>` tags in the document `<head>` are not part of the React component tree by default and do not respond to `t()` calls.
   391→2. Without `<link rel="alternate" hreflang="...">` tags, Google cannot associate the language variants of the same page, leading to incorrect indexing.
   392→
   393→**How to avoid:**
   394→1. **Use `react-helmet-async` or a similar library** to manage document head tags from React components:
   395→
   396→```javascript
   397→import { Helmet } from 'react-helmet-async';
   398→
   399→function AdmissionPage() {
   400→  const { t } = useTranslation();
   401→
   402→  return (
   403→    <>
   404→      <Helmet>
   405→        <title>{t('seo.admission.title')}</title>
   406→        <meta name="description" content={t('seo.admission.description')} />
   407→        <link rel="alternate" hreflang="vi" href="https://hutech.edu.vn/tuyen-sinh" />
   408→        <link rel="alternate" hreflang="en" href="https://hutech.edu.vn/en/admissions" />
   409→        <link rel="alternate" hreflang="x-default" href="https://hutech.edu.vn/tuyen-sinh" />
   410→      </Helmet>
   411→      {/* Page content */}
   412→    </>
   413→  );
   414→}
   415→```
   416→
   417→2. **Update `react-helmet-async`'s `Provider`** in your app root (`main.jsx`).
   418→3. **For SEO in a SPA on Vercel**, note that crawlers may not execute JavaScript to render meta tags. Either use Vercel's server-side rendering (SSR) mode, or ensure meta tags are present in the initial HTML shell (can be injected via `vite-plugin-pwa`'s `base` configuration or a `template.html` modification).
   419→4. **Add `hreflang`** for all language variants. For a SPA with client-side routing, this typically means one `<link>` tag per language variant.
   420→
   421→**Warning signs:**
   422→- Google Search Console shows "Sitemaps contain URLs noindex" or missing hreflang
   423→- English pages still show Vietnamese titles in Google search results
   424→- Lighthouse SEO score drops after i18n is added
   425→
   426→**Phase to address:**
   427→Phase 3 (i18n infrastructure) -- SEO meta tags should be part of the i18n scaffold, not bolted on later.
   428→
   429→---
   430→
   431→## Technical Debt Patterns
   432→
   433→| Shortcut | Immediate Benefit | Long-term Cost | When Acceptable |
   434→|----------|-------------------|----------------|-----------------|
   435→| Using cache-first for ALL resources (including API) | Faster perceived performance | Users see stale data; cache poisoning | Only for truly static assets (images, fonts, CSS with content hashes) |
   436→| One global SSE connection shared by all components | Simple to implement | Components tightly coupled; hard to debug which component triggered reconnect | Only if SSE is managed at app root with a context |
   437→| Hardcoding locale keys as string literals `t('home.title')` | No setup needed | Translation files get out of sync; no extraction tooling | Never for production |
   438→| Skipping `beforeunload` cleanup for SSE | Saves a few lines of code | Connection leaks on navigation; server resource exhaustion | Never |
   439→| Using `axios` interceptors for SSE connection setup | Reuses existing HTTP patterns | SSE is not HTTP; mixing patterns creates confusion | Never -- SSE needs its own connection management |
   440→| Lazy-loading locale files without a fallback namespace | Smaller initial bundle | Flash of untranslated content on route change | Only with preloading of the default namespace |
   441→
   442→---
   443→
   444→## Integration Gotchas
   445→
   446→| Integration | Common Mistake | Correct Approach |
   447→|-------------|----------------|------------------|
   448→| FCM (Firebase Cloud Messaging) | Using a separate SW for FCM that conflicts with the main vite-plugin-pwa SW | Use a single SW that handles both caching (Workbox) and FCM. Register FCM messaging in the same SW file. `importScripts` for Firebase SDK inside the SW. |
   449→| FCM + Vercel | Vercel edge functions cannot run long-running background processes like SWs | FCM push notifications require a standard Node.js server, not Vercel edge. The SSE endpoint and FCM token management should run on the Express backend (port 3001), not on Vercel. |
   450→| vite-plugin-pwa + Vercel SSR | Enabling SSR mode breaks SW registration | vite-plugin-pwa requires a static SPA build. If using Vercel SSR mode, keep PWA only on the static preview deployment, not the SSR version. |
   451→| SSE + Vercel serverless | Serverless functions have execution time limits (10s on hobby, 60s on pro) | SSE connections timeout on serverless. The SSE endpoint MUST run on the Express backend server (port 3001), not Vercel functions. Vercel is for static hosting only. |
   452→| axios + i18n | Setting `Accept-Language` header per-request manually | Set axios default headers globally once on app init: `axios.defaults.headers.common['Accept-Language'] = i18n.language;` |
   453→| sonner toasts + i18n | Using hardcoded strings in toast messages | Use `useTranslation` inside the toast or pass translated strings: `toast(t('notification.success'))` |
   454→
   455→---
   456→
   457→## Performance Traps
   458→
   459→| Trap | Symptoms | Prevention | When It Breaks |
   460→|------|----------|------------|----------------|
   461→| Pre-caching all assets on first load | 3-5 second delay on first PWA visit, users think the app is broken | Use "增量缓存" -- only pre-cache the app shell. Lazy-cache remaining assets on first access. Configure `vite-plugin-pwa` `strategy: 'generateSW'` with `navigateFallback: '/index.html'` and let runtime caching handle non-critical assets. | On slow 3G connections or devices with limited storage |
   462→| SSE event buffer accumulation | Memory grows as unread events pile up if tab is backgrounded | Implement a max buffer size. If the buffer exceeds N events, drop oldest events. On reconnect, fetch the current state via REST API, not rely on replaying missed events. | After several hours of background tab with no focus |
   463→| Loading all locale namespaces at once | Initial bundle includes all translations (VI + EN), defeating lazy loading | Split locale files by route/page namespace. Load only the namespace needed for the current route. The `common` namespace (buttons, labels) should be in the initial load; page-specific namespaces are lazy. | Every page load carries the full translation payload |
   464→| Too many SSE event handlers | Each notification component registers its own `onmessage` handler, creating competing handlers | Use a single event bus (e.g., a custom `NotificationContext`) that all components subscribe to. One EventSource, many listeners. | After 10+ notification types across admin + user views |
   465→
   466→---
   467→
   468→## Security Mistakes
   469→
   470→| Mistake | Risk | Prevention |
   471→|---------|------|------------|
   472→| SSE endpoint not validating JWT | Any user can connect to any user's SSE stream and see private admission results | Validate JWT from query param or cookie on every SSE connection. Reject with 401 if token is invalid or expired. |
   473→| SSE user ID from query param without ownership check | User A sends `?userId=B` and sees User B's notifications | On the server, extract the user ID from the verified JWT, NOT from the query parameter. Ignore any `userId` in the query string. |
   474→| PWA manifest with `theme_color` that does not match meta theme-color | Browser may show incorrect color in the address bar or splash screen | Ensure `<meta name="theme-color">` in `index.html` matches `theme_color` in manifest.json. vite-plugin-pwa can inject this automatically. |
   475→| Firebase VAPID key exposed in client-side code | Anyone can send push notifications to your FCM topic | VAPID key is intentionally public (identifies your app). However, ensure FCM server key / service account credentials are NEVER in client code. Store them in environment variables on the Express backend only. |
   476→| No CSP headers for Service Worker | XSS within SW cache poisoning | Add Content-Security-Policy headers that restrict `script-src 'self'` and do not allow inline scripts in the SW scope. |
   477→
   478→---
   479→
   480→## UX Pitfalls
   481→
   482→| Pitfall | User Impact | Better Approach |
   483→|---------|-------------|-----------------|
   484→| Install prompt shown on first visit immediately | Users do not know what the app is yet; low install conversion and annoyance | Use the "delayed prompt" pattern: track installation eligibility, show the prompt after the user has engaged meaningfully (e.g., after 2 page visits or 30 seconds on site). Use `@vite-pwa/sveltekit` deferred prompt approach. |
   485→| Notification permission requested immediately on page load | Users deny permission because they do not understand the value; cannot re-prompt | Wait until the user performs a relevant action (e.g., submits an admission form). Show an explanation card first: "Get notified when your admission result is ready." |
   486→| Language switcher loses page context | Switching language resets the user to the home page, losing their place | Preserve the current route + scroll position. Use `react-i18next`'s language detection with `lookupLocalStorage` and ensure the router navigates to the equivalent route in the new language. |
   487→| Toast notifications for every SSE event | Notification spam; users mute or ignore toasts | Implement a notification inbox model: queue events silently, show a bell icon badge. A toast appears only for high-priority events (e.g., "Your admission result is ready"). |
   488→| No offline indicator | User fills out a long form while offline, submits, and the request appears to succeed but never reaches the server | Use the SW to detect online/offline state. Show a persistent banner: "You are offline. Your submission will be sent when connection is restored." Queue the request in IndexedDB. |
   489→
   490→---
   491→
   492→## "Looks Done But Isn't" Checklist
   493→
   494→- [ ] **PWA Service Worker:** Often missing the `skipWaiting()` + `clients.claim()` flow in the SW source -- verify the generated SW in `dist/` contains these calls.
   495→- [ ] **PWA Offline:** Often the offline fallback page loads but API calls still fail silently -- verify API calls are handled with a network-first strategy that degrades gracefully.
   496→- [ ] **SSE Reconnection:** Often the reconnection works in dev but fails in production behind a corporate proxy -- test reconnection behind a VPN or corporate firewall.
   497→- [ ] **SSE Cleanup:** Often the SSE connection is closed on component unmount but not on route change within React Router -- verify React Router navigation does not leak connections.
   498→- [ ] **i18n Translation Files:** Often locale files are built once and never updated when new keys are added -- verify CI/CD fails if new keys are detected without translations.
   499→- [ ] **i18n SEO:** Often meta tags are translated but hreflang is missing -- verify all pages have correct `<link rel="alternate" hreflang>` tags.
   500→- [ ] **FCM Push Notifications:** Often the SW registration for FCM conflicts with the main caching SW -- verify only one SW file exists and it handles both.
   501→- [ ] **Manifest Icons:** Often only 192x192 and 512x512 icons are provided, causing the "Add to Home Screen" to use upscaled blurry icons -- verify icons exist at all required sizes listed in the manifest.
   502→- [ ] **Bundle Size:** Often adding PWA (Workbox runtime) + i18n (react-i18next + locale files) exceeds the 50KB constraint -- verify the production bundle size with `vite build --mode production` and check the chunk sizes.
   503→
   504→---
   505→
   506→## Recovery Strategies
   507→
   508→| Pitfall | Recovery Cost | Recovery Steps |
   509→|---------|---------------|----------------|
   510→| SW serves stale assets | MEDIUM | 1) Push a new SW version with `skipWaiting()` forcing activation. 2) If Vercel edge cache is the issue, purge the Vercel cache via dashboard or `vercel purge-url`. 3) Set cache-control headers correctly and redeploy. |
   511→| SSE connection leak (server) | LOW-MEDIUM | 1) Check `req.on('close')` handler is firing. 2) Implement a heartbeat to detect dead connections. 3) Restart Express server to clear stuck connections. 4) Set up connection count monitoring. |
   512→| Missing translation keys in prod | MEDIUM | 1) Add missing keys to locale files. 2) If hotfix: add keys directly to locale files (not source code). 3) Rebuild. 4) Add the key extraction to CI to prevent recurrence. |
   513→| Cache poisoning (old data served) | HIGH | 1) Identify the cache strategy that caused it. 2) Clear the affected cache (browser DevTools for users, Vercel CDN purge for all users). 3) Change the cache key strategy (add content hash or query param). 4) Force SW update with `self.skipWaiting()`. |
   514→| FCM SW conflicts with Workbox SW | MEDIUM | 1) Consolidate into a single SW. 2) Use Workbox's `registerRoute` for caching, inject Firebase SDK via `importScripts` within the same SW. 3) Register the unified SW in `main.jsx`. |
   515→
   516→---
   517→
   518→## Pitfall-to-Phase Mapping
   519→
   520→| Pitfall | Prevention Phase | Verification |
   521→|---------|------------------|--------------|
   522→| SW registration timing | Phase 1: PWA infrastructure | Load app in incognito, check DevTools SW status = "activated and running" before app renders |
   523→| Cache poisoning after deploy | Phase 1: PWA caching strategies | Deploy new version, verify assets have new content hashes, old SW cannot serve them |
   524→| Vercel edge caching overrides SW | Phase 1: PWA infrastructure | Check Vercel network tab for `x-vercel-cache` headers on API responses |
   525→| SSE connection never closes | Phase 2: SSE endpoint | Navigate between pages, check `netstat` on server or Vercel function logs for open connections |
   526→| SSE no exponential backoff | Phase 2: SSE endpoint | Kill the Express server, observe reconnect behavior -- should not flood |
   527→| Missing translation keys | Phase 3: i18n infrastructure | Run `npm run i18n-extract`, verify no new keys appear without translations |
   528→| RTL layout breaks | Phase 3: i18n + language switcher | Only relevant if RTL languages are added |
   529→| SEO meta tags not translated | Phase 3: i18n + language switcher | Inspect page `<head>` in browser, verify `<title>` and `<meta>` change with language |
   530→| FCM + PWA SW conflict | Phase 1: PWA + FCM integration | Register SW once, verify both caching (Workbox) and FCM messaging work together |
   531→| Bundle size exceeds 50KB | All phases | Run `vite build`, check gzip sizes of main chunks before committing |
   532→
   533→---
   534→
   535→## Sources
   536→
   537→- [vite-plugin-pwa official documentation](https://vite-pwa-org.netlify.app/) -- SW registration, caching strategies, manifest configuration (MEDIUM confidence)
   538→- [Workbox documentation](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/workbox/) -- caching strategies, cache expiration, SW best practices (MEDIUM confidence)
   539→- [react-i18next official documentation](https://react.i18next.com/) -- namespace lazy loading, language detection, interpolation (MEDIUM confidence)
   540→- [MDN: Using Server-Sent Events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events) -- EventSource lifecycle, reconnection behavior (HIGH confidence)
   541→- [Google Web Dev: PWA Checklist](https://web.dev/pwa-checklist/) -- install prompt timing, offline behavior, manifest requirements (MEDIUM confidence)
   542→- [Firebase Cloud Messaging Web Documentation](https://firebase.google.com/docs/cloud-messaging/js/client) -- FCM SW registration, VAPID keys (MEDIUM confidence)
   543→- [Vercel: Configuring Caching Headers](https://vercel.com/docs/concepts/edge-network/caching) -- cache-control headers per route on Vercel (MEDIUM confidence)
   544→- [Stack Overflow / community discussions: SSE + Express connection cleanup](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/59727602/how-to-cleanup-express-sse-connections-when-client-disconnect) -- req.on('close') pattern for connection cleanup (LOW-MEDIUM confidence, community source)
   545→
   546→---
   547→
   548→*Pitfalls research for: PWA + Real-time Notifications + i18n on React 18 + Vite 7 + Express.js*
   549→*Researched: 2026-03-30*
   550→
```
</details></tool-use>

<downstream_consumer>
Output consumed by /gsd:execute-phase. Plans need:
- Frontmatter (wave, depends_on, files_modified, autonomous)
- Tasks in XML format with read_first and acceptance_criteria fields (MANDATORY on every task)
- Verification criteria
- must_haves for goal-backward verification
</downstream_consumer>

<deep_work_rules>
## Anti-Shallow Execution Rules (MANDATORY)

Every task MUST include these fields — they are NOT optional:

1. **`<read_first>`** — Files the executor MUST read before touching anything. Always include:
   - The file being modified (so executor sees current state, not assumptions)
   - Any "source of truth" file referenced in CONTEXT.md (reference implementations, existing patterns, config files, schemas)
   - Any file whose patterns, signatures, types, or conventions must be replicated or respected

2. **`<acceptance_criteria>`** — Verifiable conditions that prove the task was done correctly. Rules:
   - Every criterion must be checkable with grep, file read, test command, or CLI output
   - NEVER use subjective language ("looks correct", "properly configured", "consistent with")
   - ALWAYS include exact strings, patterns, values, or command outputs that must be present
   - Examples:
     - Code: `auth.py contains def verify_token(` / `test_auth.py exits 0`
     - Config: `.env.example contains DATABASE_URL=` / `Dockerfile contains HEALTHCHECK`
     - Docs: `README.md contains '## Installation'` / `API.md lists all endpoints`
     - Infra: `deploy.yml has rollback step` / `docker-compose.yml has healthcheck for db`

3. **`<action>`** — Must include CONCRETE values, not references. Rules:
   - NEVER say "align X with Y", "match X to Y", "update to be consistent" without specifying the exact target state
   - ALWAYS include the actual values: config keys, function signatures, SQL statements, class names, import paths, env vars, etc.
   - If CONTEXT.md has a comparison table or expected values, copy them into the action verbatim
   - The executor should be able to complete the task from the action text alone, without needing to read CONTEXT.md or reference files (read_first is for verification, not discovery)

**Why this matters:** Executor agents work from the plan text. Vague instructions like "update the config to match production" produce shallow one-line changes. Concrete instructions like "add DATABASE_URL=postgresql://... , set POOL_SIZE=20, add REDIS_URL=redis://..." produce complete work. The cost of verbose plans is far less than the cost of re-doing shallow execution.
</deep_work_rules>

<quality_gate>
- [ ] PLAN.md files created in phase directory
- [ ] Each plan has valid frontmatter
- [ ] Tasks are specific and actionable
- [ ] Every task has `<read_first>` with at least the file being modified
- [ ] Every task has `<acceptance_criteria>` with grep-verifiable conditions
- [ ] Every `<action>` contains concrete values (no "align X with Y" without specifying what)
- [ ] Dependencies correctly identified
- [ ] Waves assigned for parallel execution
- [ ] must_haves derived from phase goal
</quality_gate>

## Phase 8: Real-time + Admin Notifications

### What This Phase Delivers

**RT-01**: Express backend has SSE endpoint with heartbeat 30s, client reconnection logic with exponential backoff
**RT-02**: Admin sees notification bell icon in header with badge counter, popup dropdown showing list of new notifications

### Context

The project has:
- Express backend at `DoAnTuyenSinh/backend/index.js` (port 3001)
- JWT auth middleware (`authenticateToken`)
- React frontend at `DoAnTuyenSinh/src/`
- UserContext at `DoAnTuyenSinh/src/contexts/UserContext.jsx` (has `user` with `id`, `role`)
- Header at `DoAnTuyenSinh/src/components/ThanhHeader.jsx`
- Existing CORS: `localhost:5173` and `do-an-tuyen-sinh.vercel.app`
- PWA Service Worker already built (Phase 7)

### SSE Architecture

The SSE endpoint must:
1. Be a GET endpoint at `/api/sse/events`
2. Validate JWT from `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header
3. Send `Content-Type: text/event-stream` with `Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store`
4. Keep connection alive with 30s heartbeat comments
5. Register client in a service that maps userId → Set of response objects
6. Clean up on `req.on('close')`
7. Broadcast events: `new_application` (admin), `status_update` (candidate)

### NotificationBell UI

- Bell icon with badge counter (number of unread notifications)
- Click opens dropdown panel showing notification list
- Each notification has: icon, title, message, timestamp
- Admin-only (check `user.role === 'admin'`)

### Plans to create:

**Plan 1 (Backend):** Create SSE service + Express SSE endpoint + trigger broadcasts
- Create `backend/services/sseService.js` (client registry with add/remove/broadcast/broadcastAll)
- Add `/api/sse/events` endpoint in `backend/index.js`
- Trigger `broadcast` from existing routes when data changes (new application → admin, status update → candidate)
- Verify with curl or test

**Plan 2 (Frontend):** Create NotificationContext + NotificationBell + integrate
- Create `src/contexts/NotificationContext.jsx` (SSE connection lifecycle, unread count, event handlers)
- Create `src/components/NotificationBell.jsx` (bell icon, badge, dropdown)
- Add `NotificationBell` to `ThanhHeader.jsx` (admin only)
- Wrap App content with `NotificationProvider` in `App.jsx`
- Test: SSE connection, badge updates, dropdown rendering

Write the PLAN.md to `.planning/phases/08-real-time-admin-notifications/8-PLAN.md`

    77→- i18n: react-i18next with lazy loading confirmed
    78→- Key pitfalls: SSE connection leaks, cache poisoning, bundle size (50KB budget)
    79→
    80→Full research: .planning/research/SUMMARY.md
    81→
    82→## Accumulated Context
    83→
    84→### Decisions
    85→
    86→| Phase | Decision | Rationale |
    87→|-------|----------|-----------|
    88→| M2    | vite-plugin-pwa + Workbox for PWA | Standard Google-maintained solution with multiple caching strategies |
    89→| M2    | SSE over WebSocket | Unidirectional updates only; simpler, ~30KB smaller bundle |
    90→| M2    | react-i18next with lazy loading | Most popular React i18n lib; lazy namespaces required to stay under 50KB budget |
    91→| M2    | SSE targets Express port 3001, not Vercel | Vercel serverless functions do not support SSE streaming |
    92→| M2    | Deferred install prompt (not auto) | Chrome penalizes aggressive auto-prompting |
    93→| M2    | PWA-04 (FCM push) deferred | Requires Firebase project setup, deferred to v2 |
    94→
    95→### Blockers/Concerns
    96→
    97→- **SSE CORS on Vercel:** Need to verify existing CORS config (localhost:5173 + do-an-tuyen-sinh.vercel.app) works for SSE streaming, not just REST responses. Flagged in research.
    98→- **Bundle size:** PWA (~15-25KB) + i18n (~10-15KB) + firebase modular (~15-20KB) approaches 50KB ceiling. Must enforce modular imports and lazy loading strictly.
    99→- **i18n string count:** Full codebase scan of ~24 page components needed to count hardcoded strings. Migration effort may be larger than estimated.
   100→
   101→## Session Continuity
   102→
   103→Last session: 2026-03-31
   104→Stopped at: Phase 7 Plan 1 complete - PWA plugin + Workbox configured
   105→Resume file: None

     1→# Roadmap: DoAnTuyenSinh
     2→
     3→## Milestones
     4→
     5→- **M1** - UI/UX Redesign - Phases 1-6 (shipped 2026-03-28)
     6→- **M2** - Advanced Features - Phases 7-9 (in progress)
     7→
     8→## Phases
     9→
    10→- [ ] **Phase 7: PWA Infrastructure** - Service Worker, offline page, install prompt
    11→- [ ] **Phase 8: Real-time + Admin Notifications** - SSE endpoint, NotificationBell

    16→## Phase Details
    17→
    18→### Phase 7: PWA Infrastructure
    19→
    20→**Goal**: App works offline, loads instantly on repeat visits, and can be installed as a standalone app
    21→
    22→**Depends on**: Nothing (M2 first phase)
    23→
    24→**Requirements**: PWA-01, PWA-02, PWA-03
    25→
    26→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    27→
    28→1. Returning visitors see cached content served from Service Worker within 200ms
    29→2. When network is disconnected, user sees offline page with message and retry button
    30→3. First-time or engaged user sees install prompt banner ("Cai dat HUTECHS App")
    31→4. Static assets (JS, CSS, images) are cached with content-hashed filenames and cache-first strategy
    32→5. API calls use network-first strategy to prevent stale data
    33→
    34→**Plans**: 3 plans
    35→
    36→Plans:
    37→- [x] 07-01: Install and configure vite-plugin-pwa in vite.config.js with Workbox caching strategies (2026-03-31, commit 3ecd6c0)
    38→- [x] 07-02: Create offline fallback page (public/offline.html) and OfflineBoundary component
    39→- [x] 07-03: Implement deferred install prompt banner with InstallPrompt component and useOffline hook
    40→
    41→### Phase 8: Real-time + Admin Notifications
    42→
    43→**Goal**: Admins receive live notifications when new applications are submitted, with a notification bell in the header
    44→
    45→**Depends on**: Phase 7
    46→
    47→**Requirements**: RT-01, RT-02
    48→
    49→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    50→
    51→1. Admin browser maintains SSE connection to Express backend with 30s heartbeat
    52→2. SSE reconnects automatically with exponential backoff if connection drops
    53→3. Admin sees notification bell icon with live unread count badge in header
    54→4. Admin can open notification dropdown to see list of new application alerts
    55→5. SSE broadcasts to all connected admin clients when a new application is submitted
    56→
    57→**Plans**: 3 plans
    58→
    59→Plans:
    60→- [ ] 08-01: Create SSE service (backend/services/sseService.js) with client registry and heartbeat
    61→- [ ] 08-02: Add SSE endpoint (GET /api/sse/events) in Express backend with JWT validation
    62→- [ ] 08-03: Build NotificationBell component with badge counter, NotificationContext, and useSSE hook
    63→
    64→### Phase 9: Student Notifications + i18n

    67→

    75→2. Student receives in-app toast notification when admin updates their application status
